observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Engineer to:
a. Prepare the plan for the whole project with a clear delineation of work for each phase, showing marks, signs, or color codes to indicate the phases of the project, and the estimated costs of the project’s total cost by phase up to its completion as a technical reference for future repair/ renovation and/or rehabilitation of the structure; and
b. In case of construction by phases for future projects, ensure that there is a clear delineation of work for each phase up to its completion, which must be usable or readily serve its purpose, and structurally sound pursuant to Item 4, Annex A of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.
observations.managementAction: No action was taken by the management
observations.reason: The City Engineer stated that they are waiting for the completion of the final phase (Phase 7) of the Batangas City Command Center with target completion date on September 13, 2025 in order to consolidate the as Built Plan of Phases 1 to 7 into one Plan.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Engineer to:
a. Facilitate the installation of the uninstalled items totaling P1,041,395.65 once all the necessary works are completed; and henceforth, b. Strictly comply with the plans and technical specifications provided in the contract agreement.
observations.managementAction: No action was taken by the management
observations.reason: Installation of the items will be made after the finishing works are completed; and the City Engineer will ensure that the two items are installed after the finishing works in compliance with the plans and technical specifications provided in the contract agreement.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor:
a. Require the City Accountant, City Treasurer and the implementing departments, specifically the City Social Welfare and Development Office (CSWDO), Office of the City Veterinary and Agricultural Services (OCVAS) and CPDO, to continuously exert efforts to locate the supporting documents and to document all procedures employed to establish the whereabouts of the debtors and borrowers including the actions taken to collect the amounts due from them; and
b. Instruct concerned City Officials to explore other appropriate actions, even the filing of appropriate case, if necessary, against erring borrowers to collect the large amount of the City’s receivables which could be used to finance other priority projects/ programs/ activities of the City. Also, instruct the City Accounting Office and the concerned implementing offices (CSWDO, OCVAS and CPDO) to reconcile the records of Loans Receivables - Others account so that the records being maintained by both offices reflect the same account balance.
observations.managementAction: No action was taken by the management The Offices of the City Accountant and other concerned offices are in the process of preparing the documents for the request for write-off of dormant receivable accounts. The concerned offices conducted reconciliation of their records, however, some of the documents were not available, and they informed the Audit Team that this will be included in the request for write-off of dormant receivable accounts.
observations.reason: Unavailability of records and documents.

observations.recommendation:
Based on the foregoing, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor to:
a. Instruct the GSO and the Accounting Unit to perform the preliminary activities of the one-time cleansing and reconcile their records thereof; 
b. Plan/strategize on how the Municipality would apply the one-time cleansing and incorporate it in the PIP to be submitted to the Audit Team at least 10 calendar days before the scheduled inventory-taking activities; and
c. Immediately start the application of one-time cleansing of the PPEs.
observations.managementAction:
The GSO prioritized the disposal of unserviceable properties.
Ongoing preparation for preliminary reconciliation of records between GSO and Accounting.
observations.reason: Changes in key officials slowed down the reconciliation of records between GSO and Accounting.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to immediately conduct confirmation among identified source agencies of the unexpended and unremitted balances and facilitate the return thereof. Otherwise, if there is still a need, the Municipality may make a written representation to the source agencies to request the use of money for other projects or purpose; and
b. Municipal Accountant ensure that all subsequent unused balance of funds received shall be returned immediately after the completion of the project pursuant to COA Circular No. 94-013.
observations.managementAction: The balances are still being reviewed since some are from previous years’ transactions. For preparation of communication with Observation No. 1, Page Nos. 55 to 57 of this Report.
observations.reason: Lack of sufficient records.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. Reconcile employees’ records at GSIS, PhilHealth and Pag-IBIG to identify the affected employees on the unremitted balances and immediately remit the amounts due to these agencies and identify the cause/s of the over remittance for some accounts and make the proper adjustments to avoid the incurrence of unnecessary financial expenses in the form of imposed penalties; and
b. Review the taxes withheld from employees, suppliers and contractors and ensure that these taxes are remitted on the prescribed period to avoid penalties and surcharges.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office is in the process of reconciling their records GSIS, PhilHealth and Pag-IBIG. The Accounting Office is tracing their records of tax withheld and remitted.
observations.reason: Unreconciled amounts pertains to old balances.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor to evaluate and review the LDRRMF Investment Plan with emphasis on the proper identification and selection of specific priority PPAs and include in the plan all PPAs to be charged against the current, continuing and previous years’ unexpended appropriation of previous years to facilitate monitoring and accelerate implementation.
observations.managementAction: LDRRM Projects charged under Current Appropriations were presented in the LDRRMFIP. Management was required to submit the original plan for further evaluation. The documents were forwarded by the Audit Team to COA Region IV-A TAS on July 9, 2024.
observations.reason: Projects, programs and activities (PPAs) of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund are not being identified and presented in the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) as per Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2012-002.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to support the receivables for unpaid rentals with subsidiary ledgers, accrue receivables and income from any unpaid rentals as of every reporting date and facilitate reconciliation with the reports of the Market Office. Likewise, record receivables for the goodwill payments as they are already due and demandable, and recognize the income and liability for the unearned portion.
observations.managementAction: The OIC- Market Supervisor submitted the list of delinquent stall holders to the Office of the Municipal Accountant.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant is yet to record the receivables in the book of accounts.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed to require the:
a. GSO to prepare and maintain road networks and property cards and Report on Local Road Network based on the report submitted by the Municipal Engineer; 
b. Inventory Committee to conduct inventory count of all the Municipality’s road network systems and accordingly submit a Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network System as of the end of the year; and
c. Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain the prescribed property ledger cards for Local Road Networks and disclose all the required information for the said property in the Notes to Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: Ongoing retrieval of documents. Ongoing retrieval of documents. Ongoing retrieval of documents.
observations.reason: Difficulty in locating documents for road networks constructed several years ago. No records available to support the Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network System. No sufficient documents for those projects implemented by other government agencies.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed to require the Municipal Budget Officer to appropriate funds for the return of subsidies received including those used for Personal Services. Further, strictly comply with the prohibition of appropriating subsidies from General Fund for PS costs of LEEs.
observations.managementAction: Ongoing review of the Municipality’s LEE on their capacity to stand alone. Prepared the communication letters to be sent to the payees informing them on the amount payable to the Municipality.
observations.reason: Municipal Market and Slaughterhouse do not have sufficient budget to facilitate the return of subsidies received.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed to require the OIC-Market Administrator to collect the overdue goodwill money particularly those past the 5th year of their lease term, check for and collect underpayments, require timely and sufficient payments of goodwill money for all stalls and use the rates provided in the 2017 Bauan Market Code, as revised by the 2020 Market Code. We also recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to recognize in the books of accounts the receivables for goodwill money and adjustment of prior years’ income and maintain subsidiary ledgers for each stallholder.
observations.managementAction: Overdue goodwill money are being collected gradually. The OIC- Market Supervisor submitted the list of delinquent stall holders to the Office of the Municipal Accountant.
observations.reason: The OIC-Market Administrator still collect the overdue goodwill money particularly those past the 5th year of their lease term, check for and collect underpayments, require timely and sufficient payments of goodwill money for all stalls and use the rates provided in the 2017 Bauan Market Code, as revised by the 2020 Market Code.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant set-up and maintain Special Accounts in the General Fund for the monitoring of operating performance and viability of the Municipality’s Economic Enterprises and 20% DF, and to serve as a decision-making tool of the agency. Also, record all transactions pertaining to these economic enterprises to the corresponding subsidiary ledgers.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant maintains subsidiary ledgers for the Municipality’s Economic Enterprises and 20% DF.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant is yet to maintain special subsidiary ledgers for the Municipality’s Economic Enterprises and 20% DF.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to request the write-off of receivables with no certainty of collection, except for those recorded under the Receivable-Disallowances/ Charges account pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005, or follow-up the request if there’s a pending request for its writing-off from the Commission on Audit. Submission of all proofs that efforts have been initiated to collect the same should be undertaken.
observations.managementAction: Prepared the communication letters to be sent to the payees informing them on the amount payable to the Municipality.
observations.reason: Difficulties in tracing the current whereabouts of many debtors.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to retrieve and evaluate all the documents pertaining to the unpaid payables, determine the legitimacy of the transactions and if found to be valid claims, communicate with the concerned payees for the payment/ settlement of the accounts. Otherwise, revert to the Unappropriated Surplus of the General Fund those that proved to be with no legal claims.
observations.managementAction: Coordinated with the concerned Offices to retrieve documents and communicate with the source agencies. The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer will retrieve documents and coordinate with the payees/source agencies for settlement of accounts.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer will retrieve documents and coordinate with the payees/source agencies for settlement of accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the concerned municipal officials to cause the refund of P48,400.00 expense incurred in CY 2015 but not included among the accounts payable and paid in CY 2016.
observations.managementAction: No action taken by the Management.
observations.reason: The Municipal officials are yet to resubmit the documents to the Audit Team since the concerned officials were already deceased.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Municipal Mayor to:
a. Instruct the Inventory Committee and the Accounting Unit to perform the preliminary activities of the one-time cleansing and reconciliation of their records; 
b. Plan/strategize on how the Municipality would apply the one-time cleansing and incorporate it in the PIP to be submitted at least 10 calendar days before the scheduled inventory-taking; and 
c. Immediately start the application of One-time cleansing of the PPEs.
observations.managementAction: The Inventory Committee and the Accounting Unit are still on the process of the reconciliation of their records. However, the Committee is trying its best to reconcile the Municipality’s records.
observations.reason: The Disbursing Officer, who also acts as the GSO, commented that the Committee is still trying its best to reconcile the records.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer to coordinate with the contractors to refund the P411,470.47 due to deficiencies of overestimated quantities of materials and works, otherwise, a Notice of Disallowance will be issued. Henceforth, it is recommended that the Municipal Engineer prepare accurate project cost estimates in the future projects.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer communicated requesting for re-evaluation/re-computation due to overestimated quantities of materials and works on the construction of three infrastructure projects.
observations.reason: The Management is still searching for the necessary documents and computation/re-computation submitted the necessary documents. The Municipal Engineer is in the process of requesting for re-computation due to overestimated quantities of materials and works on the construction of three infrastructure projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a. Hire or appoint a separate General Service Officer (GSO) who will take custody of and be accountable for all properties of the Municipality; and b. Require the Municipal Accountant and the Inventory Committee to reconcile their records and reports and adopt the procedural guidelines provided under COA Circular No. 2020-006 for the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances and initiate the application of one-time cleansing of PPE.
observations.managementAction: The Management updated the composition of the Inventory Committee. The Assistant Municipal Treasurer was designated as the GSO. The Municipal Accountant and the Inventory Committee are still reconciling their records and reports.
observations.reason: The Management admitted that they have limited knowledge on the procedures of COA Circular No. 2020-006 for the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Assessor to declare for tax assessment purposes the noted lots without Tax Declarations and to provide the Municipal Accounting Office and Municipal General Services Office the necessary information of the land owned by the Municipality; b. The Municipal Accountant to take up necessary accounting entries to recognize the unrecorded lots after the conduct of comprehensive reconciliation of lands owned by the Municipality; c. The Municipal Accountant to maintain a record of each land by using the Real Property Ledger Card, which shall include a complete description of the lot as to area, title number, tax declaration number, PIN, location and other applicable data; and d. The Municipal Accountant and Municipal GSO to follow the guidelines and procedures in the conduct of physical count of land, recognition of land items found at station and disposition of non-existing/missing land items stated in COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020 for the One-Time Cleansing of PPE account balances.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Assessor is still searching for the noted lots without Tax Declarations. The Municipal Accountant is still in the process of determining the acquisition of acquisition of lots. The Municipal Accountant has not yet recorded the parcels in the books.
observations.reason: It is still hard to determine those noted lots for lack of reference. The Management is still looking for the reference of the account land old balances.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the OIC-Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to conduct periodic reconciliation of their records, in order to come-up with accurate and reliable reports on the levy of RPT and SET. Submit the status of reconciliation to the Audit Team.
observations.managementAction: Prepare reconciliation of employees’ records related to GSIS, PhilHealth and PAG-IBIG; submit the status of reconciliation to the Audit Team. The Municipal Treasurer's office is generating monthly delinquency report to facilitate reconciliation with the Municipal Accountant.
observations.reason: Lack of relevant supporting documents. Ongoing reconciliation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor create an Inventory Team, the designated GSO and the Accounting Office to strictly follow the general guidelines, and procedural guidelines, reconciliation of inventory count per RPCPPE with property and accounting records, disposition of non-existing/missing PPEs without available records of accountability and required accounting entries and disclosure in the Notes to the Financial Statements required under COA Circular No. 2020-006 in the conduct of inventory taking for one-time cleansing of PPE.
observations.managementAction: The Inventory/Valuation Committee which shall be responsible for the conduct of actual physical count of all properties, determination and assignment of permanent 1 General Service Office (An Ordinance has already been passed by the SB). The Municipal Treasurer was designated as the GSO. The Inventory Committee is still on the process of reconciling the PPE balances.
observations.reason: The Management plans to update the composition of its Inventory Committee. Reiterated in Observation No. 1, Part II, Page Nos. 42-44 of this report. The GSO and Accounting Office are still on the process of reconciling the PPE balances.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor: a. Require the Municipal Accountant and the GSO-Designate to coordinate with the Municipal Engineer on the inventory of Infrastructure Assets such as Land Improvements, Local Road Network, Flood Control System, Water Supply System and Other Public Infrastructures; b. Exert efforts on all causes of discrepancies toward the reconciliation of their respective records to ascertain the accuracy of the PPE balances in the financial statements; c. Instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to appropriate funds necessary for land titling and creation of General Service Office, and d. Follow the guidelines and procedures in the conduct of physical count of PPE, disposition for non-existing/missing PPE items stated in COA Circular No. 2020-006.
observations.managementAction: The Management appointed a Property and Supply Officer in the absence of a permanent GSO; coordinated with the Office of the Assessor and Engineering Office to compile needed program of works, complete details of all Road Network. The Municipal Accountant is still on the process of compiling the needed program of works, complete details of all Road Network. Inventory Committee in coordination with the Municipal Engineering Office to (i) conduct physical inventory of all existing local road networks in the Municipality and preparation of Report on the Physical Count of Local Road Network to be reconciled with the accounting records; and (ii) furnish a copy of such report to the Audit Team for verification and audit.
observations.reason: Pending completion of compiling the needed program of works, complete details of all Road Network. The Management is still undermanned and lacks resources; thus, full implementation is delayed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Assessor, Municipal Treasurer and Accountant to determine the nature and amount of acquisition of these lots, as basis of valuation; and the Municipal Accountant to recognize in the Municipality’s books the unrecorded parcels of land using the market value indicated in the earliest applicable Tax Declarations under the name of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: Coordination among the offices of the Assessor, Treasurer and Accountant are ongoing to determine the nature and amount of acquisition of lots. Municipal Accountant to effect adjusting entry to take up unrecorded parcels of land using the market value indicated in the earliest applicable Tax Declarations under the name of the Municipality is pending.
observations.reason: The Municipal Assessor is still in the process of locating the documents related to the noted lots. The Municipal Accountant has not yet recorded the parcels of land in the books due to lack of necessary information.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to reconcile employees’ records at GSIS, PhilHealth and PAG-IBIG to identify the affected employees on the unremitted balances and immediately remit the amounts due to these agencies and identify the cause/s of the over remittance for some accounts and make the proper adjustments to avoid the incurrence of unnecessary financial expenses in the form of imposed penalties.
observations.managementAction: Reconciliation of employees’ records related to GSIS, PhilHealth and PAG-IBIG is ongoing. The Municipality shall exhaust all available means to secure documents and journals to trace back those old balances and identify the specific taxpayers.
observations.reason: Most of the supporting documents pertain to employees that are no longer in service; Management cannot locate the reference documents because some were damaged or spoiled.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to exert further efforts to communicate with GSIS and PAG-IBIG to confirm if the balances and/or to check with the records whether all premiums/loan repayments/contribution deducted from the payroll of employees and Government Share (GS) for all employees were remitted in order to identify the affected employees on the unremitted balance, if any. Otherwise, if the mentioned agencies were able to confirm that they have no outstanding claims from the Municipality, request for the reversion of such payables following the applicable guidelines.
observations.managementAction: Coordinate with the concerned agencies for the balances; exhaust all available means to secure documents and journals to trace back those old balances and identify the specific taxpayers. If the concerned agencies confirm no outstanding balance, request reversion of such payables.
observations.reason: There was difficulty in tracing old supporting documents; some documents were damaged or spoiled and some employees are no longer in service.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Inventory Committee, the Municipal Accountant and Municipal General Services Officer (GSO) to:
a. Plan/strategize on how would the Municipality apply the one-time cleansing and incorporate it in the Physical Inventory Plan (PIP) to be submitted at least 10 calendar days before the scheduled inventory-taking activities; and
b. Immediately start the application of the one-time cleansing of the PPEs; and
c. Prepare the RPCPPE in conformity with the format and contents prescribed by Annex 49 on Chapter II of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume II, completely fill up and include the vital information on the report pertaining the actual result of the physical inventory count undertaken.
observations.managementAction: The Executive Order concerning the composition of the Inventory Committee for the one-time cleansing of PPEs was not yet finalized but explained that they have already drafted the Physical Inventory Plan (PIP) to pursue the said undertaking this Calendar Year (CY) 2025. However, lack of conscious effort for undertaking even the preliminary activities denotes non-prioritization on this aspect despite consistently recurring audit observations on the PPE’s existence and completeness.
observations.reason: The Municipality was not able to conduct or even commence their one-time cleansing this year 2024. Reiterated with modifications in Audit Observation No. 1, Page Nos. 35 to 37, Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to refrain from assigning JO personnel to perform collection functions innate to regular accountable officers of the Municipality such as Revenue Collection Clerk/Officer as such entail responsibility and accountability for money and property. In order to have additional plantilla positions, we recommended that Management make an extra effort in raising revenue/receipts such as tax collection efficiency and generating revenue related projects or activities so as to increase the base of Personnel Services limitation for the succeeding fiscal year.
observations.managementAction: No specific management action provided in the table; matter addressed by management in other correspondence and efforts to increase revenue base were suggested.
observations.reason: JO personnel were still allowed to perform collection functions. Efforts to increase revenue base and plantilla positions were ongoing.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the GFPS TWG to:
a. Prepare GAD Agenda as soon as possible and strictly comply with the provisions on the preparation of the GAD Agenda to ensure that the PPAs indicated in the annual GPB are aligned with it or with the identified gender issues to be addressed during the three-year term of the LCE’s leadership; and
b. Submit the GPB and GAD AR within the prescribed period to facilitate evaluation and validation of the GAD PPAs.
observations.managementAction: The GAD Focal Person indicated plans to prepare GAD Agenda and submit GPB and GAD AR; training and other activities planned.
observations.reason: GAD Agenda for three-year term of the LCE was not prepared in CY 2024. Training and crafting of GAD Agenda was planned but not yet conducted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the concerned department heads to:
a. Continuously secure documents such as the Notice of Finality of Decision and/or Order of Execution to trace back those disallowances reflected in the Receivables – Disallowances/Charges account. Consider sending a letter request to COA Regional Office No. IV-A to inquire for any available documents related to the said disallowances; and
b. Immediately initiate action to enforce the settlement of the disallowances in accordance with the COA Revised Rules of Procedure on the Settlement of Accounts once the persons liable and their accountability have been established.
observations.managementAction: Tracing of pertinent documents is still on-going. Efforts were made to follow up COA Regional Office for available documents and to identify persons liable.
observations.reason: The receivable had been in the books for more than 20 years and the persons liable could not be identified; documents to support settlement were not available.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to maintain individual subsidiary ledger card per taxpayer or per barangay per year to ensure that receivables and collections for each given year as well as adjustments, if any, are posted in the respective SLs to provide the account details and balances by year, and to facilitate verification and monitoring of the said accounts’ balances and actual RPT and SET collections for each given year.
observations.managementAction: No specific action reported in the table; monitoring and reconciliation efforts were indicated to be ongoing by the Accounting and Assessor offices.
observations.reason: Lack of personnel and attention affected the preparation and monitoring of subsidiary ledgers; reconciliation issues remained as of year-end.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the concerned Municipal officials to prioritize the implementation of the project for the provision of a suitable and adequate size storage facility with installed racks that can accommodate all the financial reports and other related documents of the Municipality considering the ten-year preservation period of the accounts.
observations.managementAction: No specific management action provided in the table; reported that there is no available space in the existing LGU Hall but a new Municipal Building will be constructed in CY 2025 where the Audit Team will be provided with an office with mezzanine per proposed plan.
observations.reason: There is still no available space in the existing LGU Hall; a new Municipal Building will be constructed in CY 2025.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
a. Municipal Assessor to facilitate the registration and transfer of the title of the land properties from the previous owners to the Municipality; b. Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain Land and Land Improvements Ledger Card (LLILC) with complete description of land; and c. Municipal Assessor and Municipal Accountant to reconcile their records to come up with the correct value of the land.
observations.managementAction: No management action reported for some items; reconciliation efforts ongoing. Difficulty in coordination with property owners and lack of available documents.
observations.reason: Difficulty coordinating with the owners of the properties and lack of available documents; discrepancy between records maintained by the Municipal Assessor and Municipal Accountant remained.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Municipal Accountant to provide Allowance for Impairment for the dormant receivables of P181,423.98 for the possible losses; and
b. Former Disbursing Officer (DO) to produce all the available records pertaining to her request for relief from accountability for the held-up amount of P434,903.40 and to follow up on its status.
observations.managementAction: No specific management action provided in the table; tracing of documents and communication with former DO was being pursued.
observations.reason: No allowance for impairment was provided in the books for CY 2024; persons liable could not be identified since the receivable had been in the books for more than 20 years.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Accounting Department to provide schedule of inventory accounts and have it reconciled with the records of the GSO. Meanwhile, maintain supplies ledger cards as prescribed by the rules.
observations.managementAction: Stock cards were already maintained and preparation of Supplies Ledger Cards are still ongoing.
observations.reason: Lack of personnel to give full attention on the preparation and monitoring of the subsidiary ledgers; supplies ledger cards and reconciliation were still ongoing.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated the prior year's audit recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Inventory Committee, the Municipal Accountant and GSO to conduct the one-time cleansing of account following the guidelines and procedures under COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020 and to obtain and reconcile the list of Property, Plant, and Equipment (PPE) recorded in their books. A physical inventory of PPE has already been conducted in all barangays of the municipality, while the inventory of assets per office within the municipal government is currently ongoing. Upon completion of the office-level inventory, the Municipality shall undertake the one-time cleansing of accounts in accordance with the guidelines set forth under COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020. The target date for the completion of the one-time cleansing is March 2026.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Inventory Committee to ensure that all reported items in the RPCPPE are physically existing and duly accounted for based on the annual inventory count undertaken, and to ascertain that the said report is duly prepared in accordance with the prescribed format, supplying all the necessary particulars therein.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipality shall undertake the one-time cleansing of accounts in accordance with the guidelines set forth under COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020. The target date for the completion of the one-time cleansing is March 2026.

Physical count of PPE shall be conducted annually and will be reported using the prescribed format of the RPCPPE.
observations.reason: The Municipality had started the preliminary activities in preparation for the one-time cleansing, but the activity proper has not yet started. Municipal General Services Office has coordinated with the Municipal Accounting Office to obtain and reconcile the list of Property, Plant, and Equipment (PPE) recorded in their books. A physical inventory of PPE has already been conducted in all barangays of the municipality, while the inventory of assets per office within the municipal government is currently ongoing. Upon completion of the office-level inventory, the Municipality shall undertake the one-time cleansing of accounts in accordance with the guidelines set forth under COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020. The target date for the completion of the one-time cleansing is March 2026.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. General Service Officer (GSO) to consolidate (BAC) of goods alike and similar in nature and refer all PRs to the BAC for adoption and conduct of the appropriate procurement procedure in accordance with the approved Annual Procurement Plan (APP).
b. BAC Secretariat to prepare, based on Project Procurement Management Plans (PPMPs) submitted by implementing offices, an indicative Annual Procurement Plan (APP) based on the submitted Project Procurement Management Plans (PPMPs) to ensure that procurement requirements are aligned with the budget proposal and properly aligned with the budget proposal. To strengthen the capacity of concerned personnel, a training/workshop on the preparation of APPs and PPMPs will also be conducted.
c. GSO and BAC to undertake the procurement in accordance with the approved APP, taking into consideration the consolidation of purchases and the timeline set therein.

We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor, if necessary, increase their personnel responsible with the procurement so as not to compromise the substantive and procedural requirements of the procurement law.
observations.managementAction:
a. GSO to consolidate all Purchase Requests (PRs) of goods alike and similar in nature and refer all PRs to the BAC for adoption and conduct of the appropriate procurement procedure in accordance with the approved Annual Procurement Plan (APP).

b. BAC Secretariat to prepare the indicative Annual Procurement Plan (APP) based on the submitted Project Procurement Management Plans (PPMPs) to ensure that procurement requirements are aligned with the budget proposal. Training/workshop on the preparation of APPs and PPMPs will also be conducted.

c. GSO and BAC to undertake procurement in accordance with the approved APP, taking into consideration the consolidation of purchases and the timeline set therein.

Increase of personnel responsible for procurement to augment the staff involved in the procurement process.
observations.reason: GSO and BAC have already conducted a coordination meeting to address the noted audit observation. It was agreed that effective January 2026, the GSO shall systematically collect all Purchase Requests (PRs) from various offices and consolidate those of similar nature to ensure efficiency and compliance with the prescribed procurement modalities. Consolidated PRs shall then be referred to the BAC for the adoption and conduct of the appropriate procurement procedure in accordance with the approved Annual Procurement Plan (APP). BAC Secretariat applied for APP of CY 2026. The BAC issued memo to all departments relative to the submission of each office's PPMP, with deadline of December 1, 2025. To support the increasing workload and to avoid compromising compliance with procurement law, the Municipal Mayor has approved the hiring of two additional personnel to augment the staff involved in the procurement process.

observations.recommendation: We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer to evaluate the feasibility of planned Program, Projects and Activities (PPAs), especially those held under Continuing Appropriations, and identify projects which will no longer be implemented for possible reversion and reprogramming to other disaster-related PPAs. The MDRRMO/LDRRMC shall re-evaluate the plan and reprogram the funds available in the Continuing Appropriations and Special Trust Fund.
observations.managementAction: The MDRRMO/LDRRMC will re-evaluate the planned Program, Projects and Activities (PPAs), especially those held under Continuing Appropriations, and identify projects which will no longer be implemented for possible reversion and reprogramming to other disaster-related PPAs. The MDRRMO/LDRRMC will prioritize PPAs that are ready-for-implementation.
observations.reason: This is to be reflected in 2026.

observations.managementAction: The Municipality shall undertake the one-time cleansing of PPE in 2026. The Inventory Committee to be formally created. The GSO, Municipal Accountant and the duly created Inventory Committee to follow the procedural guidelines on the one-time cleansing of PPE prescribed under COA Circular No. 2020-006 such as (1) Preliminary activities prior to the conduct of inventory taking; (2) Inventory taking; (3) Reconciliation of inventory count per RPCPPE with property and accounting records; (4) Disposition procedures for non-existing/missing PPEs; (5) Procedures in the derecognition of non-existing/missing PPEs without available record of accountability; and (6) Accounting entries and disclosure in the Notes to Financial Statements.
observations.reason: In compliance with the recommendation of the Commission on Audit, the General Services Office has coordinated with the Municipal Accounting Office to obtain and reconcile the list of Property, Plant, and Equipment (PPE) recorded in their books. A physical inventory of PPE has already been conducted in all barangays of the municipality, while the inventory of assets per office within the municipal government is currently ongoing. Upon completion of the office-level inventory, the Municipality shall undertake the one-time cleansing of accounts in accordance with the guidelines set forth under COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020. The target date for the completion of the one-time cleansing is March 2026.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MDRRMC to evaluate the feasibility of planned PPAs, especially those held under Continuing Appropriations, and identify projects which will no longer be implemented for possible reversion and reprogramming to other disaster-related PPAs; and prioritizing the PPAs that are ready-for-implementation.
observations.managementAction: The MDRRMO/LDRRMC will re-evaluate the plan and reprogram the funds available in the Continuing Appropriations and Special Trust Fund, prioritizing the PPAs that are ready-for-implementation.
observations.reason: This will be reflected in 2026.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the GFPS to prepare the GAD Agenda pursuant to PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01.
observations.managementAction: The GAD Focal Person shall prepare the GAD Agenda.
observations.reason: GAD Agenda was already drafted but still for review and approval of the Municipal Mayor.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and the GSO to immediately comply with the full implementation of the perpetual inventory method in recording and reporting all procured goods, supplies and materials for distribution and consumption pursuant to the NGAS Manual for LGUs. We recommended that the GSO shall conduct the yearly inventory taking of all PPEs, prepare the corresponding Inventory and submit the Report of Physical Count of Inventory (RPCI) to the Accounting Office and copy furnish the Audit Team.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal GSO/Accountant and the Accounting/Inventory to end-user, Committee shall exert effort in the implementation of the perpetual inventory method of recording and reporting. At year-end, the GSO will submit to the accounting office an inventory of all remainings goods, supplies and materials for distribution and consumption in order for the Accounting Office to record adjustments on the ending inventory.
observations.reason: Since goods are generally issued to end-user, hence perpetual inventory method of recording and reporting was found impractical. As the goods were already consumed once issued, they were immediately recognized as expense.

observations.recommendation: Based on the foregoing, we recommended the Municipal Mayor to: a. Prioritize the conduct of one-time cleansing of PPE accounts as soon as possible by creating an Inventory Committee composed of adequate number of members to be able to complete the physical inventory in three months or less; b. Instruct the Inventory Committee to prepare a Physical Inventory Plan (PIP) to include the specific name of committee members, the cut-off date and the schedule specifying the dates and locations of the inventory taking activities from start up to the targeted completion of the inventory- physical inventory taking—which shall be within three months or less and duly approved by the Municipal Mayor to be submitted to the Audit Team at least ten calendar days before the scheduled inventory taking activities; c. Require the newly created Inventory Committee, General Services Office (GSO) and the Accounting Office to perform the preliminary activities by preparation of RPCPPE. However, appropriate reports such as latest RPCPPE, Inventory Count Form and the lists of those PPE items in the GSO records but not in the Accounting records and vice-versa to be used during actual physical inventory; and d. Provide transportation equipment and other necessary tools for the implementation of the one-time cleansing.
observations.managementAction: An Inventory Committee was created in July 15, 2024 through Administrative Order No. 2, Series of 2024.
observations.reason: No action was taken by the Management. Preliminary activities conducted by the Municipal Accountant and the GSO. The said Offices are yet to submit to the Audit Team the latest RPCPPE to be used during the actual inventory-taking. In addition, there is not a clearly defined timeline for the actual inventory-taking—which involves the Inventory Committee. The two offices are still in the process of completing preliminary activities and the GSO has prioritized the updating of the Report of Physical Count of PPE. Therefore, the actual inventory-taking—which involves the Inventory Committee—has yet to be carried out by the Inventory Committee.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a. Instruct the GAD Focal Point System to prepare the GAD Agenda in accordance with Section 4.C.2 of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2013-01 and with the guidelines per PCW Memorandum Circular 2018-04; b. Require the MPDO to be assisted by the GFPS and TWG to set up and maintain GAD Database and update/s, if necessary; and c. Follow the prescribed format of GAD Accomplishment Report in Annex E of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2013-01. Retain as working paper the data pertaining to check numbers for the actual expenditures incurred for easy verification.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal GAD Focal Point System participated in orientation trainings to craft the GAD Agenda. The GAD Database was utilized in CY 2024 for the preparation of sex-disaggregated data. The GAD Focal Point System ensured the GAD Accomplishment Report for CY 2024 follows the prescribed format.
observations.reason: The GAD Agenda was not fully formulated based on documented gender analysis. Determination of gender issues per GAD PAP as derived from the sex-disaggregated data or other means of data gathering used by the implementing offices was not fully documented and the responsiveness of the Municipality’s GAD PAPs was not established. Hence, gender analysis using the appropriate tools remains necessary.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct: a. Municipal Accountant in coordination with the MHO, to determine and recognize in the books the full cost of the ending inventory of COVID-19 vaccines and supplies as at year-end and henceforth record future receipts and issuances of the same. Thereafter, record future receipts and issuances of the same as and when applicable. b. The MHO to determine the costing of the received vaccines and provide the utilization report of all received vaccines due to LGU as of December 31, 2023. Henceforth record future receipts and issuances of the same.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Health Office provided monitoring and utilization report of all received vaccines with utilization rate of 97 percent as of December 31, 2024. As to the cost, there is still no reply received from the Provincial Health Office for the unit cost of the donated COVID-19 vaccine as of December 31, 2024. The LGU will regularly disclose all vaccines received in the Notes to Financial Statements, indicating the count and proper cost based on documents available.
observations.reason: There is no agreement with the Provincial Health Office to provide the costing of the received vaccines; hence, there is still no accounting entry to recognize the cost of the remaining 3 percent unused vaccines as at year-end. There is no reply received from the Provincial Health Office as to the unit cost of the donated COVID-19 vaccine as of December 31, 2024.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor to: a. Instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to ensure enough funds are appropriated for property insurance of the Municipality; b. Direct the GSO to submit the duly accomplished PIF to the Office of the Supervising Auditor or Audit Team Leader and the GIF of GSIS on or before April 30 of each year to be used as basis for the assessment of the general insurance coverage of all the Municipality’s insurable properties; and c. Direct the GSO to insure with the GIF of GSIS all the Municipality’s insurable assets and properties with the appropriate insurance coverage to protect the Municipality thru indemnification in cases of damage or loss due to fortuitous events.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Budget Officer commented that the Office will plan for the insurance needs in the coming budget cycles, as there is still no available budget for the property insurance at this time. The General Services Office has coordinated with the Municipal Assessor and the Municipal Budget Office and submitted the Property Inventory Form (PIF) to the Audit Team on May 8, 2024. The GSO insured all municipal vehicles but due to limited funds full insurance coverage of all properties could not be appropriated at this time.
observations.reason: No management action was taken. The LGU is still in the process of seeking additional funds to cover the premiums for insurance of properties.

observations.recommendation: Based on the foregoing observations, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office (MDRRMO), in coordination with the Municipal Engineer, to: a. Implement the programs and projects funded under the LDRRMF within the intended time to avoid delay in the delivery of its benefits to the constituents. Conduct thorough planning and analysis to ensure that those PPAs identified for inclusion in the LDRRM Plan are those that can be implemented and completed within the target period; and b. Review and update the LDRRMFIP annually to include the activities to be funded from the unexpended QRF and Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund - Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses (DRRM-MOOE) of the previous years which were transferred to the Special Trust Fund.
observations.managementAction: The MDRRMO implemented monitoring and consultative planning for the programs and projects under Current and Continuing Appropriation and coordinated with the Municipal Engineering Office to prioritize ongoing projects under LDRRMF. The MDRRMO reviewed and updated the LDRRMFIP covering trust funds and other sources for DRRM PPAs and prepared programming for Special Trust Funds and other sources to cover DRRM related PPAs.
observations.reason: This was not prioritized by the Management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MDRRMO to: a. Implement the programs and projects funded under the LDRRMF within the intended time to avoid delay in the delivery of its benefits to the constituents. Conduct thorough planning and analysis to ensure that those PPAs identified for inclusion in the LDRRM Plan are those that can be implemented and completed within the target period; and b. Review and update the LDRRMFIP annually to include the activities to be funded from the unexpended QRF and related sources for DRRM-MOOE of the previous years which were transferred to the Special Trust Fund.
observations.managementAction: The MDRRMO conducted monitoring and consultative planning for DRRM projects and coordinated with concerned offices. The MDRRMO committed to incorporate all available LDRRM Funds in the LDRRMFIP for allocation to disaster preparedness and mitigation PPAs and to coordinate with donors for re-allocation of donations.
observations.reason: This was not prioritized by the Management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require: a. The Municipal Assessor and the General Services Officer to exhaust all remedies to secure the Certificate of Title or other forms of ownership of the aforementioned lands and take the necessary actions to expedite the transfer of ownership to the Municipality. Coordinate with the Municipal Budget Officer regarding fund requirements for inclusion in the Annual/Supplemental Budget in the succeeding periods, if warranted; and b. The Municipal Accountant to secure copies of those documents lacking on the procurement of land and submit it to the Audit Team, once available.
observations.managementAction: The General Services Office coordinated with the Municipal Assessor and the Municipal Budget Officer and transferred ownership processing to the Municipal Assessor. The GSO furnished the Audit Team the Property Inventory Form and coordinated for the transfer processing. The Municipal Accountant will submit required documents to the Audit Team upon receipt from concerned offices.
observations.reason: Not all required documents were yet secured by management; copies of the Transfer Certificate of Title for the three lots were not submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MENRO to strictly implement issued ordinances on solid waste management and impose the appropriate fines and penalties in violation thereof.
observations.managementAction: The Sangguniang Bayan has drafted an Ordinance pertaining to Solid Waste Management including the imposition of fines and penalties. The Ordinance is on-going and consultation with Joint Ventures partners regarding cost-sharing and responsibilities is being conducted.
observations.reason: The Ordinance imposing the fines and penalties is still being processed and yet to be finalized.

observations.managementAction: An Executive Order No. 33 series of 2022, dated December 28, 2022, was issued for the reorganization of the Property Inventory and Disposal Committee. The Municipality has yet to create an Inventory Committee solely responsible for the cleansing, as required. Preliminary activities are currently ongoing between the Accounting Office and the GSO. The Property Inventory and Disposal Committee has prepared a PIP. Preliminary activities are currently ongoing between the Accounting Office and the GSO. The Property Inventory Committee has prepared a PIP, which requires revision. The updated PIP activities scheduled from June to August 2024. Preliminary activities are currently ongoing between the Accounting Office and the GSO which involves the use of Inventory Count Forms and the identification of missing items and those found at station is yet to be carried out by a new Inventory Committee. No action was taken by the Management.
observations.reason: The conduct of inventory-taking by an Inventory Committee was not prioritized during the year. The scheduled activities under the PIP were not met; therefore, there is a need to revise the PIP. The actual inventory-taking is yet to be carried out by a new Inventory Committee. Implementation will be upon the conduct of physical count.

observations.recommendation: Thus, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Administrator to countersign all the checks drawn by the Municipal Treasurer excluding those checks charged against the SB budget pursuant to the above cited regulations.
observations.managementAction: No action was taken by the Management.
observations.reason: The Municipality adheres to the current practice common among other local government units. As of CY 2025, the Municipal Mayor provided instructions to comply with the provisions.

observations.recommendation: In view of the foregoing, we reiterated our audit recommendations in prior years that the Municipal Mayor direct the: a. Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to stop the granting of cash advances to those accountable officers who have not yet fully liquidated outstanding cash advances; b. The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to refrain from granting cash advances to concerned officials and employees especially at the close of the year unless the previous cash advances given to them had been liquidated; c. Strict compliance with the rules and regulations on the granting and liquidation of cash advances pursuant to PD No. 1445 and COA Circular Nos. 2012-001 and 97-002, and Section 5.8 of COA Circular 97-002; d. Municipal Accountant to issue demand letters to concerned officials and employees to immediately settle outstanding cash advances. Failure to liquidate shall constitute a valid cause for withholding of salary.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer committed to implement measures: The Municipal Accountant is currently monitoring the status of cash advances on a monthly basis. All liquidation reports submitted are now pre-numbered and logged for easier monitoring and verification of completeness in filing. Management has submitted the deficiencies noted in the liquidation of cash advances totaling P160,539.00 showing that full liquidation transpired in 2025.
observations.reason: Multiple cash advances were granted during CY 2024 despite the non-settlement of outstanding cash advances. Cash advances remained unliquidated at year-end; demand letters were not issued for many long outstanding receivables at the time; and cash advances sometimes exceeded the maximum accountability limits.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a. Instruct the GSO and Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to properly plan and consolidate the procurement of goods/services of the Municipality and adopt the proper mode of procurement instead of cash advances; b. Direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to limit the grant of cash advances only to those cases mentioned in COA Circular 97-002, for regular cash advances to cashiers, disbursing officers, paymasters and/or property/supply officers for salaries, commutable allowances, honoraria and petty operating expenses and for special cash advance for travel expenditures and current operating expenditures undertaken in the field where it is impractical to pay by check.
observations.managementAction: No action was taken by the Management.
observations.reason: Certain purchases were still paid through cash advances instead of the appropriate procurement modes; the proper mode of procurement was not adopted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management stop the practice of spending government funds for religious or private purposes otherwise such transactions will be disallowed in audit.
observations.managementAction: No action was taken by the Management.
observations.reason: Certain disbursements for week-long cultural activities continued and were not disallowed in practice.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Development Council to conduct proper assessment and evaluation of the priority projects of each barangay and the whole Municipality to avoid idle funds and stalled implementation of development projects.
observations.managementAction: As of December 31, 2024, there were still unimplemented projects. The Municipal Budget Officer submitted the 20% Development Fund appropriation from the CY 2025 Annual Budget, presenting itemized appropriations of programs, projects, and activities in accordance with the AIP. Management committed to comply.
observations.reason: Some projects funded under the 20% DF were still unimplemented; lapses in planning such as insufficient appropriation for certain planned projects and delays in procurement resulted in non-implementation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Health Office to determine and recognize in the books the full cost of the ending inventory of vaccines and henceforth record future receipts and issuances of the same. To ascertain, conduct confirmation of identified source agencies to confirm the balances due to them and facilitate the return of the remaining unutilized unused fund. Otherwise, if there is still a need, the Municipality may make a written representation to the source agencies to request the use of money for other projects or purpose of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Health Office provided a letter from the Provincial Health Office stating that they do not disclose the costing of the vaccines due to a non-disclosure agreement with the manufacturers; hence, the valuation could not be provided. Recommendation to prepare RTS considered not doable.
observations.reason: The Provincial Health Office could not provide the costing details due to non-disclosure agreement with the vaccine manufacturers; therefore, recognition and valuation could not be completed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to: a. conduct confirmation among identified source agencies to confirm the balances due to them and facilitate the return of the remaining unutilized unspent fund; otherwise, if there is a need, request the source agencies for approval for the use of the remaining fund for other projects or purposes of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: No action was taken by Management. The Municipality is yet to find the source agencies in order to request return/refund of the unexpended balances.
observations.reason: No action taken; the Municipality has yet to contact source agencies to seek disposition of the unexpended balances.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to: a. coordinate again with the source agencies to confirm the balances due to them and request the use of the funds for the Municipality where applicable; b. refund/return the unexpended balances of the completed projects to source agencies, or otherwise obtain approval for reprogramming or use; and c. strengthen planning to avoid delays in implementation.
observations.managementAction: No action was taken. The Management is yet to identify source agencies in order to request return/refund of the unexpended balances. Some recommendations were later revisited and revised in AAR Part II.
observations.reason: No action taken; the Municipality is still to find the source agencies to request return/refund of the unexpended balances.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to set-up and maintain Special Accounts in the General Fund for the 20% Development Fund and separate books of accounts for each special account to monitor transactions and operating performance.
observations.managementAction: No action was taken in CY 2024. The Municipality planned to maintain separate records upon opening a separate bank account for the 20% Development Fund.
observations.reason: Separate books of accounts for Special Accounts under General Fund were not yet maintained; separate bank account for 20% DF not opened as of reporting.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Engineer require the contractor to complete the project as soon as possible and impose sanctions/penalties if warranted; compute the amount of liquidated damages to be deducted from payments.
observations.managementAction: No action was taken. Project implementation was suspended and delays were attributed to suspension and subsequent revisions to design; the retaining wall collapsed due to typhoon.
observations.reason: The project was suspended which resulted in prolonged days of work; implementation was revised and the retained structure was later found unsuitable due to site conditions.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer to closely monitor and institute corrective measures on the findings of COA-TAS and immediately inform the audit team of the course of actions taken. Otherwise, demand payment from the contractor for the amount of P491,563.93.
observations.managementAction: No action was taken. The COA TAS findings were substantiated and partial refund had been made by LGU officials and employees. The remaining balance was still to be refunded.
observations.reason: Documents supporting correction of the works and the amounts refunded were yet to be provided to the Audit Team; balance remained outstanding.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require: a. Coordinate again with the Provincial Accountant and reconcile their records on receivables/payables and their corresponding payables/receivables in the books of the Province to establish the correct balances; b. Coordinate with concerned officials to obtain information on long outstanding accounts and if there is no valid claimant revert the balances to surplus account and consider as invalid/undocumented obligation as required; and c. Identify long outstanding balances for appropriate action.
observations.managementAction: Only a balance of P93,000.00 was confirmed as a receivable of the Government. Remaining payables amounting to P829,384.19 were not yet refunded and reconciliation continued.
observations.reason: Reconciliation incomplete; remaining payables not yet refunded; approval for reversion to be sought.

observations.managementAction:
An Inventory Committee was
created in
February 17,
2025 through
Executive Order
No. 06, Series of
2025.

Preliminary activities are
currently ongoing between
the Accounting Office and the
GSO. The latest RPCPPE as of
December 31, 2024 was
submitted to the audit team.

Both the GSO and Municipal
Accountant were not able to
prioritize completion of the
required lists as a result of
comparing their records during
the preliminary activities. The
inventory-taking is yet to be
scheduled and planned.

Implementation of the one-time
PPE cleansing is still pending.

Logistical support and any
necessary expenses, will be
arranged upon the
commencement of the one-time
inventory cleansing.
observations.reason: As the required lists resulting from the preliminary activities have not yet been prepared and finalized by the GSO and Municipal Accountant, the inventory-taking is yet to be scheduled and planned.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the General Services Officer, Municipal Accountant and the members of the Inventory Committee to follow the procedural guidelines on the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances prescribed under COA Circular No. 2020-006 such as (1) Preliminary activities prior to the conduct of inventory taking; (2) Inventory taking; (3) Reconciliation of inventory count per RPCPPE with property and accounting records; (4) Disposition procedures for non-existing/missing PPEs; (5) Procedures in the derecognition of non-existing/missing PPEs without available record of accountability; and (6) Accounting entries and disclosure in the Notes to Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality had conducted physical count of its PPEs as of December 31, 2024. Also, there aren’t enough personnel to form the inventory committee. Reiterated with prescribed modifications under Audit Observation No. 1 page nos. 52 to 53.
observations.reason: The Municipality is still updating its Property and PPE Ledger Cards.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Administrator to countersign all the checks drawn by the Municipal Treasurer excluding those checks charged against the SB budget pursuant to the above cited regulations.
observations.managementAction: The Local Chief Executive was just recently installed and the Municipal Administrator is currently undergoing transition and communication with the banks for change of signatory.

observations.recommendation: Based on the foregoing, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a. Instruct the Inventory Committee and the Accounting Unit to perform the preliminary activities of the one-time cleansing and reconcile their records; and b. Plan/strategize on how the Municipality would apply the one-time cleansing and incorporate it in the Physical Inventory Plan to be submitted at least 10 calendar days before the scheduled inventory-taking activities and immediately start the application of one-time cleansing of the PPEs.
observations.managementAction: Ongoing
observations.reason: Leadership transition due to appointment of a new OIC in the General Service Office (GSO). Reiterated under Observation No. 1, Pages 38 to 40 of Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer, Treasurer and Accountant to cause the refund of the total amount of P296,900.00.
observations.managementAction: Ongoing retrieval of documents to prove that the wages were refunded. The Municipal Treasurer submitted the documents as proof that wages were refunded. Municipal Accountant submitted documents pertaining to the payment of Labor payroll for the Improvement of Foot Bridge.
observations.reason: Documents to support the refund of wages were not fully submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: a. Municipal Engineer to provide the pre-repair inspection report and a written and valid justification on the propriety and necessity of the repair, subject to evaluation, otherwise the said transaction shall be disallowed in audit; b. Municipal Engineer to demand from the contractor the detailed costing of the repair using the prescribed computation for procurement of goods/services and refund the resulting cost difference plus liquidated damages for the noted delay; and c. Municipal Engineer to submit for evaluation the approved plan for the excavation itemizing the site, dimensions, scope of work, and others lacking details noted and the Bids and Awards Committee for the invitation request to other contractors, otherwise suspended in audit.
observations.managementAction: Ongoing retrieval of documents. The Municipal Engineer communicated with the contractor for the submission of detailed costing for the repair.
observations.reason: Difficulty in locating documents due to transactions that transpired during the tenure of the former Municipal Engineer; pending full compliance for detailed costing and approved excavation plan.

observations.recommendation: We recommended establishment of the GAD Database that will strengthen the planning and budgeting processes for GAD and ensure that planned PPAs address identified gender issues, that objectives, performance indicators and targets are well formulated to facilitate monitoring and evaluation for the attainment of GAD objectives.
observations.managementAction: Programs were planned and implemented with focus on addressing identified gender issues. Certification was forwarded to the Office of the Provincial Accountant verifying the existence of Dormant Receivables specifically the KKK Receivables dated wayback 1992. Considering request to write-off dormant receivables.
observations.reason: Lack of manpower due to retirement of the former MSWDO. Planned programs not fully based on a sex-disaggregated GAD database.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the members of the BAC and the TWG to submit justification why the project was awarded to the winning contractor in spite non-submission of the documentary requirements of RA No. 9184. Request the submission of the lacking documents in order to further validate the propriety of the disbursements.
observations.managementAction: Ongoing retrieval of documents.
observations.reason: Neither justification nor documents was received by the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor require the:
a. City Engineer to immediately submit copies of Certificate of Project Completion for
projects completed by year-end to the Office of the City Accountant,
specifically before closing the books of accounts, so that appropriate asset and
liability accounts can be recognized; and
b. City Accountant to record the completed project in compliance with the
recognition principle of PPE and payable accounts pursuant to IPSAS 17 and PD
No. 1445 for fair presentation of the accounts in the financial statements of the
City Government.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to adopt the
prescribed estimated useful life under COA Circular No. 2003-007 in the succeeding
year, disclose in the Notes to Financial Statement the estimated useful life for
Infrastructure Assets, and prepare the necessary adjusting Journal Entry Vouchers
for the depreciation expenses of all affected PPE accounts to ensure the accuracy
and reliability of the reported balances, including the corresponding Government
Equity accounts, thus, attain fairness of the accounts’ presentation in the year-end
financial statements of the City Government at any reporting date.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to strictly observe the
proper classification of accounts based on the updated revised chart of accounts per
COA Circular No. 2015-009 to ensure accuracy and reliability in recording and
reporting the financial transactions of the City;

Likewise, require the City Accountant to prepare the appropriate adjusting journal entries
necessary to reflect the correct classification of project costs for drainage systems to
road networks accounts, including the accurate charging of depreciation pursuant to
COA Circular No. 2015-008.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the Disposal Committee and City GSO
to prioritize the disposal of unserviceable properties, in accordance with COA-DBM
JC No. 2024-1, to avoid further deterioration leading to lesser resale value, and thus,
eliminate the continuing storage cost for the government.

We recommended that the City Mayor, who is also the Chairman of the City
Development Council (CDC), direct the:
a. Bids and Awards Committee (BAC), City Planning and Development Coordinator
and City Engineer to coordinate with one another and prioritize the formulation of
an improved procurement plan, in which all development projects appropriated
under the 20 percent Development Fund will be implemented and completed
within the year, thereby achieving the full benefits intended for the beneficiaries;
b. CDC to allocate the 20 percent DF to well-planned and procurement-and-
implementation-ready infrastructure projects, sans any unresolved issues that
would prohibit their full implementation, pursuant to Joint Memorandum Circular
No. 2020-01 of the DBM, DOF, and DILG, so that all the benefits accorded to the
said projects would be attained and delivered to their intended beneficiaries, thus,
promoting the general welfare of its people; and
c. City Budget Officer, City Treasurer, and City Accountant to ensure that the
subsequent appropriation for the 20 percent DF shall be at least 20 percent of the
annual IRA received by the City, pursuant to Section 287 of RA 7160.

We recommended that the City Mayor, as head of the City Disaster Risk Reduction
and Management Council (CDRRMC):
a. Ensure the appropriate and thorough evaluation of every project to be funded
under the LDRRMF for its immediate implementation, contributing significantly to
the prevention or mitigation of any disaster-related events that adversely affect
the lives of its constituents, in accordance with Section 12(c) of RA No. 10121;
b. Coordinate with other government agencies for any planned or existing projects
within the City’s jurisdiction to avoid unnecessary duplication of projects, or
unresolved issues that may cause delay in the projects’ implementation; and
c. Prioritize the allocation of LDRRMF and utilize the available savings for PPAs that
are implementation-ready, without any restrictions or prohibitions, before their
inclusion in the LDRRMF budget so that the allocated funds can be fully utilized
within the timelines set by the CDRRM C, thereby contributing to the objectives of
establishing LDRRMF.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the:
a. CDRRMC to issue a resolution adopting the qualification criteria for the
accreditation of VMO based on the disaster risk profile and capacity needs of the
City and adhering the minimum standards and requirements set forth in NDRRMC
MC No. 64 s. 2021;
b. CDRRMO to require the 4,064 volunteers and all subsequent volunteers to
undergo the proper accreditation procedure following the policies and guidelines
on the accreditation, mobilization, and protection of community disaster
volunteers pursuant to NDRRMC MC No. 64 s. 2021; and
c. City Budget Officer and City Accountant to coordinate with the CDRRMO on the
noted deficiencies, submit to the Audit Team the proof of accreditation of 4,064
volunteers, and stop charging and processing payments for the personnel
accident insurance of volunteers not included in the official list of ACDVs against
147

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to:
a. Re-evaluate the deficiencies noted on the subsidiary ledgers of the Due to
PhilHealth, Due to Pag-IBIG, and Due to GSIS accounts, and act accordingly on
the results of such re-evaluation to ensure that unremitted balances or negative
balances are addressed, so that the affected accounts reflect only the correct and
true balances at any given reporting period; and
b. Conduct monthly reconciliation and monitor the recording of transactions for the
Due to PhilHealth, Due to Pag-IBIG and Due to GSIS accounts, in their respective
SLs to ensure that all withheld amounts are remitted as required, and to prevent
the presence of negative balances that may affect the status of members’
contributions with the relevant government agencies, potentially leading to
penalties and interest charges against the City and the concerned employees.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the:
a. City Budget Officer and GFPS ensure that at least five percent of the City’s total
yearly allocations are gender-responsive pursuant to Section 43 of RA No. 9710;
b. GFPS TWG use the HGDG Tool to gradually increase the GAD PPAs pursuant to
PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2016-1; and
c. GFPS Executive Committee and TWG develop and maintain the GAD Database,
which contains gender statistics, and sex-disaggregated data that have been
systematically produced/gathered, and regularly updated to serve as inputs or
bases for planning, programming, and policy formulation pursuant to Section 36
of RA No. 9710.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the Inventory Committee, the City
Accounting Office and the GSO to adopt and fully implement the one-time cleansing
procedures of PPE accounts incorporated into the COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated
January 31, 2020 in order to:
a. Properly address the missing/unaccounted/undocumented PPE items that have
been in the books for several years;
b. Address the discrepancy of the Land, Other Land Improvements, Infrastructure
Assets, and Buildings and Other Structures, which contributed a total
discrepancy of P2,358,609,309.42 to the total deficiencies of P2,404,664,494.54;
c. Address any deficiencies that may arise out of the conduct of complete
inventory-taking of all PPE items owned by the City; and
d. Update and maintain the required Property Cards/ Property Ledger Cards in
