observations.managementAction: The Municipality is still conducting research for their possible tickets to be issued. The Municipality still used improvised tickets for the collection of TEF and Light House Fees. All collectors accomplished the daily RCD. All collectors remitted daily collections or as soon as collections reach P5,000.00.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the GFPS and the Technical Working Group to: a. Prioritize gender mainstreaming efforts in GAD planning and budgeting by using existing gender analysis tools such as the HGDG in the identification, design, implementation, monitoring and evaluation stages of the various PPAs to determine the extent of gender-responsiveness and the amount to be attributed to the GAD budget; b. Develop a GAD Data Base or Sex-Disaggregated Data for proper utilization in the planning, budgeting, programming and policy formulation of the Municipality, as well as proper charging of the object of expenditures on actual accomplishments; and c. Strictly monitor the implementation of GAD PPAs and conduct regular coordination with the implementing offices to ensure that programs are undertaken within the target period. Submit justification why said GAD PPAs were not implemented as of year-end.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality is already using HGDG in GAD planning and budgeting. Development of GAD Data Base is still ongoing. Justifications regarding the unimplemented GAD PPAs were submitted to the Audit Team. Also, monitoring of GAD PPA’s is observed to ensure that the same is properly accomplished.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to inform the concerned suppliers for the warranty deposit and require them to prepare a letter request regarding the return/refund of the amount due them of which their guaranty claim shall be supported with the photocopy of the disbursement voucher showing such amount was deducted. In case, the concerned suppliers were not anymore existing or no response was received from them, effect the necessary adjustment in the LGU’s financial statement by debiting the account Guaranty Deposit Payable and crediting the account Prior Year’s Adjustment.
observations.managementAction: The Management sent letters to suppliers with outstanding guaranty deposit. In Calendar Year (CY) 2023, several suppliers already claimed their guaranty deposit. Some suppliers no longer existing. Also, some suppliers claimed their guaranty deposit as evidenced by the withdrawal of the Guaranty/Security Deposits Payable account.
observations.reason: Some suppliers changed their business name. Also, the management can no longer locate some of the old files, Official Receipts. In case the concerned suppliers were not anymore existing or no response was received from them, effect the necessary adjustment in the LGU’s financial statement by debiting the account Guaranty Deposit Payable and crediting the account Prior Year’s Adjustment.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. Prepare the Reconciliation between the Actual Amounts as presented in the SCBAA and the SFPer as additional disclosures in the NFS to clarify the discrepancies between the two reports, and submit the same to the Audit Team as well as the Statement of Net Cash Flows from Operating Activities; and
b. Prepare the NFS for the succeeding year using the prescribed Pro-forma under Annex G of COA Circular No. 2016-004. Ensure that each item on the face of the statement of financial position, statement of financial performance, statement of changes in net assets/equity and cash flow statement shall be cross-referenced to any related information in the notes.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant was not able to reconcile the actual amounts as presented in the SCBAA and the SFPer for CY 2024. The Municipal Accountant was able to submit the CY 2024 NFS using the prescribed Pro-forma under Annex G of COA Circular No. 2016-004.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant was not able to reconcile the actual amounts as presented in the SCBAA and the SFPer for CY 2024; however, the NFS for CY 2024 was prepared using the prescribed pro-forma.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal Treasurer to send written notice to the payee for the presentation and submission of stale checks as requirement prior to issuance of the replacement check and to practice the sending of written notice to the payee at least one month before a check becomes stale; b. Municipal Accountant to restore the amount to Cash in Bank and to set up the related accounts payable accounts for the cancellation of the checks for the fair presentation of the related accounts in the financial statements; and c. Municipal Accountant to adjust all valid reconciling items determined during the preparation of the Monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements on a timely basis for accurate account balances in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer sent written notice to some payees in CY 2024. The Municipal Accountant has not yet made the necessary adjustments. For some bank accounts there were reconciling items (e.g., Landbank) not accounted for as of year-end.
observations.reason: The Municipality sent written notices to some payees; adjustments were not fully made and some bank reconciling items remained unaccounted for as of year-end.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. Reconcile employees’ records with GSIS, PhilHealth and PAG-IBIG to identify the affected employees, if any, on the unremitted amounts and immediately remit the amounts due to these agencies and identify the cause/s of the over remittance for some accounts and make the proper adjustments to avoid the incurrence of unnecessary financial expenses in the form of imposed penalties; and
b. Review the taxes withheld from employees, suppliers and contractors and its corresponding remittances, and ensure that these taxes are remitted within the prescribed period to avoid penalties and surcharges.
observations.managementAction: Reconciliation and remittances were regularly made. The taxes withheld were remitted within the prescribed period. The Municipal Accountant and offices coordinated to effect remittances.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal GSO to:
a. submit justification why the information as to brand was not indicated in the POs and ensure that all future POs include the brand name, model and complete detailed specification of the items being procured to ensure accuracy of the items delivered by suppliers; b. submit valid explanation why the delivered dump trucks were not in accordance with the specifications provided for in the POs for further evaluation by the Audit Team and if the said deficiency will not be addressed sufficiently, the procurement will be considered an irregular expenditure and may be disallowed in audit; and c. instruct the concerned barangay officials to make corrective repairs to the damaged rear wheel of the donated dump truck for it to be usable again for the benefits of the barangay constituents.
observations.managementAction: The Management has submitted their justification to the Audit Team on January 23, 2025 and incorporated the brand name and complete detailed specification for future POs.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor directs the:
a. Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Engineer to review the reversion of unexpended balances and unobligated appropriations of completed PPAs and consider the reversion of those unobligated appropriations of completed PPAs which are no longer to be pursued by the Municipality to be used for other potential developmental projects; b. Municipal Planning and Development Council to assess the viability of the identified PPAs in the planning stage to ensure that only those projects which are procurement and implementation ready will be given budget allocation; and c. Municipal Engineer and Implementing Departments to ensure the optimal and timely implementation of the projects to be funded of the 20 percent DF to address the development needs of the residents and to reap the desirable socio-economic benefits at the earliest pursuant to DBM-DILG-DOF JMC No. 2020-01.
observations.managementAction: No specific management action provided for some items; for others the MDC/LFC assessed and reprogrammed certain appropriations and negotiation with property sellers were ongoing. The LFC agreed to revert appropriations to unappropriated surplus where projects could not be implemented.
observations.reason: Three projects funded from the current and continuing appropriations of the 20 percent DF were not implemented as of December 31, 2024 due to owners’ refusal to sell lots and other procurement/implementation readiness issues; unexpended continuing appropriation balances remained to be reverted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the LDRRM Officer to:
a. Submit the Municipality’s plan for the implementation of the LDRRM Fund; b. Submit written justifications and explanations regarding the unutilized balance of LDRRM Fund; c. Ensure correct classification of PPAs to be charged under the five percent LDRRM Fund whether for Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses or Capital Outlay for proper treatment thereof of the unutilized portion/savings; and d. Judiciously plan on how to maximize the utilization of the LDRRM Fund and to accelerate their implementation in order to achieve the goal/target of the Municipality regarding disaster relief, recovery, and risk reduction and management.
observations.managementAction: The LDRRM Officer provided detailed justification on the noted unutilized LDRRM Fund and submitted a status of these PPAs that were implemented in CY 2024. Several projects were awaiting approvals (e.g., PAGASA) or were not needed due to existing equipment.
observations.reason: Several projects funded out of the LDRRMF totaling P2,930,914.47 were not implemented as of year-end caused by inadequate planning and monitoring; some items awaited external approvals or were not procured due to insufficiency of appropriated amounts.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, as the Chairperson of the concerned Executive Committee and the Technical Working Group to:
a. Submit the Annual GPB to the Provincial Planning and Development Office (PPDO), from PPDO to DILG Provincial Office and secure the Certificate of Endorsement from the DILG Provincial Office before incorporating to the Municipality’s Annual Investment Plan (AIP) and Annual Budget to properly ascertain the relevance of the gender issues identified and gender-responsiveness of the PPAs indicated in the GPB; b. Submit the GAD AR within the deadline set by the Joint Memorandum Circular to facilitate timely evaluation and validation of GAD reports, budget, and utilization; c. Comply with the proper prescribed format of the Annual GPB to present the significant data needed for the assessment of the gender-sensitivity of the GPB; and d. Ensure reconciliation of the contents of the GAD AR and the GPB with other reports prepared by the Municipality to ensure the fairness and reliability of the reports.
observations.managementAction: The GAD Focal Person stated the Lian GFPS entered into a Data Sharing Agreement with DSWD Listahanan which was used as GAD database; the Lian GFPS submitted the 2025 GPB to the PPDO thru DILG GAD Plan and Budgeting Monitoring System and obtained Certificate of Review and Endorsement from DILG for 2025 GPB. The GPB form used previously was not compliant with the prescribed form but steps were taken to comply.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. Observe the disclosure requirements in the preparation of the financial statements in accordance with IPSAS and COA Circular No. 2016-004 to assist users in understanding the financial statements and to provide them with information relevant to decision-making; and
b. Provide the necessary disclosures and reconcile the discrepancies noted in the financial statements and effect the necessary adjustments.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant indicated that she will prepare and incorporate in the Notes to Financial Statements the reconciliation in the succeeding periods.
observations.reason: The NFS was prepared but reconciliation between some financial statements items (e.g., SCBAA and SFPer) remained outstanding for CY 2024 and will be addressed in succeeding periods.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to evaluate and compare the amount of collectibles and payables with the records of the Provincial Government to ascertain the legality and validity of both claims so both parties may make the necessary adjustments on their accounts to facilitate financial statements free of any material misrepresentation that could affect the fairness of presentation of the accounts in the financial statements of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: Regular coordination was made with the Provincial Accounting Office for the reconciliation of records.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that the reciprocal accounts Due To Other Fund and Due From Other Fund will present the same balances at year-end.
observations.managementAction: Transactions for the year recorded in these reciprocal accounts were accounted for except for P47,602.79 which pertains to old balances with no details which is for disclosure in the NFS of CY 2025.
observations.reason: Discrepancy for P47,602.79 pertains to old balances with no details and will be disclosed in the CY 2025 NFS.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the concerned offices to maximize the utilization of GAD Funds and see to it that GAD-related PPAs will fully address and adhere to the basic purpose and objectives for which the funds were provided.
observations.managementAction: For CY 2024, the Municipality reported 20 implemented and one not implemented GAD PPAs with a utilization rate of 96.67 percent and indicated plans to maximize utilization of GAD funds.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) Chairman to provide explanation for the inconsistencies in the different phases of the procurement process and to immediately submit all the lacking documents to the Auditor concerned to check the propriety of the award of contracts and for further evaluation of the transaction to avoid suspension/disallowance in audit.
observations.managementAction: The former BAC Chairman had already submitted his justification and the documents related to the projects were submitted to the former Audit Team. The projects were used for their intended purposes and continue to be utilized.
observations.reason: Some documents remained unsubmitted but justification and related documents were provided and the projects continued to be used for their intended purposes.

observations.recommendation:
Foregoing considered, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to:
a. Adjust the Accumulated Depreciation and Accumulated Surplus by ₱1,736,768.28.
b. Commence the recognition of depreciation at the date of its completion, and not at the date of final payment.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accountant shall review the schedule and effect the necessary adjustments.

The Municipal Accountant shall commence the recording of the depreciation at the date of completion or delivery and effect necessary adjustments.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. Review the validity of Guaranty/Security Deposits Payable which are outstanding for over one year; and securing the Certification from the end-users/Municipal Engineer concerning the final acceptance of the projects/goods and the concerning validity of the release of the retention money to various contractors.
b. Notify the contractors and their private entities concerned to file their claims for the immediate release of the retention money to avoid accumulation of the account balances in the books. Otherwise, require them to execute a waiver of their right to claim such deposits for the reversion to the unappropriated surplus of the general fund.
observations.managementAction:
a. The Municipal Accountant shall review the validity of Guaranty/Security Deposits Payable which are outstanding for over one year and securing the Certification from the end-users/Municipal Engineer concerning the final acceptance of the projects/goods and the concerning validity of the release of the retention money to various contractors.

b. Notify the contractors and their private entities concerned to file their claims for the immediate release of the retention money.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant is still waiting for the report of said BAC offices.

observations.recommendation: Hence, we recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and GSO to facilitate the immediate disposal of unserviceable properties, including the determination of the appropriate mode of disposal and appraisal of the same, if necessary, to prevent further deterioration and reduction of the properties' economic value.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant and GSO to facilitate the immediate disposal of unserviceable properties, including determination of the appropriate mode of disposal and appraisal of the same, if necessary, to prevent further deterioration and reduction of the properties' economic value. Creation of Inventory and Disposal Committee to oversee the conduct of appraisal, determination of the appropriate mode of disposal, and preparation of the required documentation. Target of conducting the actual disposal proceedings by November.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the General Services Office and Municipal Engineering Office to strictly comply with Section 301 of PD No. 1096 (National Building Code of the Philippines) by securing proper demolition permits issued by the Municipal Building Official prior to implementation. We likewise recommend that the Municipal Mayor instruct the concerned Building Official to undertake proactive measures in implementing the required permit for government properties where the fact of demolition came to his knowledge.
observations.managementAction: GSO and Municipal Engineering Office to strictly comply with Section 301 of PD No. 1096 by securing demolition permits prior to undertaking any demolition of government structures. Municipal Building Official to exercise stricter monitoring and require the on-going submission of demolition permit applications for any government structure subject to demolition.

observations.recommendation: Therefore, we recommend that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal General Services Officer to immediately issue ICS acknowledged by the designated end-users for semi-expendable items that are not yet covered by the said form to establish clear accountability and proper monitoring of government properties.
observations.managementAction: The GSO shall immediately issue ICS acknowledged by the designated end-users for semi-expendable items that are not yet covered by the said form.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Municipal Accountant and General Services Officer to prepare the Local Road Network Ledger Card and Local Road Network Property Card.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant and GSO shall prepare the Local Road Network Ledger/Property Cards using the prescribed format under COA Circular No. 2015-008 for all recorded road networks of the Municipality.
observations.reason: While the Local Road Network Ledger Card and Property Card have been prepared, the GSO is still to accomplish the corresponding recorded property cards.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor to: a. Prioritize the conduct of one-time cleansing of PPE accounts as soon as possible by creating an Inventory Committee composed of adequate number of members to be able to complete the physical inventory in three months or less; b. Instruct the Inventory Committee to prepare a Physical Inventory Plan (PIP) to include the specific name of committee members, the cut-off date and the schedule specifying the dates and locations of the inventory taking activities from start up to the targeted completion of the inventory- physical inventory taking—which shall be within three months or less and duly approved by the Municipal Mayor to be submitted to the Audit Team at least ten calendar days before the scheduled inventory taking activities; c. Require the newly created Inventory Committee, General Services Office (GSO) and the Accounting Office to perform the preliminary activities by preparation of RPCPPE. However, appropriate reports such as latest RPCPPE, Inventory Count Form and the lists of those PPE items in the GSO records but not in the Accounting records and vice-versa to be used during actual physical inventory; and d. Provide transportation equipment and other necessary tools for the implementation of the one-time cleansing.
observations.managementAction: A Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) No. 100-3866C dated June 30, 2024 was drawn based on proper PPE balance as of December 31, 2023 and another JEV No. 100-8218 dated Dec 31, 2023 for discrepancies in general ledgers.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to implement the necessary provisions of Sections 4.1 and 4.2 of COA Circular No. 2022-004. Henceforth, use the new capitalization threshold in recording similar assets.
observations.managementAction: A Journal Entry Voucher No. 100-3866D dated June 30, 2024 was drawn to reflect the reclassification in view of the capitalization provisions of Sections 4.1 and 4.2 of COA Circular No. 2022-004. Henceforth use the new capitalization threshold in recording similar assets.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require: a. the Municipal Accountant to reclassify the remaining balance to appropriate income accounts and record the subsequent 20 percent share of the Municipality from the Fire Code Fees as Permit Fees and shares from the collected from cutting of coconut trees remitted by the Philippine Coconut Authority as Share from National Wealth in the General Fund pursuant to COA Circular No. 2015-009, Republic Act (RA) No. 9514 and BLGF MC No. 03-01-2017.
observations.managementAction: A Journal Entry Voucher No. 100-3866E dated June 30, 2024 was drawn for the recognition of fund transfers as Share from National Wealth.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to effect the necessary adjustments to correct the erroneous recognition of insurance premium payments as outright prepaid insurance expense in the books of the Municipality and to fairly present its financial statements and records. Henceforth, strictly follow the aforementioned rules under Section 112 of PD No. 1445 and observe caution in the allocation of its insurance premiums.
observations.managementAction: Adjustment amounting to P44,962.77 was made under JEV No. 100-2604 dated May 2, 2024 to record the adjustment for prepaid insurance premiums for motor vehicle in CY 2023.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to recognize in the books of accounts the taxes withheld simultaneously with the setting up of Accounts Payable (AP) for goods received or services rendered, inspected and accepted, and for construction/infrastructure projects partially or fully completed and inspected pursuant BIR Memorandum Circular No. 10-2018.
observations.managementAction: Taxes were withheld upon accrual of payables at year-end 2024.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to: a. Review the figures posted in the general ledger versus actual payroll deductions to ensure accuracy and make the necessary correcting entries, if necessary; b. Ensure that posting of transactions is done on proper ledger to monitor the balances; and c. Identify the affected employees on the unremitted balances and immediately remit the amounts due to the concerned agencies. Identify the cause/s of the over remittance for some accounts and make the proper adjustments.
observations.managementAction: Adjustments were posted in the general ledgers and accounts. Adjustment of the accounts Due to GSIS, Pag-IBIG and PhilHealth were made through JEV Nos. 100-2599 to 100-2605 for the corrections and adjustment of the Due to GSIS, Pag-IBIG and PhilHealth.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require: a. The Inventory Committee, immediately after the conduct of complete physical count, to reconcile inventory results with property and accounting records; b. The Property and Accounting units to perform reconciliation of the PPE balance particularly on the noted difference of P69,125,576.18 and make the necessary adjustments/recording entries, if any; c. The Inventory Committee to ensure that all counted items are properly specified and identified detailing physical attributes and location; d. The Municipal Accountant disclose in the Notes to the Financial Statements the results of reconciliation of RPCPPE and accounting records as well as the amount of PPE items, if any, without available supporting documents; and e. The existing Inventory Committee or a newly created Inventory Committee study the provisions of COA Circular 2020-006 on one-time cleansing of PPE and consider its implementation to account for the non-existing PPEs and to present reliable balances in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Inventory Committee immediately after the conduct of complete physical count of PPE prepared the RPCPPE. The Property and Accounting units performed reconciliation and made necessary adjustments/recording entries. The Inventory Committee ensured that all counted items are properly specified and identified. The Municipal Accountant disclosed in the Notes to the Financial Statements the results of reconciliation of RPCPPE and accounting records as well as the amount of PPE items without available supporting documents. The Inventory Committee studied the provisions of COA Circular 2020-006 on one-time cleansing of PPE and considered its implementation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require: a. The Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and other concerned officials to evaluate and cause the implementation of the project funded by PAGCOR upon completion of the Site Development in order to deliver the expected benefits to intended beneficiaries; b. The Municipal Accountant to conduct confirmation among NGAs, identified source agencies to confirm the balances due to them and facilitate the return of the remaining unutilized/unused fund. Otherwise, if there is still a need, the Municipality may make a written unexpended balance representation to the source agencies to request the use of the money for other projects or purpose of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The LGU returned P229,101.15 with JEV No. 300-014(2023) P15,000.00, JEV No. 300-134 (2024) P212,986.00, and JEV No. 100-7324 (2024) P1,115.15. The remaining balance of P97,370.05 were reclassified as prior period income under JEV No. 100-8132D.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: a. General Services Officer to conduct annually the physical count of all Property, Plant and Equipment Supply and reconcile the RPCPPE with the accounting records to fully identify the discrepancies noted. Complete all the fields required in the prescribed Reports on the Physical Count and necessary remarks. With regard to disposal, complete the necessary documents and perform the subsequent procedures in accordance with the Circular. Likewise, ensure that all PPEs are fully supported with all the PPE Cards by the GSO; b. Accounting Department to submit copies of PPE Ledger Cards and Lapsing Schedule of PPE to the audit team for CY 2021 onwards. Have those records reconciled with the GSO and document the results to be the bases of adjustments, if any. Likewise, ensure that the Subsidiary Ledgers/Lapsing Schedule of PPE tally with the balances in the Financial Statement. Lastly, review the breakdown of the items per PPE account and ensure that there is consistent application of accounting policies in recording PPEs. If there is a reclassification needed, prepare adjusting journal entry and have it supported with the necessary documentation; and c. Municipal Engineer to assist the General Services Office and the Municipal Accounting Office in the conduct of the physical count and reconciliation of PPEs on particularly to their Infrastructure Assets and Buildings and Other Structures accounts. Ensure that all PPEs in those groups of accounts are accounted and verified.
observations.managementAction: General Services Officer and the Accounting Department performed the required actions: GSO to conduct physical count and reconcile RPCPPE with the accounting records; Accounting Department submitted copies of PPE Ledger Cards and Lapsing Schedule of PPE to the audit team and performed reconciliation; Municipal Engineer assisted in the conduct of the physical count and reconciliation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require: c. The Municipal Treasurer to return to the Bureau of the Treasury the undisbursed/unexpended fund pursuant to Section 28, Chapter 4, Book VI of E.O. No. 292, s. 1987. The remaining unutilized/unused fund of P327,381.20 to be confirmed among NGAs and facilitate the return of unutilized/unused fund. Otherwise, if there is still a need, the fish cages for the Municipality may make a written unexpended balance representation to the source agencies to request the use of the money for other projects or purpose of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: JEV No. 300-014(2023) P15,000.00, JEV No. 300-134 (2024) P212,986.00, and JEV No. 100-7324 (2024) P1,115.15. The remaining balance of P97,370.05 were reclassified as prior period income under JEV No. 100-8132D.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require: • General Services Office to conduct annually the physical count of all Property, Plant and Equipment and reconcile the RPCPPE with the accounting records to fully identify the discrepancies noted. Complete all the fields required in the prescribed Reports on the Physical Count and necessary remarks. With regard to disposal, complete the necessary documents and perform the subsequent procedures in accordance with the Circular. Likewise, ensure that all PPEs are fully supported with all the PPE Cards by the GSO; • Accounting Department to submit copies of PPE Ledger Cards and Lapsing Schedule of PPE to the audit team for CY 2021 onwards. Have those records reconciled with the GSO and document the results to be the bases of adjustments, if any. Likewise, ensure that the Subsidiary Ledgers/Lapsing Schedule of PPE tally with the balances in the Financial Statement. Lastly, review the breakdown of the items per PPE account and ensure that there is consistent application of accounting policies in recording PPEs. If there is a reclassification needed, prepare adjusting journal entry and have it supported with the necessary documentation; and • Municipal Engineer to assist the General Services Office and the Municipal Accounting Office in the conduct of the physical count and reconciliation of PPEs particularly to their Infrastructure Assets and Buildings and Other Structures accounts. Ensure that all PPEs in those groups of accounts are accounted and verified.
observations.managementAction: The General Services Office conducted the physical count and reconciled the RPCPPE with the accounting records. The Accounting Department submitted PPE Ledgers and Lapsing Schedule and reconciled with GSO records and prepared necessary adjustments. The Municipal Engineer assisted in physical count and reconciliation for infrastructure and buildings accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Inventory Committee headed by the General Services Office to conduct the physical count of its Local Road Network and prepare the RPCLRN. Make use of the template provided in Annex C of COA Circular No. 2015-008 or Appendix 63 of the Government Accounting Manual for LGUs and complete the necessary information in every field. Submit the accomplished RPCLRN to the Accounting Office for reconciliation in the books.
observations.managementAction: The Inventory Committee conducted the physical count of the road network and prepared the RPCLRN. Make use of the template provided in Annex C of COA Circular No. 2015-008 and submitted the RPC to the Accounting Office for reconciliation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a. Prioritize the conduct of one-time cleansing of PPE accounts as soon as by creating an Inventory Committee composed of adequate number of members to be able to complete the physical inventory in three months or less; and, b. Prepare and submit a Physical Inventory Plan (PIP) that includes the specific name of all committee members, the cut-off date and the schedule specifying exact dates and locations of the inventory taking activities from start up to the targeted completion of the physical inventory which shall be within three months or less and duly approved by the Municipal Mayor to be submitted to the Audit Team at least ten (10) calendar days before the scheduled inventory taking activities.
observations.managementAction: In compliance to the COA Circular No. 2020-006, preliminary engagement activities as well as physical inventory count were conducted by the Inventory Committee from October 1 to December 19, 2024. The GSO has furnished the Audit Team the revised RPCPPE, Property Inventory Plan (PIP) and other documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to reclassify the balance of the Bureau of Fire Protection share to appropriate income account and record the balance of the share of the LGU from PCSO as Share from PCSO in the General Fund pursuant to COA Circular No. 2015-009, RA 9514 and BLGF MC No. 06-01-2017.
observations.managementAction: The Office of the Municipal Accountant furnished the Audit Team copy of the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) Nos. 100-24-05-1141 and 300-24-05-0065 both dated May 2, 2024 to correct the balances of the affected accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjusting entries to correct balances of the affected accounts and henceforth, strictly observe the policies on the recognition of depreciation for PPE prescribed in PAG 3 of IPSAS 17 and COA Circular No. 2015-008.
observations.managementAction: The Office furnished copies of the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) Nos. 100-24-04-0972 and No. 200-24-04-0115 both dated April 19, 2024 to correct the balances of the affected accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to: a. Strictly record claims as Accounts Payable only when recorded claims are supported by complete supporting documents as to validity; and, b. Immediately require the submission of documents for all outstanding payables as of January 31, 2024 and validate the claims based on valid and complete supporting documents and/or proof of the actual delivery of goods and services. Otherwise, effect necessary adjustments/reversal in the books to conform with regulations. We likewise recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the General Services Officer to strictly ensure that documents evidencing delivery of goods and services, such as the Delivery Receipts, Statement of Account/Contractor’s Bill, as applicable, and Inspection and Acceptance Reports (IARs) along with the procurement documents are submitted in support of claims for payment forwarded to the Municipal Accountant.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant submitted documents for all outstanding payables as of January 31, 2024. The General Services Office required the submission of Delivery Receipts and other documents evidencing deliveries from suppliers and forwarded Inspection and Acceptance Reports (IAR) to the Municipal Accountant.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Administrator to countersign all the checks drawn by the Municipal Treasurer excluding those checks drawn by the SB budget pursuant to the above cited regulations.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer requested for a Resolution authorizing the Administrator to countersign all the checks as evidenced by the Sangguniang Bayan Resolution No. 2024-04-106 dated April 29, 2024. The Municipal Administrator started countersigning the checks in July 2024 up to October 4, 2024 only, due to the latter’s resignation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the BAC to require concerned contractors of ongoing infrastructure projects to submit their DOLE-approved CSHP in compliance with DOLE DO No. 13, s. 1998 and Section 37.2.3(f) of the RIRR of RA No. 9184.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal BAC required the contractors to submit CSHP duly approved by DOLE prior to the construction of future projects. The Municipal Accountant required the contractors to submit the DOLE-approved CSHP before processing of payment.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Development Council to assess the feasibility of the implementation and responsiveness to the current needs of the constituents of those unimplemented projects and prioritize development projects that are well-planned and procurement-and-implementation-ready pursuant to DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1, s. 2020.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Development Council convened for deliberation and the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator collaborated actively with the concerned offices and agencies to ensure effective monitoring and implementation. The Municipal Development Council is taking into consideration the recommendations to further enhance its planning and implementation and is coordinating with the Office of the Municipal Engineer to thoroughly assess each project.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor as the Chairperson of the Executive Committee together with the GFPS and the GFPS Technical Working Group (TWG) require members to: a. Exert active efforts in accomplishing all the targeted and planned client-focused and organization-focused PPAs; b. Coordinate with the Municipal Human Resource Management Officer to require all members of the GFPS including elected local officials and department heads to undergo capacity building programs on GAD such as gender-sensitivity training, gender-responsive planning and budgeting and other GAD tools; and c. Coordinate and reconcile with the Municipal Budget Office to accurately present the total appropriation and utilization of the GAD Fund.
observations.managementAction: The GAD Section coordinated with every office/section for the timely implementation of their planned programs. The GAD personnel and members of GFPS attended GAD Planning and Budgeting Seminar and other capacity building activities. The GAD Section coordinated and reconciled with the Municipal Budget Office to accurately present the total appropriation and utilization of the GAD Fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to: a. Reconcile all employee records at GSIS, PhilHealth and Pag-IBIG to identify the affected employees on the unremitted accounts and immediately remit the amounts due to these agencies and identify the cause(s) of the over remittance for some accounts and make the proper adjustments to avoid the incurrence of unnecessary financial expenses in the form of imposed penalties; and b. Review the taxes withheld from employees, suppliers and contractors and ensure that these taxes are remitted on the prescribed period to avoid penalties and surcharges.
observations.managementAction: All employee records at GSIS, PhilHealth and Pag-IBIG were reconciled and the Accounting Office reviewed and remitted the necessary amounts. Adjusting entries were made where necessary.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the General Services Section Chief to conduct the required annual physical inventory to determine the status or condition of all the PPEs reported in the Inventory Report or adopt the one-time cleansing of PPE to ensure that recorded PPEs were existing and to rid the books of unaccounted properties; and b. The Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain the prescribed property ledger cards for Local Road Networks and disclose all the required information for the said property in the Notes to Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: Preliminary engagement activities as well as physical inventory count were conducted by the Inventory Committee from October 1 to December 19, 2024. The GSO furnished the revised RPCPPE, Property Inventory Plan (PIP) and other supporting documents which were submitted to the Audit Team on May 19, 2025. The Municipal Accountant prepared property ledger cards and disclosed the information in the Notes to Financial Statements for CY 2024.
observations.reason: Closed for having been revised, and approved by the Regional Director on February 14, 2025, to be excluded in the ensuing year report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to undertake a thorough review and analysis of the details of the payable accounts that remained outstanding for two years or more. Secure documents that will support such payables and coordinate with the identified creditors to ascertain the existence, validity and legality of their claims with the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant undertook a review and analysis of the details of the payable accounts. The Municipal Accountant coordinated with the concerned suppliers and creditors and requested supporting documents. Some documents were submitted and partial settlements were made.
observations.reason: Not all required supporting documents were submitted; some documents to substantiate the validity of long outstanding payables were not yet provided to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Engineer and Municipal Planning and Development Officer to ensure optimal and timely utilization of 20 percent DF in the succeeding period by including development projects that are well-planned and procurement-and-implementation-ready; and to lessen frequent changes in the planned PPAs to ensure immediate implementation and completion within the target date.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal MPDC and Municipal Development Council coordinated with the Office of the Municipal Engineer to assess and prioritize ready projects. The Municipal Development Council has been consistently coordinating with the Office of the Municipal Engineer to thoroughly assess each project to ensure technical soundness and feasibility.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the Honorable Members of the Sangguniang Bayan include among its top priorities the construction of a building for the safekeeping and accommodation of the increasing volume of documents generated by the various departments/offices of the Municipality, particularly those for submission to the audit team, with consideration on the preservation period of ten years as mandated by law.
observations.managementAction: The construction of the new municipal building is ongoing and includes provision for temporary storage rooms and container vans to ensure safekeeping of documents. The facility was provided as temporary storage rooms and container vans to ensure safety of documents. The new stockroom will be provided upon completion of the new municipal building.
observations.reason: Not all documents needed to be transferred to the new stockroom were yet provided to the Audit Team; another storage for the documents was not yet provided to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant and GSO with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for all road projects; Inventory Committee to conduct inventory count of all the Municipality’s Road Network systems and accordingly submit a Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network System as of the end of the year; and the Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain the prescribed property ledger cards for Local Road Networks and disclose all the required information for the said property in the Notes to Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer submitted a road inventory to the Municipal Accountant, MPDC and GSO every year. The General Services Office requested the Municipal Engineering Office to furnish copies of the complete road network information for reference and documentation purposes. The Municipal Accountant prepared the property ledger cards for Local Road Networks and included disclosure in the Notes to Financial Statements for CY 2024.
observations.reason: Previous inquiry disclosed that the Municipal Engineer is still in process of preparing the reports based on the prescribed format; some details of the road network were not yet provided to the Inventory Committee.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to religiously issue demand letters to the beneficiaries to enforce collection; coordinate/ask assistance from other government offices, for instance, COMELEC where current registered addresses of the debtors might be available.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer issued demand letters to the beneficiaries of granted loans and coordinated for assistance from other government offices. Promised partial settlements were arranged on a staggered basis.
observations.reason: Some beneficiaries promised to partially settle their loans on a staggered basis; as of May 23, 2025 a total amount of P763,416.08 was already collected.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal General Services Officer – Designate, Municipal Accountant and the members of the Inventory Committee to follow the procedural guidelines on the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances prescribed under COA Circular No. 2020-006 such as (1) Preliminary activities prior to the conduct of inventory taking; (2) Inventory taking; (3) Reconciliation of inventory count per RPCPPE with property and accounting records; (4) Disposition procedures for non-existing/missing PPEs; (5) Procedures in the derecognition of non-existing/missing PPEs without available record of accountability; and (6) Accounting entries and disclosure in the Notes to Financial Statements..
observations.managementAction: The General Services Officer – Designate have commented on his letter dated May 22, 2025 that they are currently planning to conduct quarterly inventory to account of LGUs assets. Nevertheless, they assured compliance with all the necessary requirements for the conduct of one time cleansing. In addition, during the exit conference, they had added that due to insufficient manpower, they were not able to include personnel in the inventory committee that will conduct one-time cleansing of PPEs.
observations.reason: The Municipality is still on the process of updating their Property Cards and PPE Ledger Cards, thus the conduct of the one-time cleansing of its PPEs were not yet made at year-end. Likewise due to limited manpower to constitute the inventory committee, they were not able to conduct the one-time cleansing of PPEs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to coordinate with the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the discrepancies noted in the debits and credits reported in the monthly transactions of the Cash Book and General Ledger for CY 2023 and immediately update the Audit Team on the results of the reconciliation/adjustment of the reports and records.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant had commented in their letter dated May 16, 2025 that they already made adjustment for the stale checks and had provided copy of Journal Entry Vouchers (JEV) No. 100-2025-05 dated May 2024 had been provided to the Audit Team. She further added on her letter dated May 22, 2025 that an adjusting entry under JEV No. 100-2025-05-24248 was issued for the adjustment of depreciation. Copy of the JEV was provided to the Audit Team. The Municipal Accountant had also submitted the lacking subsidiary ledger of the noted accounts. On the other hand, she had provided the revised GL of Office Supplies Expenses and Donations. As to the unreleased checks, she had ensured compliance with the audit recommendation in the succeeding year-end.
observations.reason: Due to limited manpower and the recent appointment as the head of the Treasurer’s Office, the Acting Municipal Treasurer was not able to conduct regular reconciliation of records with the Accounting Office. Reiterated with modifications in Audit Observation Nos. 2 and 3, Page Nos. 33 to 38, Part II of this Report. For preparation of Recommendation Tracking Sheet (RTS) for recommendations considered closed for having reiterated with modifications.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to review the noted observations above and effect the necessary adjustments after relevant documents are secured. Submit copies of the adjusting journal entries made to the audit team. If adjustments will not be made, submit an explanation letter supported with necessary schedules.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant had commented in their letter dated May 16, 2025 that they already made adjustment for the stale checks and had provided copy of Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) No. 100-2025-05 dated May 2024. She further added on her letter dated May 22, 2025 that an adjusting entry under JEV No. 100-2025-05-24248 was issued for the adjustment of depreciation. Copy of the JEV was provided to the Audit Team. The Municipal Accountant had also submitted the lacking subsidiary ledger of the noted accounts. On the other hand, she had provided the revised GL of Office Supplies Expenses and Donations. As to the unreleased checks, she had ensured compliance with the audit recommendation in the succeeding year-end.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the following: Municipal Accountant to exhaust efforts to secure copies of documents to verify the existence and validity of such fund transfers. Conduct confirmation among identified source agencies to confirm the balances due to them and facilitate the return of the remaining unutilized/unused fund; Municipal Human Resource Management Officer and Municipal Accountant to reconcile employees’ records with the GSIS to identify the affected employees on the unremitted balances of P1,261,973.86 and immediately remit the amounts due to these agencies and identify the cause/s of the over remittance for some accounts and make the proper adjustments to avoid the incurrence of unnecessary financial expenses in the form of imposed penalties.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor will instruct the Municipal Treasurer to provide the Acting Municipal Accountant the list of receivables from operations of Waterworks with the amount due and collectible and the latter to recognize in the books of accounts the accrued income and subsequently follow the accrual method of accounting in recording waterworks system fees.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to withhold those taxes arising from Accounts Payable of CY 2023 still outstanding as of date. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the department heads for the delays in submission of documents pertaining to the obligations incurred at year-end. Likewise, ensure that the corresponding supporting documents of obligations that will form part of Accounts Payable for CY 2024 will be submitted on time to the Accounting Office so as to properly record and withheld taxes and be remitted on the subsequent month.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality had already withheld the taxes which were noted in the CY 2023 AAR. The Municipal Accounting Office had submitted copies of Journal Entry Voucher Nos. 100-2024-03-22130 and 100-2024-03-22131 dated March 8, 2024 for the set-up of Payables for CY 2024 and the corresponding withholding Taxes to Due to BIR account.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the following: a. Provide an explanation for the non-implementation and completion of the identified five projects with an appropriation of P2,657,583.40 and completion within the year of the on-going project of P10,963,728.40; and b. Cause the immediate implementation of the noted unimplemented projects and the completion of the on-going projects identified in the audit finding. Likewise, provide an update to the audit team as to the status of those projects. If the projects will no longer be implemented and the funds reprogrammed to another projects, include those along with the reasons in the status for submission to the audit team.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality had provided the reasons for the non-implementation of the projects and the status. The Municipal Bids and Bid Evaluation Report and other supporting documents were already prepared and provided in the Disbursement Voucher for CY 2024. The Municipality already complied accordingly to the audit recommendations & ensure the completeness of all the supporting documents. The Municipal Engineer prepared the Report of Inspection, Certificate of Completion and Acceptance. The Municipality already prepared the Request Letter of Payment and Affidavit of Payment of labors and materials were already attached in the Disbursement Vouchers starting June 2024 onwards.

observations.recommendation: Based on the foregoing observations, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: a. Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office (MDRRMO) to implement the programs, projects and activities under the Current, Continuing Appropriation and Trust Liability, which were noted in this audit findings. Conduct thorough planning and analysis to ensure that those identified PPAs to be included in the LDRRM Plan are those that can be implemented and completed within the target period; b. Municipal Accountant to provide Subsidiary Ledgers per year of transfer of the unexpended balance of DRRM Funds that were transferred to the Trust Liability Account to fully identify which of the balances are over five years and due for transfer to the General Fund for programming under social services; and c. MDRRMO and Municipal Accountant to conduct periodic reconciliation of their reports pertaining to the plans, budgets and utilization of the 5 percent LDRRMF, starting from the observations noted in this audit finding.
observations.managementAction: The MDRRMO had provided the following updates on the noted PPAs: various PPAs were implemented or partially utilized; some funds were realigned; Formulation of CLUP, CDRA, LCCAP On-going; Construction of Flood Control System and other projects were implemented and funds utilized. The Municipal Budget Officer will evaluate the unexpended balances from completed projects for possible reversion to the unappropriated surplus and be included in the succeeding financial planning and budget deliberations. Likewise, coordination will be made with MDRRMO for the necessary documentation and fund status.
observations.reason: The Municipality partially implemented several projects disbursed under the Trust Fund in CY 2024 were sourced only from fund transfers received for their purpose. Reiterated with modifications in Audit Observation No. 6, Page Nos. 46 to 50, Part II of this Report. For preparation of Recommendation Tracking Sheet (RTS) for recommendations considered closed for having reiterated with modifications.

observations.recommendation: Based on the foregoing observations, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the GFPS Focal Point Person and GAD TWG to conduct thorough planning and analysis to ensure that those identified PPAs to be included in the GAD Plan and Budget for CY 2024 are those that can be implemented and completed within the target period. Take into consideration the feasibility and propriety of the proposed PPA before including it in the plan.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality had partially implemented various PPAs charged under the Gender and Development Fund for CY 2024. The Municipal Budget Officer commented that certain development that necessitates re-alignment of funds have occurred. However, they also disclosed that the funds were realigned to other GAD-related programs that can optimize utilization of available resources. They assured compliance with the audit recommendation through enhancing the capacity of the GFPS – Technical Working Group on results-based gender planning. They also ensured that future PPAs are timely, feasible and fundable within the fiscal year.
observations.reason: Due to arise of demands and circumstances that necessitate immediate action, some of the PPAs were not implemented and their funds have been reprogrammed to another purpose.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the following: a. Municipal Accountant to exhaust efforts to secure copies of documents to verify the existence and validity of the fund transfers. Conduct confirmation among identified source agencies to confirm the balances due to them and facilitate the return of the remaining unutilized/unused fund; and b. Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to communicate with the departments concerned the fund transfers which are subject for full utilization/immediate implementation. Provide updates to the audit team as to the status of those unimplemented/on-going projects.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant will exert effort to confirm and verify from the sources of long outstanding fund transfers. Subsequently, they will also initiate the process of return once the balances had been verified. Some fund transfers were partially utilized in CY 2025 as shown in the submitted subsidiary ledger as of May 2025. The Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator is coordinating with the concerned offices; however, due to voluminous workloads and limited manpower, the coordination and retrieval of documents and information are ongoing.
observations.reason: Due to voluminous workloads and limited manpower, the Municipal Accountant and Planning Coordinator were still in the process of coordinating with various Department Heads to obtain information and secure documents for those unexpended fund transfers.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to: a. Provide justification why cash advances are granted to the two disbursing officers despite previous cash advances which were still unliquidated and submission of disbursement vouchers lacking the required certification from their office; b. Ensure that cash advances will only be granted to accountable officers with no previous unliquidated cash advances; and c. Ensure that Liquidation Reports were completely filled-up particularly the control numbers and date of minor liquidation.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality provided the reasons for granting cash advances to disbursing officers with existing unliquidated advances, citing that the purpose of those cash advances are mostly payment of honorarium and salaries where authorizing officials who will sign in the disbursements were on official leave. Since those expenses must be immediately paid and settled, cash advances were made despite their previous cash advances were unsettled. The Municipality indicated that they will renew the fidelity bond in CY 2025 and consider the noted observation in determining the amount of premiums to be made to increase the amount of accountability of the disbursing officers. The Municipal Accountant replied that as of her writing, their Municipality had only one disbursing officer who will be allowed to make cash advances. Nevertheless, they will coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer and adhere with the audit recommendations on the proper grant and liquidation of cash advances.
observations.reason: No action had been taken earlier due to the newly designated Acting Municipal Treasurer being in the process of familiarizing with the rules and regulations on cash advances; some cash advances granted in early 2024 exceeded the accountability due to reliance on previous bond applications. Reiterated with modifications in Audit Observation No. 9, Page Nos. 54 to 56, Part II of this Report. For preparation of Recommendation Tracking Sheet (RTS) for recommendations considered closed for having reiterated with modifications.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to: a. limit the grant of cash advance only to those cases mentioned in COA Circular 97-002, for regular cash advances for salaries, commutable allowances, honoraria and current operating expenditures and for special cash advance for travel expenditures and current operating expenditures undertaken in the field where it is impractical to pay by check; and b. ensure compliance with procurement rules.
observations.managementAction: Cash advances granted for CY 2024 by the Disbursing Officers were only for those allowable expenses provided in COA Circular No. 97-002.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the following: a. GSO – Designate to prepare and submit the consolidated Property Inventory Form (PIF) listing of all the insurable properties and other assets to GSIS for their evaluation and approval, copy furnish the Office of the Auditor, in compliance to Section 5 of RA No. 656 and COA Circular 2018-002; and b. Municipal Budget Officer to take into consideration including in the annual budget adequate appropriations to fund the application of insurance of all the insurable properties. If the same is not feasible, provide an explanation and submit it to the audit team.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality had already started coordination with GSIS for the determination of the amount of insurance of its insurable properties. The Municipality is still on the process of determining all the insurable property. The Consolidated Property Inventory Form were still on-going.
observations.reason: Not implemented due to limited manpower and voluminous PPEs of the Municipality, the identification of insurable properties and preparation of the Consolidated Property Inventory Form were still on-going. Since the audit findings were only communicated to the Municipality after the preparation of the CY 2024 budget, adequate appropriation for the insurance of its properties were not made.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the following: • Municipal Engineering to conduct thorough inspection of every completed projects and prepare the Inspection Report before issuance of the Certificate of Completion and Acceptance. Likewise, prepare the Statement of Time Elapsed to provide useful information on project implementation including completion, suspensions, resume order, time extensions and slippage. • Municipal Engineer to require the preparation and submission of As-built Plans by the contractor, subject for their review and evaluation. • Municipal Accountant ensure that CY 2024 claims of contractors for completed works were duly supported with Request Letter of Payment, Affidavit of Payment of Materials and Laborers and other necessary supporting documents as required under COA Circular No. 2012-001 before certifying in the Disbursement Voucher.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality had already complied accordingly to the audit recommendations & ensure the completeness of all the supporting documents. Bids and Bid Evaluation Report which showed the necessary eligibility and screening process done by the BAC were already prepared and provided in the Disbursement Voucher for CY 2024. The Municipal Engineer had already started requiring the contractor to prepare and submit the As-built Plans. Request Letter of Payment and Affidavit of Payment of Laborers and Materials were already attached in the Disbursement Vouchers starting June 2024 onwards.
observations.reason: Since the audit findings were only communicated to the Municipality on May 2024 they were not aware of the said supporting documents; some Disbursement Vouchers for payments January to May 2024 still lacked documents which were later submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality adhere to the provisions of Sections 4(3) and 4(8) of PD No. 1445 that trust fund be utilized for the specific purpose it was intended and exercise sound fiscal administration in the local government operations.
observations.managementAction: In compliance to the provisions of PD No. 1445, the management will adhere to the audit recommendations. The projects disbursed under the Trust Fund in CY 2024 were sourced only from fund transfers received for their purpose. The Cash in Bank – Local Currency, Current Account was reclassified into receivable account.
observations.reason: The Municipality still incurred cash deficit due to the closed depository bank from previous years under the Trust Fund, which the Cash in Bank – Local Currency, Current Account was reclassified into receivable account.

observations.recommendation: Therefore, we recommended that the Municipality provide a definite plan or program regarding utilization of the procured inventory to make it beneficial to the identified end-users and to save the property from further deterioration and risk of loss.
observations.managementAction: Sewing machine and foot spa were already distributed to the beneficiaries.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality fully implement the approved Municipal Solid Waste Management Plan covering the period CY 2020 to 2029 in compliance with declared policy of the State to adopt a systematic, comprehensive and ecological solid waste management program and knowing that the LGUs are primarily responsible for the implementation and enforcement of the provisions of the Ecological Solid Waste Management Act.
observations.managementAction: As a 4th class Municipality, it is more cost effective to form partnership or enter into a Memorandum of Agreement with the Sanitary Landfills (SLFs) instead of developing our LGU owned landfill because aside from the positive budgetary parameters from establishing partnership with SLFs rather than having own, the majority of Municipality’s geographical location is included on the National Integrated Protected Area System (NIPAS) and/or the Protected Area Management Bureau – Taal Volcano Protected Landscape (PAMB-TVPL). The EMB suggested that we can update our Solid Waste Management plan only for the preparation on the deliberation for the CY 2029 because any alterations to the existing plan won’t take effect unless it has undergone deliberation. Geographical Location of Municipality of Sta. Teresita was presented for reference.
observations.reason: Update of Solid Waste Management for the succeeding plan in the CY 2029 will be allowed on the deliberation for the CY 2029.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to provide the Acting Municipal Accountant the list of receivables from operations of Waterworks with the amount due and collectible and the latter to recognize in the books of accounts the accrued income and subsequently follow the accrual method of accounting in recording waterworks system fees.
observations.managementAction: The former Municipal Treasurer and the newly designated Acting Municipal Treasurer will instruct the Municipal Treasurer to provide the Office of the Municipal Accountant with the list of receivables from operation of Waterworks with the amount due and collectible. Transferring MOA had already been signed by both parties as well as operation. Operations of Waterworks were scheduled to be transferred to private entity (PPP) Balibago Waterworks with operation.
observations.reason: Transferring MOA had already been signed by both parties as well as operation. The Municipal Treasurer is yet to provide the list of receivables from operation of Waterworks to the Municipal Accountant.

observations.recommendation: Therefore, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to effect the necessary correcting entries to reflect the transfer of the completed projects to their respective asset or PPE account depending on the purpose and intention of the LGU on the use of such. Henceforth, ensure that CIP accounts by year-end only pertain to those ongoing projects with accumulated cost or incurred progress billings during construction.
observations.managementAction: Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) No. 300-24-01-02827, 300-24-01-02832 and 300-24-01-02830, dated January 1, 2024, were drawn by the Municipal Accountant to correct the Constructions in Progress account.

observations.recommendation: Therefore, we reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant, in cooperation with the Municipal Budget Officer, to reconcile the figures in the SCBAA and in the Statement of Financial Performance, have it disclosed in the Notes to Financial Statements as required in IPSAS 24 and ensure its prompt submission to the Commission on Audit, otherwise sanction per Section 122 of PD 1445 should be imposed.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget Officer reconciled the figures in the SCBAA and the Statement of Financial Performance, and the reconciled information was duly disclosed in the Notes to the Financial Statements for CY 2024.

observations.recommendation: Hence, property, plant and equipment (PPE) accounts have not yet been reclassified to their proper accounts affecting their fair presentation in the CY 2023 financial statements. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to implement the provisions of Sections 4.1 and 4.2 of COA Circular No. 2022-004. Henceforth, use the new capitalization threshold in recording similar assets.
observations.managementAction: JEV No. 100-24-01-26544/26540/26548/30735/26543/26542 26545, all dated January 1, 2024 were drawn by the Municipal Accountant to reclass semi-expendable properties. For CY 2024, the Municipality had adopted the capitalization threshold for PPE.

observations.recommendation: Hence, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant, in cooperation with the Municipal Treasurer, to: a. Review the figures posted in the subsidiary ledger versus actual payroll deductions to ensure accuracy and make the necessary correcting entries, if necessary; and b. Ensure that posting of transactions is done on the proper subsidiary ledger to monitor the balances; c. Identify the affected employees on the unremitted balances and immediately remit the amounts due to the concerned agencies. Identify the cause/s of the over-remittance for some accounts and make the proper adjustments.
observations.managementAction: JEV No. 100-24-01-26488 dated January 1, 2024 was drawn by the Municipal Accountant to correct posting errors made in Due to GSIS, Pag-IBIG, PhilHealth and BIR (Withholding Tax). Proof of remittance and/or reconciliation has not yet been provided to the Audit Team. Several posting errors were still noted for CY 2024.
observations.reason: Several posting errors were still noted for CY 2024.

observations.recommendation: Therefore, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to reclassify the remaining balance to the appropriate income accounts and record the subsequent 20% share of the Municipality from the Fire Code Fees as Permit Fees in the General Fund pursuant to COA Circular No. 2015-009 and RA 9514.
observations.managementAction: JEV No. 300-24-01-02829 under TF and JEV No. 100-24-01-26507 under GF, both dated January 1, 2024, were drawn by the Municipal Accountant to reclassify the remaining 20% share of the Municipality from the Fire Code Fees.

observations.recommendation: We recommended stop granting allowances to DepEd personnel considering the absence of legal basis for such otherwise succeeding similar transactions will be disallowed. For the granted financial assistance to Barangays, require the Punong Barangays to submit official receipts acknowledging the financial assistance and liquidation reports and supporting documents evidencing the disbursement of funds related to the Barangay activities for which the funds were intended. Lastly, strictly adhere to the documentary requirements set for all types of cash advances and their liquidation.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer presented proof of liquidation of allowances. For the grants to barangays, Notice of Disallowances were issued to the persons liable.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to submit the cash advance voucher and its supporting documents for confidential expenses within the prescribed period to enable the Audit Team to render timely audit of the said transaction.
observations.managementAction: The cash advance voucher and supporting documents for confidential expenses were submitted to the Audit Team within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the HRMO, in coordination with the concerned Department Heads, to consider the update of daily wage rate of Job Order workers of the Municipality taking into consideration the daily minimum wage rate in the private sector, and if funds are available, daily rate of comparable position in the government sector.
observations.managementAction: As of January 1, 2025, the salary of JO personnel was increased from P250.00 to P300.00, the driver’s current salary is P350.00, and the Municipal Emergency Response and Disaster Operation and Traffic Coordination Group is P400.00 per day.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the GAD Focal Point System to prepare the GAD Agenda in accordance with Section 4.C.2 of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular and the guidelines per PCW Memorandum Circular 2018-04.
observations.managementAction: A GAD Agenda has been prepared and was evaluated and duly approved by the SB. The GAD Agenda has been prepared and evaluated and approved.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a. The Inventory Committee, immediately after conduct of complete physical inventory, to reconcile inventory with property records; b. The Property and Accounting units to reconcile the PPE balance and make necessary adjustments/recording entries, if any; c. The Inventory Committee to ensure that all counted items are properly specified and identified detailing physical attributes and location; d. Municipal Accountant disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements the results of reconciliation of RPCPPE and accounting records as well as the amount of PPE items, if any, without available supporting documents; and e. The existing Inventory Committee or a newly created Inventory Committee study the provisions of COA Circular 2020-006 on one-time cleansing of PPE and consider its immediate implementation to account for the non-existing PPEs and to present reliable balances in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: An Inventory Committee was created; the Inventory Committee conducted reconciliation and prepared required reports. The Inventory Committee conducted activities and reconciliation; reports were prepared and submitted. The actions have been implemented and revised reports were approved by the Regional Director on June 28, 2024.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to: a. Recognize income in the Trust Fund books upon utilization of funds received from national agencies or any other sources, whether private or government, that are considered as non-exchange transactions and record entries relevant to the transactions; and b. Prepare and submit for succeeding years the Statement of Financial Performance for the Trust Fund along with other financial statements of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant prepared the Statement of Financial Performance for the Trust Fund for CY 2024 and submitted other relevant statements. The recommendation was considered in subsequent years and included in Part II.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the balances of the RPT and SET Receivables and their reciprocal accounts with the total Tax Delinquency reported and effect the necessary adjustments.
observations.managementAction: The RPT and SET Receivables, along with their corresponding accounts, were reconciled with the total tax delinquencies reported by the Municipal Treasurer as of December 31, 2024.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated prior recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant, in cooperation with the Municipal Budget Officer, to reconcile the figures in the SCBAA and in the Statement of Financial Performance, disclose them in the Notes to Financial Statements as required in IPSAS 24 and ensure prompt submission to COA.
