observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget Officer reconciled the figures in the SCBAA and the Statement of Financial Performance, and the reconciled information was duly disclosed in the Notes to Financial Statements for CY 2024.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer to: a. Provide a backup computation of the project’s ABC for re-evaluation to justify the cost, otherwise, the excess amount shall be refunded; and b. Immediately require the contractor to complete the project as soon as possible and impose sanctions and penalties, if warranted. Further, compute the amount of liquidated damages to be deducted on the payments to the contractor.
observations.managementAction: ND No. 25-01-300-(20) dated January 21, 2025 was issued to persons found liable. Partial refund had been made by the LGU officials and employees. The remaining amount of P18,000.00 is yet to be collected from resigned/retired employees.

observations.recommendation: Henceforth, strictly comply on the timely submission of the liquidation reports and supporting documents. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to immediately submit the liquidation reports and the lacking supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor for verification, otherwise, the concerned shall be subject to Section 122 of PD No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant submitted the noted liquidation reports and supporting documents and implementation actions were taken as necessary.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor stop the practice and require the Municipal Treasurer to: a. make necessary action to inform and demand the concerned personnel to immediately settle the amounts due, and cause immediate collection of the amounts due; b. regularly send demand letters and statement of accounts to the listed debtors to settle their obligations; c. Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to verify and reconcile the discrepancies in the existing records of various Advances accounts; d. analyze the accounts that are dormant and long outstanding and make necessary actions for their proper disposition in compliance with COA Circular No. 2016-005.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer has taken measures to recover balances and has presented proof of collection for some amounts. The Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant have been coordinating for reconciliation. Some demand letters and follow-ups were undertaken.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require: a. the Inventory Committee to fully account for all the PPEs of the Municipality and conduct Regional Director investigation/disposition of those properties reported in lumpsum amounts; b. the Inventory Committee to prepare the RPCPPE properly using the format and contents prescribed under Annex 49, Chapter II of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, and ensure submission to the Auditor concerned on or before January 31 of each year; c. Municipal Accountant and ICO-MGSO to prepare and maintain updated records specifically of the lacking and outdated PPELCs and Property Cards, respectively; and d. concerned offices to strictly comply with the provisions of Section 375 RA No. 7160, Sections 111 and 122 of PD No. 1445, and COA Circular No. 2020-006.
observations.managementAction: Inventory Committee and related actions were implemented. The Inventory Committee conducted activities; the RPCPPE and related reports were prepared and reconciled; revised reports were approved by the Regional Director on June 28, 2024.

observations.recommendation: Therefore, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor require: a. Municipal Treasurer to strategize ways and exert all efforts to collect the unsettled cash advances from persons liable and require repayment; b. Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to refrain from granting subsequent cash advances to concerned officials and employees to ensure previous advances are liquidated; c. strictly comply with rules and regulations on the granting and liquidation of cash advances; d. issue demand letters; e. request write-off of unliquidated balances of deceased employees where applicable.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer and related officials took steps to notify and collect outstanding cash advances; settlements have been effected for certain amounts and some side actions were started. The Municipal Treasurer provided proof of liquidation of balances totaling P507,000.00. A write-off was approved by the Sangguniang Bayan for certain amounts and the Municipal Accountant provided documentation.

observations.recommendation: Henceforth, strictly enforce the rules and regulations embodied under COA Circular No. 97-002 requiring accountable officers to maintain and reconcile cashbooks and ensure arrived book balances correspond to actual cash in the possession of the accountable officer at all times.
observations.managementAction: Cashbooks were prepared and maintained by the accountable officers; reconciliations and compliance with COA Circular No. 97-002 were observed.

observations.recommendation: Foregoing considered, we reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to: a. disclose reconciliation of Actual Amounts on a Comparable Basis and Actual Amounts in the Financial Statements; and b. fully observe the prescribed requirements of IPSAS and the use of the proforma Notes to Financial Statements as prescribed by COA Circular No. 2016-004.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant submitted reconciliation of SCBAA and Financial Statements. The prescribed proforma Notes to Financial Statements are being followed by the Municipal Accountant.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to observe proper recording of trust receipts and disbursements under the Trust Fund; refrain from making shortcut entries for proper monitoring of account balances; and prepare Statement of Financial Performance for the Trust Fund.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant prepared required adjusting entries and observed proper recording of trust fund transactions. The Statement of Financial Performance for the Trust Fund was prepared.

observations.recommendation: Henceforth, strictly comply on the timely submission of liquidation reports and supporting documents. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to immediately submit the liquidation reports and the lacking documents to the Office of the Auditor for verification; otherwise, the concerned shall be subject to suspension of salary under Section 122 of PD No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: The necessary liquidation reports and supporting documents were submitted and verified, and the municipal accountant provided evidence for the settlements. Some documentation still pending submission to the Audit Team.
observations.reason: Some supporting documents were still pending submission to the Audit Team at the time of reporting.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to reclassify the remaining balance to appropriate income accounts and record the subsequent 20% share of the Municipality from the Fire Code Fees as Fees, shares from fees collected from cutting coconut trees remitted by the Philippine Coconut Authority as Share from National Wealth and shares from STL as Share from PCSO in the General Fund pursuant to COA Circular No. 2015-009, RA 9514 and BLGF MC No. 03-01-2017.
observations.managementAction:
In CY 2024, the Municipal Accountant duly recorded the taxes withheld in the accounting books concurrently with the establishment of Accounts Payable for construction/infrastructure projects partially or fully completed and inspected pursuant BIR Memorandum Circular No. 10-2018.

Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) Nos. 300-2024-05-02151 and 100-2024-05-39996, both dated May 2024, were used to facilitate transfer and reclassification of income from the GOCC.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to recognize in the books the taxes withheld simultaneously with the setting up of Accounts Payable for goods received or services rendered, inspected and accepted.
observations.managementAction: In CY 2024, the Municipal Accountant duly recorded the taxes withheld in the accounting books concurrently with the establishment of Accounts Payable for construction/infrastructure projects partially or fully completed and inspected pursuant BIR Memorandum Circular No. 10-2018.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Budget Officer to prepare the Registry of Appropriations, Allotments and Obligations (RAAO) for Continuing Appropriations which would be used as basis to prepare SAAOB and subsequently SCBAA to ensure accurate balances. Likewise, we recommend that the Municipal Budget Officer verify the discrepancies noted among the reports, reconcile and submit the corrected reports to the Audit Team.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Budget Officer submitted the CY 2024 RAAO for Continuing Appropriations to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Administrator to countersign all the checks drawn by the Municipal Treasurer excluding those checks charged against the SB budget pursuant to the above cited regulations.
observations.managementAction: Management continues the practice of issuing checks to be countersigned by the Municipal Administrator. At the same time, the Sangguniang Bayan has approved of the designation of the Municipal Administrator to countersign the checks. The Municipal Administrator is already bonded with the Bureau of Treasury.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant, DRRMO and Budget Officer to: a. thoroughly assess its actual disaster-related needs and to achieve optimal utilization of the 5 percent Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (LDRRMF) with total unutilized balance of P24,148,695.77, from current and previous years, contrary to Section 12 of Republic Act No. 10121 and COA Circular No. 2012-002 thus, hampering its aim to strengthen the capacity of the Municipality to build a disaster-resilient community and enhance disaster preparedness and response; b. verify the availability of actual cash back up in coordination with the Municipal Treasurer; and c. make necessary adjustments and corrections as necessary (as detailed in the report).
observations.managementAction: The revised Utilization Report for April 2024 was submitted to the Audit Team. The MDRRMO confirmed with the Municipal Treasurer the availability of cash to support the revised Continuing Appropriation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: a. Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant to present savings from completed projects in previous years to be considered in the planning of other disaster preparedness and mitigation PPAs; and b. MDRRMC to evaluate the feasibility of planned PPAs with continuing appropriations and identify projects which will no longer be implemented for possible reversion and reprogramming to other disaster-related PPAs.
observations.managementAction: Savings from completed projects from the previous years were considered in the planning of other disaster preparedness PPAs. An amount of P6,000,937.84 from Continuing Appropriations was reprogrammed and/or implemented in CY 2024.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a. Instruct the GAD Focal Point System to prepare the GAD Agenda in accordance with Section 4.C.2 of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2013-01 and with the guidelines per PCW Memorandum Circular 2018-04; and b. Follow the prescribed format of GAD Accomplishment Report in Annex E of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2013-01.
observations.managementAction: The 6-year GAD Agenda has been prepared and is currently awaiting evaluation and approval by the Sangguniang Bayan (SB). The submitted GAD Accomplishment Report in Annex E of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2013-01 was prepared in accordance with the prescribed format.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to: a. reconcile the balances of the RPT and SET Receivables and their reciprocal accounts with the total Tax Receivables, Delinquency reported, particularly at the end of the year; and b. make the necessary adjustments to their respective records.
observations.managementAction: A reconciliation was conducted by the Municipal Accounting Office and the RPT and SET reciprocal accounts were reconciled with the total tax delinquencies reported by the Municipal Treasurer as of December 31, 2024.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the: a. Municipal Health Officer to obtain from the donors the necessary information as to the unit cost and quantities of the COVID-19 vaccines or any other medicines acquired by the Municipality in the future. Likewise, render a monthly report to the Municipal Accounting Office on the receipt, issuance and balances of vaccines and other inventories directly received from outside source; and b. Municipal Accountant in coordination with the MHO, to determine and recognize in the books the full cost of the ending inventory of vaccines and supplies as at year-end and henceforth record future receipts and issuances of the same.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Health Officer coordinated with the Provincial Health Office to be able to provide the costing of the vaccines; however, the Provincial Health Office could not provide the necessary information due to non-disclosure agreement with the manufacturers. The Municipal Accountant informed that due to the vaccines being already issued and the absence of supporting costing documents or recommendations for the preparation of the Tracking Sheet (RTS) for the MHO, the ending inventory was not recognized in the books. The RTS was considered for preparation but the vaccines were already issued at the time.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the LDRRMO and Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council to: a. prepare the LDRRMFIP in accordance with the prescribed form and procedure; and b. evaluate and study the feasibility of the projects, especially from the unutilized continuing appropriations to ensure delivery of its intended services to the public and maximize all available sources of DRRM Funds.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer (MDRRMO) provided a proposal for utilization which will be reviewed and approved by the MDRRMC. The MDRRMO provided the revised report on sources and utilization and coordinated with the Municipal Treasurer on the availability of cash. The MDRRMO will present the proposal to the MDRRM Council for endorsement.

observations.recommendation: We further recommended that the Municipality follow the guidelines set forth in COA Circular No. 2020-006 for the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts and require the Municipal Mayor to: a. create a separate Inventory Committee for one-time cleansing as required under COA Circular No. 2020-006 and approve the PIP necessary for the conduct and complete physical inventory; b. direct the Inventory Committee to assign Property Number (PN) for each PPE items and incorporate the report such PN; c. instruct the duly created Inventory Committee, the Municipal General Services Office and Accounting Office to reconcile inventory count per RPCPPE with property and accounting records; d. instruct the Municipal Accountant to disclose in the Notes to the Financial Statements the results of reconciliation of RPCPPE and the accounting records as well as the amount of PPE items, if any, without available supporting documents; and e. the existing Inventory Committee or a newly created Inventory Committee study the provisions of COA Circular 2020-006 on one-time cleansing of PPE and consider its implementation to account for the non-existing PPEs and to present reliable balances in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: An Inventory Committee was created and the PIP prepared. The Inventory Committee and concerned offices performed preliminary activities. The Inventory was scheduled and the RPCPPE was submitted to the audit team. The Municipality followed the guidelines of COA Circular No. 2020-006 and excluded certain items as appropriate. The Inventory Committee reconciled records and performed required actions as approved by the Regional Director on June 28, 2024.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated that the Municipal Mayor require the: a. Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to: i. make necessary action to inform and demand the personnel about their obligations to the municipality and cause the immediate refund/restitution of the cash shortage and collection of the amount due from municipal officials and employees representing unpaid personal telephone bills and bank loans; ii. analyze the accounts that are dormant and long outstanding and make necessary actions for their proper disposition in compliance with COA Circular No. 2016-005; b. coordinate with DepEd personnel and exhaust all possible means to reconstruct or locate the liquidation documents/transmittal of records submitted by them in support to their unliquidated cash advances; c. initiate appropriate legal actions to recover from officials and employees who were already separated from the service; d. intensify collections of Loans and Other Receivables, designate key personnel who will monitor the loans/assistance granted and enforce payment thereof. Otherwise, resort to appropriate legal remedies; and e. coordinate with DepED personnel and exhaust all possible means to reconstruct or locate the liquidation documents/transmittal of records submitted by them in support to their unliquidated cash advances.
observations.managementAction: Demand letters were sent to listed debtors. Restitution of the cash shortage of the former Municipal Assistant Treasurer was acknowledged by supporting documents. The Municipal Accountant is reviewing dormants and determining eligibility for write-off and analyzing accounts to determine necessary actions. Some legal actions were not pursued given priorities.
observations.reason: The Management did not give sufficient focus and priority to take legal action to recover from separated employees; The matter has not been given much priority and documents are not readily available.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: a. BAC to submit justification on the noted observation and submit immediately all lacking documents enumerated in Annex K to the Office of the Auditor for verification and audit; and b. require the Municipal officials to demand liquidated damages from erring contractors and enforce strictly and sternly the penalties provided under Paragraph 8.1 of Annex E of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 on the incurrence of delays in the completion of government procurement contracts in order to safeguard government resources as well as the interest of all citizenry.
observations.managementAction: The BAC submitted the Annual Procurement Plan and the noted plans/drawings of the projects to the Office of the Auditor for verification. The Municipality collected liquidated damages for some identified projects: P3,612.08 and P2,841.17 as noted in the report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the: a. Municipal Accountant in coordination with Municipal Human Resources Office to exert extra effort to locate the supporting documents pertaining to unpaid/remitted underpayment of GSIS premium of municipal officials and employees and withheld in 2024 and compare the payroll records with that of the GSIS schedule to determine if really there are amounts not unpaid/ underpayments remitted by the Municipality; and b. Municipal Accountant and her staff to expedite the analysis of the Due to GSIS account to eliminate the P120,112.73 negative balance and make necessary adjustment/s, if warranted. Attach relevant documents and analysis of the accounts to the JEVs for any adjustment to be made.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality is currently reconciling payroll records and GSIS schedules. The Municipal Accountant and staff are reviewing payroll records and remittances to determine if discrepancies relate to employees on leave without pay or under suspension. The matter is being reconciled with GSIS.
observations.reason: The monthly payroll records for 2012 and 2013 could not be located; the negative balance originated from unresolved discrepancies in prior years and further analysis is ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the management agreed that the Municipal Mayor require the Human Resource Management Officer, Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to control the accommodation given to financing institutions and to ensure that minimum monthly take-home pay of Five Thousand Pesos (P5,000.00) prior to approval.
observations.managementAction: The HRMO monitors loan applications and coordinates with the Treasurer and Accountant to ensure that the monthly net take-home pay does not fall below the minimum threshold of Five Thousand Pesos prior to approval.

observations.recommendation: We further recommended that the Management submit a written justification for non-filing of application for disposal/divestment of Talisay Public Market as well as that of other unserviceable property plant and equipment to the Commission. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit the JEVs and their supporting documents which was used as basis for drawing a JEVs for the derecognition for the said property plant and equipment.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant submitted the JEV and supporting documents which were used as basis for derecognition of some properties. The Inventory of PPE derecognized and Waste Materials Report (WMR) were prepared. Some unserviceable material documentation was not available and the Municipality prepared necessary documentation and submitted available JEVs to COA.
observations.reason: Some supporting documents for certain derecognized items were not available at the time but JEVs were submitted to COA.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to: a. Release the retention money to the concerned suppliers in accordance with Section 62.1 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184; and b. Conduct a thorough review and analysis and ascertain the cause of the negative and small amount of balances of this payable accounts and effect the necessary reclassification or adjustments. Attach relevant documents and analysis of the accounts to the JEVs for any adjustment to be made;
observations.managementAction: Released the retention money of four (4) suppliers as of December 2023. The Municipal Accountant coordinated with BPLO to gather the documents of the existing businesses with suppliers. A reconciliation was conducted; matters are ongoing to locate documents and to effect necessary adjustments.
observations.reason: Located the supporting documents for some suppliers; other supporting documents remain missing and reconciliation and adjustments are ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to: a. Review the noted observations regarding depreciation, effect the necessary adjustments and submit copies of the adjusting journal entries to the audit team for further evaluation. Henceforth, strictly observe the policies on the recognition of depreciation for PPE prescribed in PAG 3 and 6 of IPSAS 17 and Section 14 of COA Circular No. 2015-008; and b. Record the advance collections during the year and in the succeeding years under the Deferred Real Property Tax and Deferred Special Education Tax accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality had submitted Journal Entry Voucher Nos. 100-2024-02-329 to 332 dated February 28, 2024 and 100-2024-04-778 dated April 30, 2024 to record adjustments in depreciation. Similarly, Journal Entry Voucher No. 100-2024-4-721 dated April 30, 2024 had been drawn to adjust the unexpired portion of insurance. Income in CY 2024 per Statement of Comparison of Budget and Actual Amounts (SCBAA), Reconciliation Statement of SCBAA in Notes to Financial Statements and per Statement of Financial Performance was reconciled.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the: a. Local Scholarship Committee, Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator, Municipal Budget Officer, Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to provide an explanation letter for the non-implementation of the project with an appropriation of P400,000.00 and inconsistencies on the reported figures of submitted reports and documents; b. Local Scholarship Committee to ensure that the guidelines provided in the Executive Order are met and strictly followed in the grant of scholarship and educational assistance for CY 2024. If amendments are to be made, ensure that approval of the Sangguniang Bayan will be secured. Likewise, ensure that scholars who graduated were updated regularly to check if the goal of the program materialized; and c. Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to cause the immediate implementation of the unimplemented project. Likewise, provide an update to the audit team as to the status of the projects. If the projects will no longer be implemented and the funds reprogrammed to another projects, include those along with the reasons in the status for submission to the audit team.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality submitted an explanation letter on the scholarship and educational assistance and the reasons of the non-implementation of the project. The Local Scholarship Committee strictly followed the guidelines established in the grant of scholarship and educational assistance. Likewise, Sangguniang Bayan Resolution No. 2024-075 dated October 21, 2024 was issued, approving Executive Order (EO) No. 30 series of 2024. The said EO established the guidelines for the provision of local educational assistance program of the Municipality. The unimplemented project was declared as savings and reprogrammed into other PPA in CY 2024. The Municipality submitted the sources and utilization reports for reconciliation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: a. Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office (MDRRMO) to implement and complete the programs, projects and activities under Current Appropriation and Trust Liability in the succeeding years; b. Municipal Accountant to provide Subsidiary Ledgers per year of transfers of the unexpended balance of DRRM funds that were transferred to the Trust Liability Account to fully identify which of the balances are over five years and due for transfer to the General fund for programming under social services; and c. MDRRMO, Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Budget Office to conduct periodic reconciliation of their reports pertaining to the plans, budgets and utilization of the 5 percent LDRRMF, starting from the observations noted in this audit finding.
observations.managementAction: For CY 2024, subsidiary ledgers per year of transfer were maintained by the Municipal Accounting Office. The MDRRMO, Municipal Accounting Office and Budget Office for the sources and utilization of LDRRMF for CY 2024 have been reconciled. The Municipality committed to improving its internal systems for planning, implementation, and coordination to ensure efficient use of the LDRRMF and uphold transparency and accountability.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Accountant to: a. Provide explanation letter for the occurrence of granting cash advances despite existence of previous unliquidated cash advances; b. Ensure that cash advances will only be granted to accountable officers with no previous unliquidated cash advances and attach the necessary certification in the Disbursement Voucher; and c. Municipal Treasurer to maintain separate cash book per disbursing officer for proper monitoring of their accountabilities.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant submitted an explanation letter stating that due to having only two disbursing officers, cash advances were granted without prior settlement. The Municipal Treasurer’s Office maintains cashbook per disbursing officer. The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer commented that they will comply with the provisions of Treasury Circular No. 02-2019 and ensure the timely renewal of the fidelity bond prior to its expiration and will implement regular monitoring.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Human Resource Management Officer and Municipal Accountant to reconcile employees’ records with the GSIS, PhilHealth and PAG-IBIG to identify the affected employees on the unremitted balances and immediately remit the amounts due to these agencies and identify the cause/s of the over remittance for some accounts, if there is any, and make the proper adjustments to avoid the incurrence of unnecessary financial expenses in the form of imposed penalties.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant and HRMO had started reconciling the records for CY 2024.

observations.recommendation: We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant provide depreciation to all properties of the Municipality as provided in IPSAS 17 and further emphasized in Philippine Application Guidance 3 and 4, issued by Public Sector Accounting Standards Board (PSASB), to fairly present the affected accounts in the financial statements. Likewise, it was mentioned that recorded amount of P103,425.00 was already the Net Book Value of the PPE which was used as the new cost upon conversion from New Government Accounting System (NGAS) to Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards.
observations.managementAction: The submitted lapsing schedule as of December 31, 2024 showed no depreciation for the item noted in the audit finding – Other Property, Plant and Equipment account under the Special Education Fund which pertains to the acquisition of prior years. The Municipality submitted Journal Entry Voucher Nos. 100-2025-05-922 dated May 2, 2025 to effect noted adjustment.
observations.reason: Closed for having been revised/restated and approved by the Regional Director on July 3 2024, to be excluded in the ensuing year report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality adhere to the guidelines provided in Section C.1 (5) of Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 of the Philippine Commission on Women (PCW), Department of Interior and Local Government (DILG), Department of Budget and Management (DBM) and National Economic and Development Authority (NEDA) to effectively plan and implement GAD PPAs of the LGU: a. Conduct thorough planning and analysis to ensure that those identified PPAs to be included in the GAD Plan and Budget are those that can be implemented and completed within the target period. Take into consideration the feasibility and propriety of the proposed PPA before including it in the plan to avoid reversion of funds; and b. Ensure that the actual expenditures were aligned with the GAD PPA in order to address the identified gender issue/gender mandate. Provide supporting documents justifying the determined cost of the noted PPAs that is charged to GAD and explanation letter for those actual expenditures not aligned with the planned activity in the GPB.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality submitted a Supplemental GAD Plan and Budget, along with an explanation letter detailing the reasons for opting not to utilize the fund or change its actual implementation. The listed GAD PPAs for CY 2024 were implemented by the Municipality and the targets were achieved.
observations.reason: Closed for having been revised/restated and approved by the Regional Director on July 3 2024, to be excluded in the ensuing year report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality fully implement the approved Municipal Solid Waste Management Plan covering the period CY 2017 to 2027 in compliance with declared policy of the State to adopt a systematic, comprehensive and ecological solid waste management program and knowing that the LGUs are primarily responsible for the implementation and enforcement of the provisions of the Ecological Solid Waste Management Act.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality continuously implements the plan in the submitted AAPSI. The LGU has intensified its waste collection throughout the year, activated the Material Recovery Facility, mobilized the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board, submitted reportorial documents on solid waste management as required by the DENR, and driven the implementation of the barangay SWM plan. The LGU has an existing venture with a sanitary landfill facility where the LGU has been charged free of service; thus, it does not need the construction of SLF.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the General Services Officer reconcile the physical count report with the accounting records and investigate and clear the noted discrepancies, if any, and render the yearly physical count report of the local road network as prescribed in Section VI (3) of COA Circular No. 2015-008. We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the General Services Officer to maintain the subsidiary records for property, plant and equipment or the property cards prescribed in the accounting manual and reconcile the physical count report with the accounting records, study and resolve the noted discrepancies, if any.
observations.managementAction: The submitted lapsing schedule as of December 31, 2024 showed depreciation and adjustments for various items. The Municipal Accountant recorded adjustments and provided Journal Entry Vouchers to effect noted adjustment in the accounting records.
observations.reason: Closed for having been revised/restated and approved by the Regional Director on July 3 2024, to be excluded in the ensuing year report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality fully implement the approved Municipal Solid Waste Management Plan covering the period CY 2017 to 2027 in compliance with declared policy of the State to adopt a systematic, comprehensive and ecological solid waste management program and knowing that the LGUs are primarily responsible for the implementation and enforcement of the provisions of the Ecological Solid Waste Management Act.
observations.managementAction: The LGU implemented activities under the SWM plan: systematized waste collection among its barangays, activation of Material Recovery Facility, crafting of the 10-year SWM plan and submissions of reportorial requirements. The LGU has an existing venture with a sanitary landfill facility where the LGU has been charged free of service; thus, it does not need the construction of SLF.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to: a. Reclassify the loan payable due beyond 12 months from reporting date to non-current liabilities; and b. Coordinate with the bank for the reconciliation of records and prepare the necessary adjusting entries and/or disclosures on the Notes to Financial Statements, if needed.
observations.managementAction: Coordinated with the bank and effect the proper adjustments.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the accountable officers to provide justification on the late liquidation of cash advances mentioned above. Henceforth, strictly adhere to Section 1.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 on timely liquidation of cash advances.
observations.managementAction: Justification was submitted.

observations.recommendation: Based on the foregoing, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor as Local Chief Executive, instruct the Chairperson of GFPS-TW G to: a. To include programs and activities in the GPB with consideration of the essential elements such as active GFPS, sex disaggregated data and gender analysis; b. To submit the GPB within the deadline to enable timely review and endorsement of the PPDO and DILG; and c. To strictly follow the guidelines in PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 as amended by JMC No. 2016-01.
observations.managementAction: Programs and activities planned and implemented addressed gender issues. Submitted the GPB to the PPDO and DILG within the deadline.
observations.reason: Not Implemented: To strictly follow guidelines – Lack of manpower due to retirement of the previous MSWDO.

observations.recommendation: Based on the foregoing, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Inventory Committee to strictly follow the general guidelines, procedural guidelines, reconciliation of inventory count per RPCPPE with property and accounting records, disposition procedures for non-existing/missing PPEs, procedures in the derecognition of non-existing/missing PPEs without available records of accountability, applicable accounting entries and required disclosure in the Notes to the Financial Statements required under COA Circular No. 2020-006.
observations.managementAction: The GSO – designate to conduct the physical count of its Local Road Network and prepare the Report on the Physical Count of the Local Road Network (RPCLRN). Make use of the template provided in Annex C of COA Circular No. 2015-008 and complete the necessary information in every field and submit the accomplished RPCLRN to the Accounting Office for reconciliation with the books. Also prepare the Road Network Property Card for each Local Road Network.
observations.reason: Leadership transition due to appointment of new OIC in the General Service Office.

observations.recommendation: The GSO – designate to conduct the physical count of its Local Road Network and prepare the Report on the Physical Count of the Local Road Network (RPCLRN). Make use of the template provided in Annex C of COA Circular No. 2015-008 and complete the necessary information in every field and submit the accomplished RPCLRN to the Accounting Office for reconciliation with the books. Also prepare the Road Network Property Card for each Local Road Network.
observations.managementAction: Conducted the physical count of its Local Road Network and submit the Report on the Physical Count.
observations.reason: Leadership transition due to appointment of new Officer in Charge (OIC) in the General Service Office.

observations.recommendation: Based on the foregoing, we recommended the Municipal Mayor to instruct the Municipal Accountant to immediately submit the lacking documents listed in the Annex for further evaluation of the transactions.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant have partially submitted the lacking documents. The Municipal Accountant requested the five remaining lacking documents on April 18, 2023 and eight documents on April 28, 2023.
observations.reason: Partially submitted. The five remaining projects' lacking documents are yet to be submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the municipal accountant and concerned officials/employees to immediately submit the disbursement vouchers, their supporting documents and refund of cash advances. Henceforth, strictly adhere to Section 1.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 on timely liquidation of cash advances.
observations.managementAction: The Official Receipts of the cancelled activities were submitted. The Municipal Accountant submitted the supporting documents and the refund of cash advances for the cancelled activities.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor require: a. Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to submit the following: - approved Annual Procurement Plan or any supplemental or revision thereof inclusive of all purchases made in response to COVID-19 pandemic; - BAC Resolution for the justification of alternative mode of procurement and recommendation for the award of the contract, proof of posting of Request for Quotation, Notice of Award and Purchase Order, Income Tax Return (ITR) for CY 2019, original copy of Omnibus Sworn Statement (OSS) and Mayor’s Permit or Business Permit for CY 2020 of BSMA Trading for the purchase of medicines; and - justification for inconsistencies in the posting of Notice for Negotiated Procurement in the Philippine Government Electronic Procurement System, Contract Agreement and distribution of the relief goods purchased from L and H Marketing under Check No. 1758900.
observations.managementAction: The BAC to submit the required documents. The Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) submitted some documents to the previous Audit Team.
observations.reason: Partially implemented: some documents were submitted to previous Audit Team; others still pending full compliance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to undertake the immediate disposal of unserviceable PPE to declutter the books of non-value items.
observations.managementAction: Inspection and Inventory Report of Unserviceable Property were prepared but not presented to the Audit Team.
observations.reason: Leadership transition due to appointment of new Officer in Charge (OIC) in the General Service Office. Municipality intends to initiate the disposal of unserviceable properties, with a specific focus on motor vehicles that have been reduced to skeleton forms.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Accountant, as part of the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC)-Technical Working Group agreed to submit the documents to avoid suspension in audit.
observations.managementAction: The submissions of the noted lacking documents were undertaken to avoid suspension in audit. The damages were computed and deducted.
observations.reason: Transmittal letters showing submission of lacking documents to the previous Audit Team were not provided to the current Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor to cause the immediate submission of the noted lacking documents. Otherwise, transactions shall be suspended in audit.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant submitted some documents for verification.
observations.reason: Transmittal letters showing submission of the lacking documents to the previous Audit Team were not submitted to the current Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor to require the concerned contractors to rectify immediately the deficiencies noted to conform with the approved program of works. Otherwise, forfeit the Performance Security/Warranty Security and consider blacklisting of the concerned contractors who refuses to effect the necessary corrections and submit documents to support the action taken.
observations.managementAction: Ongoing retrieval of documents. The Municipal Engineer communicated with the contractor for rectification. Liquidated damages were computed and deducted where applicable.
observations.reason: Supporting documents/proof of rectification were not submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: a. Municipal Accountant and the concerned officials/employees to submit the liquidation reports and documents of P114,600.00 cash advances, Itinerary of Travel and Reimbursement Expense Receipt (RER) for van hire totalling P121,743.10. Otherwise, said transactions shall be suspended in audit; and b. Concerned officials/employees to refund the excess P95,700.00 travel expenses and P4,900.00 registration fee.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant submitted the liquidation reports and documents of P114,600.00 cash advances. Out of P95,700.00, a total of P76,980.00 and P9,280.00 had been refunded last CY 2021 and CY 2022, respectively.
observations.reason: Ongoing retrieval of documents for the remaining balance. Full settlement of remaining P9,440.00 was not made as of CY 2022; pending.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the members of the BAC and the TWG to submit justification why the project was awarded to the winning supplier/contractor in spite non-submission of the documentary requirements of RA No. 9184. Request the submission of the lacking documents in order to further validate the propriety of the disbursements.
observations.managementAction: The BAC and TWG were requested to submit the justification and lacking documents.
observations.reason: No justification was received by the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor and the Foreman refund the amount of P100,000.00 and P9,000.00, respectively. To submit the documents pertaining to the payment of Labor payroll for the Improvement of Foot Bridge amounting to P100,000.00.
observations.managementAction: Submitted the computations and bases of payment. Explanation on the bases of payment was submitted by the former Municipal Accountant.
observations.reason: No refund was made in CY 2025 for the P9,000.00; ongoing retrieval of documents since transactions transpired during tenure of former Municipal Accountant and Municipal Engineer.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit the computations and bases on the payments made and provide clarifications and supporting documents.
observations.managementAction: Explanation on the bases of payment was submitted by the former Municipal Accountant.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Assessor to evaluate the appraised values of properties under the jurisdiction of the City based on prevailing market prices and propose the necessary revisions on the City Code and every three years thereafter, to comply with the provisions of Sections 201 and 219 of RA No. 7160.
observations.managementAction: The City Assessor has evaluated the appraised values of properties under the jurisdiction of the City based on prevailing market prices and submitted the same to the City Mayor.
observations.reason: The City Government has yet to prepare a proposal for the necessary revision of the City Code and submit the same to the Sangguniang Panlungsod.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Engineer in coordination with City Planning and Development Officer (CPDO) to:
a. Conscientiously carry out detailed engineering works addressing any issues on the viability of the projects; b. Thoroughly review and evaluate the Program of Works to ensure that project designs and estimates are properly prepared and suit the needs of end-users and that all phases of the projects are covered to minimize time extensions; and c. Strict monitoring and supervision of the implemented projects.
observations.managementAction:
The CPDO and City Engineering Office (CEO) conducted thorough review and evaluation of the viability of the projects. The CPDO enhanced its monitoring and capability in its coordination with the CEO. The CEO conducted further analysis as well as appropriate implementation addressing issues and minimizing suspensions and extensions. Strict monitoring of projects is being implemented by the CEO resulting to the identification and resolution of unforeseen circumstances.
Quarterly Report of Government Project/Program/Activities (GPPA) are already submitted to COA.
observations.reason: There was still late implementation of projects in CY 2023. Several projects planned in CY 2023 were not implemented; Lapses in implementation indicate the need for further analysis of project implementation by the CEO.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Accountant, City Treasurer and the implementing departments (CSWDO, OCVAS, CPDO) to continuously exert efforts to locate the supporting documents and to document all procedures employed to establish the location or whereabouts of the debtors and borrowers including actions taken to collect the amounts; explore other appropriate actions, including filing of appropriate case, if necessary, against erring borrowers; and if all possible actions are exhausted but still no progress then seek approval for write-off of invalid receivables.
observations.managementAction: The City Accounting Office and concerned implementing offices (CSWDO, OCVAS and CPDO) continuously exerted efforts to locate the supporting documents and to collect the amounts. Individual borrowers were traced and collections were made; some borrowers repaid partially and a few fully settled their loans. Plans were made to strategize collections from various debtors.
observations.reason: Some of the borrowers are deceased or cooperatives ceased operations. Records maintained by the City Accountant and concerned offices remain unreconciled due to unavailability of records.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the: a) OIC-City Accountant, in coordination with respective offices, to prioritize the determination and verification of the composition of the dormant asset and payable accounts by tracing back and retrieving all possible documents and records pertinent thereto within 60 days from receipt hereof; and b) City Administrator to submit to the Audit Team the results of validation of the dormant asset and payable accounts and the actions taken thereon duly submitted.
observations.managementAction: The City started tracing back and retrieving possible documents and records pertinent thereto.
observations.reason: Management was not able to undertake the validation of the dormant asset and payable accounts due to difficulty in gathering the necessary documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the City Mayor to direct the OIC-City Accountant to: a) prioritize the reconciliation of records and evaluation of the documents and assess the needs for those unexpended balances from NGAs. Should the funds be proven no longer needed, remit immediately to the SA, but if still needed, request authority to use the fund for other related projects of the City; and b) prepare the appropriate adjusting journal entries for the (i) transfer of the grants and donations recognized in the GF book; and (ii) reclassification of the misclassified livelihood financial assistance within 15 days from receipt hereof.
observations.managementAction: The City Accountant prioritized reconciliation and evaluated documents; some carry over balances still need reconciliation.
observations.reason: Considering that carry over balances still need to be reconciled, management is yet to establish the unexpended amount to be refunded back to SA or to be used for other purpose, subject to authority.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor require the Inventory Committee to strictly follow the procedural guidelines in the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts such as (a) preliminary activities prior to and during the conduct of inventory taking, (b) reconciliation of inventory count per RPCPPE with property and accounting records, (c) disposition for non-existing/missing PPEs, (d) derecognition of non-existing/missing PPEs without available record of accountability, and (e) accounting entries and disclosure in the Notes to the Financial Statements, pursuant to COA Circular No. 2020-006.
observations.managementAction: Inventory taking was conducted for some properties and included in the submitted RPCPPE to the Audit Team. Property cards were being maintained.
observations.reason: While the inventory count and reconciliation of some properties were started, the application of One-Time Cleansing of PPE is still on-going and substantially unimplemented as the concerned personnel could not yet focus thereto due to their regular daily responsibilities.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the: a) Municipal Accountant to reconcile the balances as of December 31, 2020 of RPT and SET Receivables with total delinquency reported by the Municipal Treasurer and effect the necessary adjustment and base the setting up of collectibles at the beginning of the year on the Certified List showing the names of taxpayers and amount collectible for the year to be prepared by the Municipal Treasurer; and b) OIC-Municipal Assessor and Municipal Treasurer to verify and tie-up the identified unidentified real property units (RPUs) and determine the owners of the unidentified RPUs and submit to the Municipal Accountant for proper recording/adjustment or disclosure in the Notes to the Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: The City Treasurer and OIC-Municipal Assessor started the reconciliation of records. Some real property units were determined and corrected by the Office of the City Assessor and City Treasurer.
observations.reason: Some reconciliation remains ongoing and not fully completed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: a) Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain the Local Road Network Ledger Card (LRNLC) for the road and its component namely: a) road lot, b) road pavement, c) drainage and slope protection structures, and d) other structures; and prepare and submit the lapsing schedule for the computation of the depreciation for each road component at the end of the year in accordance with COA Circular No. 2015-008; b) GSO to prepare and submit the Report on the Physical Count of Local Road Network and Report on the Local Road Network in the prescribed format and maintain a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its component; and c) Municipal Engineer to prepare the complete description and cost segregation of road components of every road project undertaken.
observations.managementAction: LRNLC was prepared partially; some data used in the ledger cards of the Accounting Office were provided. The City Engineer and his staff are preparing the required descriptions and cost segregation.
observations.reason: Data on the road lot components of the road networks were still not provided by the Office of the City Engineer due to lack of manpower; lapse in completing lapsing schedule and property cards.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor to instruct the: a. General Services Officer to prepare the PIF by extracting the data from the RPCPPE and submit to Audit Team and GIF of the GSIS which will be used as a basis for the assessment of the general insurance coverage of all the City’s insurable properties pursuant to COA Circular No. 2018-002; b. General Services Officer and the members of the Inventory Committee to adopt the procedural guidelines on the conduct of one-time cleansing of PPE accounts and prepare the Property Inventory Plan (PIP) and submit to the Audit Team pursuant to Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2020-006.
observations.managementAction:
The Management noted that the mentioned inventory working papers were prepared and provided by the CGSO to the Inventory Committee in CY 2024. Likewise, the PIP was prepared and approved by the City Mayor and submitted to the Audit Team on April 29, 2024.
They also assured the Audit Team that they will comply with the audit recommendation and will implement the one-time cleansing of PPEs in CY 2024.
observations.reason: The City is currently in the preliminary stage of the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts. The City plans to implement the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts in CY 2024.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor require the: a. City Budget Officer to prepare and maintain the RAAO and Statement of Appropriations, Allotments, Obligations and Balances (SAAOB) for Continuing Appropriations which will be the basis for the monitoring of appropriation funded out of continuing appropriation and submit the SAAOB to the Office of the City Accountant before the finalization of the SCBAA; and b. City Accountant to prepare the SCBAA based on the SAAOB submitted by the City Budget Officer to present the correct balances of the Continuing Appropriations original and final budgets, and the remaining balances at year-end.
observations.managementAction: The Office of the City Budget Officer reasoned out that RAAO and SAAOB for Continuing Appropriations were provided only by the Office of the City Accountant in February 2024, hence, they will start to prepare RAAO for Continuing Appropriations in CY 2024. The correct balances of original and final budgeted amounts of the Continuing Appropriations were still not presented in the SCBAA as of December 31, 2023.
observations.reason: RAAO and SAAOB for Continuing Appropriations were not prepared by the Office of the City Budget Officer; the balances of previous years’ Continuing Appropriations were accounted only in February 2024, hence, the original and final budgeted amounts as of December 31, 2023 were not presented in the SCBAA.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the duly created Inventory Committee, the City General Services Office and City Accounting Office to strictly follow the general guidelines, procedural guidelines, reconciliation of inventory count per RPCPPE with property and accounting records, disposition procedures for non-existing/ missing PPEs, procedures in the derecognition of non-existing/ missing PPEs without available records of accountability, applicable accounting entries and required disclosure in the Notes to the Financial Statements required under COA Circular No. 2020-006 in the conduct of inventory taking for one-time cleansing of PPE.
observations.managementAction: Likewise, the City initiated its plan to avail of one-time cleansing of PPE in accordance with the guidelines issued by COA. The City Mayor issued Executive Order No. 06 Series of 2022 entitled “Creating the Lipa City Inventory for the Conduct of Physical Count and Disposition of Non-existing/Missing PPE for One-Time Cleansing Pursuant to COA Circular No. 2020-006”. Preliminary activities were conducted to prepare for the application for one-time Cleansing of PPE to ensure proper compliance with the reportorial requirement. As per initial inventory taking of the Inventory Committee in CY 2022, PPEs with total cost of P424,803,028.43 were not found during actual count, hence, missing, demolished and non-existing properties. GSO and Accounting are currently reconciling the inventory count with property and accounting records. Other related documents like Property Cards (PC), Property Acknowledgment Receipt (PAR) and Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) are required being reviewed and updated by GSO. To comply with COA Circular No. 2020-006, the Inventory Committee is actively working on the disposition procedures for non-existing PPEs. Accounting will disclose the result of the one-time cleansing in the 2024 Notes to Financial Statements.
observations.reason: The City is in preliminary activities; one-time cleansing activities and reconciliation are ongoing and planned for CY 2024.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Accountant to establish the correct balance of the total Loans Receivable – Others account by validating/evaluating the related transactions with the records maintained by the City Social Welfare and Development Officer (CSWDO) and City Cooperative Officer and to prepare adjusting entries, as necessary.
observations.managementAction: The Office of the City Accountant is coordinating with the implementing department/offices to validate the records on collections; in compliance with COA Circular No. 2023-008 dated August 17, 2023, the City Accounting Office sent letters to the City Cooperatives Office and CSWD requesting copies of records/details maintained by their respective offices relevant to the loans. The CSWD replied that files/pertinent documents were damaged and destroyed during Typhoon Glenda. The City Cooperatives Office replied that they cannot provide details as no available data pertaining to such transactions. Please refer to Annex L3.
observations.reason: Supporting records maintained by implementing offices were damaged/destroyed or not available; reconciliation and validation with concerned offices pending.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Accountant cause the immediate submission of the remaining unsubmitted paid DVs and their supporting documents to the Auditor for verification and audit as required in Section 347 of Republic Act No. 7160 to avoid the issuance of notices of suspension and/or disallowance in audit on the subject transactions. Otherwise, cause the suspension of payment of the salaries of the officials concerned relative to the unsubmitted DVs pursuant to Section 122(2) of PD No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: Upon validation with depository banks, 360 DVs in CY 2018 and 12 DVs in CY 2017 with total payments of P55,659,769.09 and P1,383,275.38 respectively were reported in the AAR CY 2018, Part II. The City Treasurer informed that the office exerted all possible means to locate the subject vouchers but they could no longer locate them. Banks adhere to processes to ensure cash is released to the rightful payee; issuances of checks were based on duly approved DVs and Accountant’s Advice of Local Disbursement. Some additional DVs were later provided; several DVs were returned to the City Treasurer for issuance of checks to concerned payees.
observations.reason: 13 DVs remained unsubmitted and documents could not be located; some DVs were cancelled and additional documents were processed but not all required DVs were submitted to COA.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Inventory and Disposal Committee, in cooperation with all offices concerned, to prioritize the implementation of the one-time cleansing of PPEs, including reconciliation of accounting records versus physical count pursuant to the provisions and guidelines set forth in COA Circular No. 2020-006.
observations.reason: Gradual disposal of unserviceable properties before the implementation of one-time cleansing. Inventory Committee is yet to be created and there are numerous unserviceable properties.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct: a. The Municipal Accountant to take up necessary accounting entries to recognize unrecorded lots after the conduct of comprehensive reconciliation of lands owned by the Municipality. Also, to take the proper identification and acquisition costs of the concerned parcels of land with noted deficiencies; and b. The Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Assessor to include in the total amount of appropriations the expenses necessary for the transfer of title into the name of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality has appropriated a P1,500,000.00 for transfer of title of land under the Municipality’s name.
observations.reason: The Municipal Assessor is still verifying the identification and acquisition costs of the concerned parcels of land with noted deficiencies. No proper documentation on donated lands received from previous years.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor require the: a. Business Permits and Licensing Officer (BPLO) and OIC-Market Supervisor to maintain a detailed record on the rentals and goodwill payments of all stalls as appropriate receivables and income; and b. Municipal Accountant to support the receivables for unpaid rentals with subsidiary ledgers, accrue receivables and income from any unpaid rentals as of every reporting date and facilitate reconciliation with the reports of the Market Office. Likewise, record receivables for the goodwill payments as they are already due and demandable, and recognize the income and liability for the unearned portion.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant is yet to record the receivables in the book of accounts. The OIC-Market Administrator submitted detailed record on the rentals and goodwill payments to the Audit Team and the Municipal Accountant.
observations.reason: No available data; No records are available to support the recorded rentals and goodwill payments. The Municipal Accountant is yet to record the receivables in the book of accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed to require the: a. GSO to prepare and maintain road networks property cards and Report on Local Road Network based on the report submitted by the Municipal Engineer; b. Inventory Committee to conduct inventory count of all the Municipality’s road network systems and accordingly submit a Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network System as of the end of the year; and c. Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain the prescribed property ledger cards for Local Road Networks and disclose all the required information for the said property in the Notes to Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: Ongoing retrieval of documents.
observations.reason: No records available to support the Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network System. No sufficient documents for those projects implemented by other government agencies.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed to require the Municipal Budget Officer to appropriate in the budget of the LEEs funds for the return of subsidies received including those used for Personal Services. Further, strictly comply with the prohibition of appropriating subsidies from the General Fund for PS costs of LEEs.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Officials will inform the Budget Officer to appropriate in the budget of the LEEs funds for the return of subsidies received.
observations.reason: There is no appropriation in the budget to facilitate the return of subsidies received.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed to require the OIC-Market Administrator to collect the overdue goodwill money particularly those past the 5th year of their lease term, check for and collect their underpayments, require timely and sufficient payments of goodwill money for all stalls and use the rates provided in the 2017 Bauan Market Code, as revised by the 2020 Market Code. We also recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to recognize in the books of accounts the receivables for goodwill money and adjustment of prior years’ income and maintain subsidiary ledgers for each stallholder.
observations.managementAction: The OIC-Market Administrator will inform the stallholders that there will be adjustments in the amount to be paid to the Municipality and will check for and collect underpayments gradually. The Municipal Accountant agreed to recognize in the books of accounts the receivables for goodwill money.
observations.reason: No appropriate reports were submitted to serve as basis for the recording of receivables.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to recognize the receivables of the noted unpaid rentals supported by individual subsidiary ledgers. Prospectively, we recommended to accrue the monthly receivables and income from rentals and establish individual subsidiary ledgers for each stallholder and reconcile unpaid balances with the reports of the Market Administration as of any reporting period. Finally, we recommended and the Sangguniang Bayan agreed to make clarification/amendments on the inconsistencies on the rates of market rental fee and penalty provided in Section 12 of Revised Market Code of 2020 and Section 5A.01 of Article A of Chapter 5 of Vol. II of Revised Revenue Code of Bauan 2020.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant agreed to recognize the receivables of the noted unpaid rentals supported by individual subsidiary ledgers. Prospectively, the Municipal Accountant agreed to accrue the monthly receivables and income from rentals and establish individual subsidiary ledgers for each stallholder and reconcile unpaid balances with the reports of the Market Administration. The Sangguniang Bayan agreed to make clarification/amendments on the inconsistencies.
observations.reason: No appropriate reports were submitted to serve as basis for the recording of receivables. The Revenue Code and Market code are for revision and are currently under Committee level.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Treasurer and BPLO to verify, assess and validate the reported under collection totaling P708,688.76 and consequently demand the collection/ payment thereof or recouped through adjustment of fees to be collected in the ensuing year. The absence of documented action or information to counter the under collection shall mean the issuance of the corresponding Notice of Charge.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer prepared the schedules and letters of communication for business entities with underpayments.
observations.reason: No appropriate reports were submitted to serve as basis for the recording or recovery; actions being undertaken (schedules/letters prepared).

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant set-up and maintain Special Accounts in the General Fund for the monitoring of operating performance and viability of the Municipality’s Economic Enterprises and 20% DF, and to serve as a decision-making tool of the agency. Also, record all transactions pertaining to these economic enterprises to the corresponding subsidiary ledgers.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant will set-up and maintain Special Accounts in the General Fund for the monitoring of operating performance and viability of the Municipality’s Economic Enterprises and 20% DF, and will record all transactions pertaining to these economic enterprises to the corresponding subsidiary ledgers.
observations.reason: Special accounts in the General Fund for the economic enterprises and utilization of the 20% Development Fund are being prepared.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to request the write-off of receivables with no certainty of collection, except for those recorded under the Receivable-Disallowances/ Charges account pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005, or follow-up the request if there’s a pending request for its writing-off from the Commission on Audit. Submission of all proofs that efforts have been initiated to collect the same should be undertaken.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor instructs the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to request the write-off of receivables with no certainty of collection, except for those recorded under Receivable-Disallowances/Charges account pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005. Submission of all proofs that efforts have been initiated to collect the same should be undertaken.
observations.reason: Request for write-off are being prepared for these receivables. The Municipality will send notices and demand letters to payees before the request for write-off.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to retrieve and evaluate all the documents pertaining to the unpaid payables, determine the legitimacy of the transactions and if found to be valid claims, communicate with the concerned payees for the payment/ settlement of the accounts. Otherwise, revert to the Unappropriated Surplus of the General Fund those that proved to be with no legal claims.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer will retrieve and evaluate all the documents pertaining to the unpaid payables, determine the legitimacy of the transactions and if found to be valid claims, communicate with the concerned payees for the payment/ settlement of the accounts. Appropriate actions will be taken.
observations.reason: The unexpended balances in Due to Other NGAs and Other Payables account under the General and Trust Fund books are being reviewed and appropriate actions will be taken. Documents are being retrieved and coordinated with payees/source agencies for settlement of accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the concerned municipal officials to cause the refund of P48,400.00 expense incurred in CY 2015 but not included among the accounts payable and paid in CY 2016.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor instructs the concerned municipal officials to cause the refund of P48,400.00 expense incurred in CY 2015 but not included among the accounts payable and paid in CY 2016. The concerned officials are yet to resubmit the documents to the Audit Team since the concerned officials were already deceased.
observations.reason: The concerned municipal officials are yet to resubmit the documents to the Audit Team since the concerned officials were already deceased.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated that the Municipal Mayor:
a. Hire or appoint a separate General Service Officer (GSO) who will take custody of and be accountable for all properties of the Municipality; and
b. Require the Municipal Accountant and the Inventory Committee to reconcile their records and reports and adopt the procedural guidelines provided under COA Circular No. 2020-006 for the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The Management admitted that they have limited knowledge on the procedures of COA Circular No. 2020-006 for the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances. Also, they will update the composition of their Inventory Committee.
observations.reason: Due to budgetary constraints, hiring of GSO was not prioritized.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. Instruct the newly created inventory committee to adopt the procedural guideline in the conduct of inventory for one-time cleansing within three months from receipt thereof; 
b. Conduct an investigation to determine the last known location/ office of non-existing/ missing PPE, persons accountable or the circumstances of loss and the persons responsible for such loss. Demand the presentation of the PPE accountable personnel to produce the item or pay the depreciated replacement cost thereof; and
c. Adopt the procedures stipulated in Paragraph 8 of COA Circular No. 2020-006 for the derecognition in the books of accounts of the non-existing and missing PPE.
observations.managementAction: The Inventory Committee already started adopting the procedural guidelines in the conduct of the One-Time Cleansing of its PPE accounts. Investigation was made and the persons liable for the missing/unaccounted PPEs were determined. Demand the accountable personnel to produce the item or pay the depreciated replacement cost thereof; and The Inventory Committee is still preparing the request and compiling the supporting documents for submission.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Inventory Committee to expedite the completion of the one-time cleansing activity, in compliance with COA Circular No. 2020-006. Henceforth, adopt the guidelines and procedures on the recognition of properties found at the station and disposition of the non-existing and/or missing PPEs as prescribed by the cited circular.
observations.managementAction: The conduct of inventory taking as part of the one-time cleansing activities take longer than expected due to lack of manpower and other activities handled by the Inventory Committee members. Moving forward, they are planning to amend the composition of the Inventory Committee to resolve this concern. Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 01, Part II, Page Nos. 45 - 48.
observations.reason: Ongoing one-time cleansing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: a. Municipal Assessor to declare for tax assessment purposes those lots without Tax Declarations and to provide the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal General Services Officer (MGSO) the complete list and necessary information and documents pertaining to the parcels of land owned by the Municipality. Henceforth, municipal accountant to take up necessary accounting entries to recognize unrecorded lots after facilitating reconciliation of records; b. Inventory Committee to coordinate with the Municipal Assessor’s Office for the conduct of physical count of land to determine the actual location and status of the lots; c. Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain Real Property Ledger Card for each lot which shall include a complete description of the lot as to area, title number, tax declaration number, Property Identification Number (PIN), location and other applicable data; and d. MGSO to maintain Property Cards for each parcel of land owned by the Municipality containing details such as land area, title number, tax declaration number, PIN, location, actual utilization of such lot and specific improvements or buildings erected in the lot and other applicable data of each lot.
observations.managementAction: The concerned offices will verify their respective records for land to come up with reconciled reports. Property records were reconciled and some were recognized in the books at cost or fair value where applicable. The Municipal Assessor, Property and Accounting offices are on process of reconciling their records on land. The Municipal Assessor explained that their office is in the process of preparing property cards for each land owned by the Municipality. The one-time cleansing is underway.
observations.reason: Additional manpower is needed to modify the records and do the reconciliation; the one-time cleansing is still ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: a. Municipal Accountant, in coordination with all concerned Municipal officials, to prioritize the determination and verification of the composition of the long outstanding asset and payable accounts by tracing back and retrieving all possible information, documents and records pertinent thereto; b. concerned Municipal officials to prepare the request for the write-off/adjustments of the receivable accounts and the reversion of the long outstanding payables, with authority from Sangguniang Bayan, both showing that the accounts have been dormant for a long time and duly supported with complete documentation. Include applicable certifications and proof of the actions and the extent of validation undertaken thereon; and c.
