Municipal Administrator to submit to the Audit Team the results of validation of the dormant asset and payable accounts and the actions taken thereon duly supported with relevant and sufficient documents.
observations.managementAction: The management has commented that they will verify the long outstanding accounts with the concerned officials and retrieve the necessary documents, if still available. Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 02, Part II, Page Nos. 48 - 50.
observations.reason: There was no request for write-off. The municipal office is in the process of verifying long outstanding payables and will send request for reversion to the Sangguniang Bayan.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Human Resources and Management Officer to: a. Trace back and determine the validity of the long outstanding amounts under the Due to GSIS, Due to PAG-IBIG and Due to PhilHealth accounts which were existing since CY 2001; and b. Make representation with the inter-agencies, perform reconciliation of records, and adjust accordingly any discrepancies noted for the eventual cleansing of the long outstanding amounts. We also recommended strict compliance with RA Nos. 8291, 7875 and 9679 on remittance/payment of withheld contributions and/or loan payment to the GSIS, PAG-IBIG and PhilHealth, respectively.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant stated that verification of the dormant balances under the Due to GSIS account is in process and some of the amounts thereof had been paid which was supported by Disbursement Vouchers. Likewise, she informed the Audit Team that after verifying the accounts and found out that there were no longer valid obligations, they will request authority for reversion from the Sangguniang Bayan.
observations.reason: Verification with the agencies is still in process; some amounts have been paid and others require representation with the agencies.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC-Municipal Engineer to: a. submit a written justification pertaining to the deficiencies noted in the contract implementation of the Supply of Labor and Materials for the Construction of 100 Units of Residential Houses for the PAGCOR Project; and b. require full completion of the project or demand payment of P2,438,840.62 from the contractor, for the cost of items that were uninstalled and not in accordance with the plans and specifications. Otherwise, concerned Municipal officials can be held liable for failing to take the necessary measures to ensure that the project is implemented in accordance with the stipulations of the contract agreement.
observations.managementAction: The OIC-Municipal Engineer provided a notice to the contractor requiring completion of the project or payment for deficiencies. The OIC-Municipal Engineer informed the Audit Team that the noted deficiencies had been communicated to the contractor in a letter dated May 17, 2023, aiming for explanations and ways to resolve the matter.
observations.reason: There was no response from the contractor; the matter requires further action.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our audit recommendations in prior years that the Municipal Mayor direct the: a. Members of the Inventory Committee to conduct the complete physical inventory of all the PPEs and investigation/disposition of those reported in lumpsum amounts; b. Inventory Committee to prepare and submit the RPCPPE to the Municipal Accountant to facilitate reconciliation of records and the Audit Team for verification. Failure to do so shall automatically cause the suspension of payment of salaries of the officials concerned until they shall have complied with the requirements of the Commission; c. Municipal Accountant and ICO-MGSO to prepare and maintain updated records specifically of the Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards (PPELCs) and Property Cards, respectively; and d. concerned offices to strictly comply with the provisions of Section 375 RA No. 7160 and COA Circular No. 2020-006.
observations.managementAction: The one-time cleansing of PPE accounts is in progress as at year-end. Inventory Reports for the General and Special Education Funds were submitted to the Municipal Accountant and to the Audit Team. The composition of the Inventory Committee was reorganized as prescribed under Administrative Order No. 2020-006. Planning and initial physical counts were conducted.
observations.reason: The one-time cleansing is ongoing and conduct of inventory taking is not yet completed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the ICO-MGSO to: a. account all the properties of the Municipality which were found unserviceable, obsolete and/or no longer needed, prepare the IIRUP and initiate immediate disposal proceedings to avoid further deterioration and/or unnecessary consumption of storage space; b. ICO-MGSO and the Municipal Accountant to update the property cards and ledger cards of the unserviceable properties worth P18,246,618.36 under their custody and facilitate reconciliation thereof; and c. concerned Municipal officials to strictly adhere to the provisions of Section 379 of RA No. 7160, paragraph 6.3.1(h) of COA Circular No. 2020-006 and paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1.
observations.managementAction: The ICO-MGSO prepared and submitted the IIRUP that was submitted with a cut-off date of December 31, 2023. The one-time cleansing of PPE accounts is in progress. Property ledger cards and property cards are being updated for all PPEs including the unserviceable properties.
observations.reason: The one-time cleansing of PPE accounts is not completed; disposal processing is ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer: a. exert all efforts to intensify the collection of all RPT, both current and delinquent taxes in order to generate more income to the advantage of the Municipality; and b. enforce the tax remedies provided under Book II of the Local Government Code for the collection of taxes due to the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: Unpaid RPT/SET was reduced to P39,585,485.51 in CY 2022. The imposition of tax remedies was beyond the control of the Municipality. The Municipal Treasurer distributed the Statement of Accounts to taxpayers. Coordination with the Provincial Office for further assistance was pursued.
observations.reason: Imposition of tax remedies and enforcement was beyond the LGU's control; coordination with Province ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the concerned officials and employees to: a. Refrain from granting subsequent cash advances to concerned officials and employees, especially at the close of the year unless the previous cash advances given to them had been liquidated. Ensure that all cash advances are fully settled at the end of the year; and b. Strictly comply with the rules and regulations on the granting and liquidation of cash advances pursuant to PD No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 97-002.
observations.managementAction: Settlement of cash advances in prior periods, subject of this observation was made. The management acknowledged and commits to comply with the audit recommendations in accordance with COA Circulars No. 97-002.
observations.reason: The Municipality still has unliquidated cash advances as at year-end.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor direct the MPDC to: a. assess the viability of the identified PPAs in the planning stage to ensure that only those projects which are procurement and implementation ready will be given budget allocation; and b. ensure the optimal and timely implementation of the projects to be funded of the 20% Development Fund to address the development needs of the residents and to reap the desirable socio-economic benefits at the earliest.
observations.managementAction: Management has yet to comply to this audit observation; some projects were implemented subsequently. The MPDC stated plans to implement remaining projects. The BAC commits to comply with recommendations for succeeding procurement.
observations.reason: Some projects remained unimplemented at the end of CY 2023; procurement readiness issues persisted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and her staff to: a. continuously coordinate with the Provincial Accountant and reconcile their records on receivables/payables and their corresponding payables/receivables in the books of the Province to establish the correct balances of the intra-agency accounts; and b. coordinate with the concerned officials and departments heads to obtain information on those long outstanding accounts. Secure the necessary documents to properly identify the creditor/local government unit to whom it’s due. Once identified, verify reported balances in the subsidiary ledgers through confirmation letters and exhaust efforts to fully ascertain the validity and legality of those obligations. As to the financial assistance from the Province secure copies of agreement to determine provisions on the unexpended balance. Otherwise coordinate with the Province for proper guidance on those long outstanding balances.
observations.managementAction: Continuing coordination with the Province was undertaken; however, reconciliation remained outstanding. The municipal accountant is following up with the provincial office and will reconcile upon receipt of counterpart records.
observations.reason: Pending coordination and confirmation from the Provincial office; no full reconciliation yet.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Sangguniang Bayan and the Local Chief Executive terminate/rescind the Deed of Assignment entered into by PAPISSS Inc., Prestonsburg Development Corporation and Philippine Primark Properties, Inc. and strictly observe the proper guidelines on lease of government property in order to correct the present practice of the Municipality allowing lessees to assign the rights over the leased property due to financial losses or for whatever reasons to private entities of their own choice to the detriment of the interests of the Municipal Government, stallholders and the public as a whole. We also recommended that the Municipality impose the corresponding past due penalties and damages to PAPISSS Inc. for its failure to perform its obligations and to immediately rescind the contract and the Deed of Assignment and submit the certified computation of the lease based on DPWH guidelines in order to determine whether the amount of P551,000.00 offered by PAPISSS Inc. was reasonable and advantageous to the Government.
observations.managementAction: The Local Chief Executive informed the Head, Operations and Leasing Department of the Philippine Primark Properties Inc. that the Contract of Lease in favor of PAPISSS Inc. and Prestonsburg Development Corp. and the Deeds of Assignment in favor of Primark be cancelled and rescinded. In such case, the Municipality of Lemery will take over the operation and management of the entire public market of the Municipality. However, due to practical difficulties the Municipal Government also considered options for market operations and possible lease/management alternatives.
observations.reason: Terminating/rescinding and re-acquiring operations involve complex legal and operational matters; further steps pending and alternatives being considered.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Engineer and other concerned officials impose the liquidated damages on the contractor as prescribed by Section 8 Annex E of the RIRR of RA No. 9184.
observations.managementAction: The contractor provided explanations and remedial actions; the Municipal Engineer claimed delays were due to agency causes and thus did not impose liquidated damages. The municipality is considering remedies.
observations.reason: No liquidated damages were imposed because the Municipal Engineer attributed delays to agency-related causes; further review ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the concerned Municipal Officials to exert intensive effort to adopt effective marketing strategies to encourage possible stallholders or investors to rent or lease available space in the satellite market in order to increase the occupancy rate therein. Likewise, we recommended that the Municipality duly consider the instructive guidelines on the implementing procedures for existing local economic enterprise provided in the Manual on Setting Up and Operation of Local Economic Enterprises for Local Government Units 2016. We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer strictly enforce the full collection of the market right fees, execute a contract of lease agreement with the stallholders of the satellite market synchronized with the market codes in order to protect the rights of the Municipal Government of Lemery and impose corresponding penalty for non-payment of arrears. Furthermore, the Office of the Economic Enterprise shall exert effort to improve the occupancy rate in the satellite market and institute corrective actions on the noted inconsistencies above.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality implemented marketing and leasing initiatives. The Municipality also considered lease options and par tial operations; some efforts were made to improve occupancy. Monitoring and enforcement activities were undertaken.
observations.reason: Market remained suspended; closure of nearby highway affected operations; alternative measures being considered.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to evaluate the validity of the long outstanding payables and seek authority from the Sanggunian Bayan for the reversion of the long outstanding payables to Unappropriated Surplus in pursuant to Section 122 of the GAAM and Sections 4.9 and 6.7 of COA Circular No. 94-013.
observations.managementAction: A request for reversion of the accounts for approval of the Local Chief Executive was prepared in a letter dated May 8, 2024.
observations.reason: Waiting for Sanggunian Bayan resolution approving reversion of long outstanding payables.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant set-up and maintain Special Accounts in the General Fund for the monitoring of operating performance and viability of the Municipality’s economic enterprises and 20% DF, and to serve as a decision-making tool of the agency. Also, record all transactions pertaining to these economic enterprises to the corresponding subsidiary ledgers.
observations.managementAction: A special account for the Local Economic Enterprises was not made since the public markets of the Municipality are leased and operated by private companies.
observations.reason: The public market operations are managed by private entities; LGU did not set up special accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Assessor to cause the necessary action to secure the corresponding Certificate of Titles from the Land Registration Authority/Register of Deeds. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to cause the submission of all the lacking supporting documents to allow the purchase of lot and to strictly comply with the requirements on the procurement of land as mandated in Section 13 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 and pertinent provision of PD No. 1529. Ensure that all claims against government funds be supported with all the required documentations. Refrain from paying disbursement vouchers with incomplete supporting documents.
observations.managementAction: Certificates of Titles from the Land Registration Authority/Register of Deeds for most land in the name of the Municipality were not yet secured. The Municipal Assessor and Accounting Office are in the process of verifying the supporting documents and complying with procurement requirements for land acquisitions.
observations.reason: Certificates of Title not yet obtained; verification and submission of required supporting documents ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Sangguniang Bayan and the Local Chief Executive terminate/rescind the Deed of Assignment and the contract and observe proper guidelines on lease of government property; impose damages and penalties to PAPISSS Inc.; and submit certified computation of the lease based on DPWH guidelines.
observations.managementAction: The Local Chief Executive informed the Head, Operations and Leasing Department that the Contract of Lease in favor of PAPISSS Inc. and Prestonsburg Development Corp. and the Deeds of Assignment in favor of Primark be cancelled and rescinded. However, terminating the agreements and taking over the market involves complex legal and operational considerations; alternatives such as re-structuring operations and renegotiation were also considered.
observations.reason: Termination/rescission and transfer of market operations entail complex legal/operational matters; actions pending.

observations.managementAction: a. Municipal Accountant and the Acting General Services Officer (MGSO) to reconcile their respective records and reports and consider the inclusion of the PPE items’ specifications for proper identification; b. Municipal Accountant to provide depreciation for PPE costing P14,748,921.45 for a fairly presented balance in the financial statements and submit to the COA Audit Team the Journal Entry Voucher for the adjustments made; c. Inventory Committee to adopt the procedural guidelines under COA Circular No. 2020-006 for the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances; d. Municipal Administrator to submit to the COA Audit Team the Action Plan of Management for the implementation of this audit recommendations.
observations.reason: The Municipality is still in the process of doing the One-Time Cleansing of PPE.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Human Resources and Management Officer (HRMO) to validate the negative amounts and long outstanding balances under the Due to BIR and Due to GSIS accounts, respectively. If necessary, make representation with the inter-agencies, perform reconciliation of records, adjust accordingly any discrepancies noted for the eventual closing of the outstanding liability, and submit to the COA Audit Team the actions taken thereon. Henceforth, strictly comply with the aforesaid BIR and GSIS regulations on the remittance of taxes withheld, contributions and loan payments.
observations.managementAction: The Office of the Municipal Treasurer and HRMO staff visited the GSIS office several times to verify the discrepancies and to establish accurate information. They will make the necessary reconciliation and representation with inter-agencies and submit the results to COA Audit Team.
observations.reason: Lack of time and manpower to focus on the task; outstanding balances are still being checked to establish the correct amount to be remitted to the concerned agencies.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to evaluate the technical viability of the development projects during the planning stage to ensure that only those projects that are procurement and implementation ready will be funded by the 20% DF.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator committed to evaluate technical viability. Many projects funded by the 20% DF were completed; moving forward only readily implementable projects will be listed as chargeable from the 20% DF.
observations.reason: Some of the projects were realigned or delayed due to road right of way problems, late national-funded projects, late submission of required documents from contractors; implementation is ongoing and several projects were later completed.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Inventory Committee and General Services Officer (GSO) to conduct the yearly inventory taking of all PPEs, prepare the corresponding report and submit to the COA within the prescribed period; and
b. GSO, Inventory Committee and Municipal Accountant to adopt the guidelines and procedures provided in the COA Circular No. 2020-006 for the implementation of the one-time cleansing of PPEs.
observations.managementAction:
The GSO informed that the Municipality hired three job order employees to handle inventory taking and initially gathered, collated and validated all pertinent PPE records to serve as data to be reported in RPCPPE. He also informed that they are still in the process of complying in Item No. 5.1 of COA Circular No. 2020-006 and will create an Inventory Committee.

In addition, the Municipal Accountant assured that they will review the PPE schedules and accounting records will be ready and available once the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts is implemented.
observations.reason: Not Submission of the RPCPPE to COA

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Development Council, headed by the Municipal Mayor to assess the feasibility of the implementation and responsiveness to the current needs of the constituents of those unimplemented projects totaling P9,849,464.61 and include development projects that are well-planned and procurement-and-implementation-ready pursuant to DBM, DOF and DILG JMC No. 2020-01.
observations.managementAction: The management assured the Audit Team that they will comply with the audit recommendation in succeeding years.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. MDRRMC to evaluate the feasibility of planned PPAs, especially those held under Continuing Appropriations, and identify projects which will no longer be implemented for possible reversion and reprogramming to other disaster-related PPAs; and
b. MDRRMC to include in the LDRRMFIP the planned PPAs to be funded by the total unexpended balance of prior years’ LDRRMF in the TF book.
observations.managementAction:
The MDRRMO and Municipal Budget Officer provided the status as of May 15, 2024 of the seven projects mentioned above funded out Continuing Appropriations, as follows:

Projects Status
Preparedness/Mitigation Coconet at San Andres, Bagong Pook, and San Gregorio - Completed - P1,000,000.00
Improvement/Construction of Operation Center - For implementation in CY2024 - P103,923.55
Purchase/Installation and Rehabilitation of CCTV Camera- For implementation in CY2024 - P89,795.00
Construction of Line Canal at Brgy. Bilucao - For re-evaluation/reprogram - P500,000.00
Construction of Line Canal at Brgy. Luta Sur - Completed - P875,000.00
Installation of CCTVCameras - For implementation in CY2024 - P162,370.00
Medical Equipment - For implementation in CY2024 - P3,574,525.00

The management also assured the Audit Team that they will comply with the audit recommendations in succeeding years.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor to require the GFPS to prepare the GAD Agenda pursuant to PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01.
observations.managementAction: The GAD Focal Point Person noted that there will be a training on GAD-Related Laws, Gender Mainstreaming and Analysis and GAD Agenda Formulation on June 3-4, 2024.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and members of the Municipal Development Council (MDC) to assess the feasibility of the implementation of the remaining unimplemented projects and ensure that the development projects to be funded out of the 20 percent DF are well-planned and procurement-and-implementation-ready pursuant to DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1, s. 2020.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor shall ensure that all the unimplemented projects be implemented immediately. The MDC shall ensure that all the subsequent development projects are fully implemented as of year-end. (Attached herewith is the SAAOB for the CY 2022 20% Development Fund as of September 30, 2024)

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer (MDRRMO) to:
a. judiciously plan on how to maximize the utilization of the LDRRMF especially those under the Mitigation Fund (MF) - Capital Outlay (CO); and
b. reevaluate programs included under the MF - CO in order to achieve the goal/target of the Municipality regarding disaster relief, recovery, and risk reduction and management.
observations.managementAction: The MDRRMO shall evaluate the feasibility of the planned PPAs, including those in the Continuing Appropriations, and identify the projects which are no longer for implementation. The MDRRMC has commenced with the reversion and evaluation of planned PPAs. However, the reversion and reprogramming are yet to be implemented. The MDRRMC shall ensure that the reversion and reprogramming be implemented within the CY2024.

observations.recommendation: After which, require the immediate reconciliation of the GSO report versus the Accounting records to facilitate the review of the noted variances and effect the necessary adjustments.
observations.managementAction:
The GSO and the Municipal Accountant shall proceed with the preliminary activities in connection with the One-Time Cleansing of PPEs. The GSO has started verifying the existence of the PPE records found in the records of the Accounting, and preparing reports relative to the items which are no longer in physical existence.

Continue with the ongoing preliminary activities to arrive at the reconciled balances of PPE.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant, the GSO and the Municipal Treasurer to ensure coordination towards the full implementation of the perpetual inventory method in recording and reporting all procured goods, supplies and materials for distribution and consumption pursuant to GAM for LGUs.
observations.managementAction: The GSO shall exert effort in the implementation of the perpetual inventory method in recording and reporting all procured goods, supplies and materials for distribution and consumption. At year-end, the GSO will submit to the accounting office an inventory of all remaining goods, supplies and materials for distribution and consumption in order for the Accounting Office to record adjustments on the ending inventory.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. Require the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and other concerned officials to evaluate and cause the implementation of the project funded by PAGCOR upon completion of the Site Development in order to deliver the expected benefits to intended beneficiaries;
b. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to conduct confirmation among identified source agencies to confirm the balances due to them and facilitate the return of the remaining unutilized/unused fund. Otherwise, if there is still a need, the Municipality may make a written representation to the source agencies to request the use of money for other projects or purpose of the Municipality; and
c. Require the Municipal Accountant to ensure that all the subsequent unused balance of funds received shall be returned immediately after the completion of the project pursuant to COA Circular No. 94-013 and to disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements those unutilized balances for which the Municipality has valid reasons for non-remittance for fair presentation.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator continuously coordinate with PAGCOR and other concerned national agencies such as DHSUD and PAGCOR to implement the project which could only be done after the site development. The Municipal Accountant to return to the Bureau of the Treasury the undisbursed/unexpended fund transfer pursuant to Section 28, Chapter 4, Book VI of E.O. No. 292, s. 1987. Also to confirm with source agencies for the return of unused funds.
observations.reason: Waiting for PAGCOR Communication with PAGCOR. Fund remains unutilized. No action plan was provided by the concerned offices.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the lapsing schedule for all of its properties and reconcile it with the recorded amounts in the books of accounts to ensure the correctness of the reported balances of the property, plant and equipment in the financial statements. Also, maintain the subsidiary records for property, plant and equipment or the Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Card (PPELC) required in Section 114 of Chapter 7 of the New Government Accounting System Manual for Local Government Units. We further recommended that the General Services Officer also maintain the subsidiary records for property, plant and equipment or the property cards prescribed in the accounting manual and reconcile the physical count report with the accounting records and investigate and clear found discrepancies, if any. Likewise, we recommended that the General Services Officer check thoroughly the recorded properties under Special Education Fund and those donated items and include in the RPCPPE to reconcile with the Accounting Records.
observations.managementAction: The GSO is still on the process of checking properties under the Special Education Fund. The GSO maintains subsidiary documents but not yet fully reconciled with accounting records. The Accounting Office is to submit lapsing schedule and PPE Ledger Cards to the audit team. Have those records reconciled with the GSO and document the results to be the bases of adjustments, if any.
observations.reason: Reconcilia- tion of PPEs is still ongoing. There are lacking documents to support unidentified beginning balances. Property cards are not available for properties acquired in prior years.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to return to the Bureau of the Treasury the undisbursed/unexpended fund transfer pursuant to Section 28, Chapter 4, Book VI of E.O. No. 292, s. 1987. Also to ensure proper accounting of fund transfers and observe the Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 9184 and Local Budget Circulars.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer to return to Bureau of the Treasury the undisbursed/unexpended fund transfer pursuant to Section 28, Chapter 4, Book VI of E.O. No. 292, s. 1987. The Municipal Accountant to reconcile the municipal records with province’s books of accounts to establish the correct balances of reciprocal accounts.
observations.reason: No supporting documents for the return of unused fund balance. No request for write off received. The Municipal Treasurer has not yet returned the balance.

observations.recommendation: Based on the foregoing observations, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the following: The General Services Officer conduct annually the physical count of all Property, Plant and Equipment and reconcile RPCPPE with the accounting records to fully identify the discrepancies noted. Complete all the fields required in the prescribed Reports on Physical Count and the necessary remarks. With regard to disposal, complete the necessary documents and perform the subsequent procedures in accordance with the Circular. Likewise, ensure that all PPEs are fully supported with all the PPE Cards by the GSO; The Accounting Department submit copies of PPE Ledger Cards and Lapsing Schedule of PPE to the audit team for CY 2021 onwards. Have those records reconciled with the GSO and document the results to be the bases of adjustments, if any. Likewise, ensure that the Subsidiary Ledgers/Lapsing Schedule of PPE tally with the balances in the Financial Statement. Lastly, review the breakdown of the items per PPE account and ensure that there is consistent application of accounting policies in recording PPEs. If there is reclassification needed, prepare adjusting journal entry and have it supported with the necessary documentation; and The Municipal Engineer assist the General Services Office and the Municipal Accounting Office in the conduct of the physical count and reconciliation of PPEs particularly to their Infrastructure Assets and Buildings and Other Structures accounts. Ensure that all PPEs in those groups of accounts are accounted and verified.
observations.managementAction: The Inventory Committee headed by the General Services Office to conduct the physical count of Road Networks in accordance with COA Circular No. 2015-008 due to complexity of the matter. The Accounting Department conduct reconciliation with the GSO. The Municipal Engineer assist in the conduct of the physical count and reconciliation of PPEs particularly to their Infrastructure Assets and Buildings and Other Structures accounts.
observations.reason: Reconcilia- tion is still ongoing. Inventory of local road network accounts are underway; RPCLRN not yet done.

observations.recommendation: Based on the foregoing, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the following: The Inventory Committee headed by the General Services Office conduct the physical count of its Local Road Network and prepare the RPCLRN. Make use of the template provided in Annex C of COA Circular No. 2015-008 or Appendix 63 of the Government Accounting Manual for LGUs and complete the necessary information in every field. Submit the accomplished RPCLRN to the Accounting Office for reconciliation in the books;
observations.managementAction: The GSO to conduct the physical count of Road Networks and prepare the RPCLRN using the COA template Report. Submit RPCLRN to the Accounting Office for reconciliation in the books.
observations.reason: Inventory of Local Road Network accounts are underway. RPCLRN was not yet done and complexity of the matter.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a. Require the Municipal Accountant and the Inventory Committee to coordinate with the Accounting Office and reconcile their records and reports and adopt the procedural guidelines provided under COA Circular No. 2020-006 for the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances of the Municipality; and b. Require the Appraisal and Disposal Committee to facilitate the disposal of the unserviceable properties with acquisition cost of P4,174,768.83 to prevent further deterioration of the asset and decongest the storage area, in accordance with existing rules and regulations and in compliance with Section 79 of PD No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: The Inventory Committee will coordinate with the Accounting Office for reconciliation of records and eventually come up with the process of how to avail of the One-time cleansing of PPE balances. Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 1, Part II, Page Nos. 40-42 of this Report. Disposal of unserviceable properties were not prioritized by the Disposal Committee.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a. Require the Municipal Assessor to declare for tax assessment purposes the noted lots and provide the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal General Services Office complete and necessary documentary information presented; b. The Municipal Accountant to take up necessary accounting entries to recognize unrecorded lots after the conduct of comprehensive reconciliation of lands owned by the Municipality; c. The Municipal Accountant to maintain a record of each land by using the Real Property Ledger Card, which shall include a complete description of the lot as to area, title number, tax declaration number, PIN, location and other applicable data; d. The Municipal General Services Officer (GSO) to maintain Property Cards for each land owned by the Municipality containing details such as land area, title number, tax declaration number, PIN, location, actual utilization of such lot, specific improvements or buildings erected in the lot and other applicable data of each lot; e. The MGSO to coordinate with the Municipal Assessor’s Office for the conduct of physical count of land to determine the actual location of the lot; and f. The Municipal Accountant and MGSO to follow the guidelines and procedures in the conduct of physical count of land, recognition of land items found at station and disposition of non-existing/missing land items, for the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020 for the One-Time Cleansing of PPE account balances.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Assessor declared for tax assessment purposes just for tax purposes the land owned by the municipality as per title/deed of sale/donation presented. The Municipal Accountant recognized in the books all unrecorded lots. The Municipal Accountant recognized in the books all unrecorded lots. Property Cards are yet to be maintained or prepared by the GSO. Real Property Ledger Cards are yet to be maintained or prepared by the Municipal Accountant.
observations.reason: Property Cards are yet to be maintained or prepared by the GSO. Real Property Ledger Cards are yet to be maintained or prepared by the Municipal Accountant.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the: a. Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal Accountant at the beginning of every year a certified list of unpaid taxes supported with details containing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year to establish a reliable balances of RPT and SET Receivables; b. Municipal Accountant to set-up receivables relative to RPT and SET at the beginning of the year, in compliance with NGAS Manual; and c. Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to perform periodic reconciliation of balances based on collections and proper postings to the General Ledger to warrant the accuracy and reliability of presented RPT and SET Receivables.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer is yet to furnish a certified list of unpaid taxes and to furnish them at the beginning of every year a certified list of unpaid taxes to establish a reliable balances of RPT and SET Receivables. The Municipal Accountant and Treasurer shall perform periodic reconciliation of balances based on collections and proper postings to the General Ledger to warrant the reliability of presented RPT and SET Receivables.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to: a. Undertake a thorough review and analysis of the details of the payable accounts that remained outstanding for more than two years and submit the result of analysis to the Audit Team; b. Prepare the corresponding Journal Entry Voucher, after seeking authority from the Sangguniang Bayan (SB) to revert to unappropriated surplus all APs that remained outstanding for two years and more, duly supported by the list of APs reverted; and c. For all APs that were reverted to unappropriated surplus, determine first the existence, validity and legality of claims before making the payments.
observations.managementAction: Letters to the creditors to validate their claims with the LGU are yet to be prepared. Pending the results of validation, unvalidated claims shall be reverted to the unappropriated surplus upon approval of the Sangguniang Bayan. For all APs that were reverted to unappropriated surplus, determine first the existence, validity and legality of claims before making the payments.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Inventory Committee headed by the GSO to continue the conduct of physical count of PPE in order to determine the existence of the recorded PPE accounts and reconcile the results of the count with the accounting records and establish the causes of discrepancies, whether items have already been missing, donated, sold and unserviceable to arrive at the correct balance in the financial statements. As to the infrastructure assets, coordinate with the Municipal Engineer to verify their existence, prepare and maintain the required Property Cards and Acknowledgment Receipt of Equipment (ARE) to facilitate the reconciliation of RPCPPE with the accounting records; b. the Municipal Accountant in coordination with the GSO to clearly identify the items under Replacement Costing and Labor Payroll, amounting to P81,384,996.97 as to what specific PPE items or accounts they were attributed; and c. the GSO, in coordination with the Municipal Accountant to consider the remedy provided in COA Circular No. 2020-06, dated January 31, 2020 to assist in coming up with the reliable PPE balances that are verifiable as to existence, condition and accountability.
observations.managementAction: The inventory Committee headed by the GSO to continue the conduct of physical count of PPE in order to determine the existence of the recorded PPE accounts and reconcile the results of the count with the accounting records and establish the causes of discrepancies, whether items have already been missing, donated, sold and unserviceable to arrive at the correct balance in the financial statements. Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 1, Part II, Pages No. 40-42 of this Report. Not implemented: Replacement Costing and Labor Payroll is yet to be performed by the Municipal Accountant and GSO.
observations.reason: Replacement Costing and Labor Payroll is yet to be performed by the Municipal Accountant and GSO.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the: a. GSO to (i) prepare the stock cards for the purchased supplies; (ii) conduct periodic inventory of supplies; (iii) prepare the corresponding RPCI and submit the same to the Accounting Office for reconciliation with the reported inventory balances in the books; and b. Municipal Accountant to (i) record the purchases under the proper Inventory account as Asset instead of as Expenses; and (ii) maintain supplies ledger cards for all inventory accounts to support the recorded balance of inventory.
observations.managementAction: The GSO will implement the preparation and maintenance of Stock Cards. The Municipal Accountant is yet to perform proper recording of purchases and maintenance of supplies ledger cards for all inventory accounts.
observations.reason: Proper recording of purchases and maintenance of inventory records deters the implementation of recommended actions by the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MPDC, in coordination with the GAD Focal Point, to: a. spearhead the establishment of the GAD Database, manual or electronic, consisting of sex-disaggregated data and other GAD-related information; b. ensure the effective implementation of the database that contains systematically gathered and regularly updated information relevant to gender-responsive planning, programming, and policy formulation; and c. evaluate thoroughly the data gathered from the database to come up with gender-responsive and gender-sensitive plans and programs which are in accordance with the GAD mandate.
observations.managementAction: In prior year, the Municipality commented that the plan to include GAD through CBMS remained unimplemented as of CY 2023. The MPDC assures to comply and to spearhead the establishment of the GAD Database. Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 15, Part II, Pages No. 69-70 of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the concerned department heads to immediately initiate action to enforce the settlement of the disallowances in accordance with the COA Revised Rules of Procedure on the Settlement of Accounts. Secure documents such as the Notice of Finality of Decision/COA order of Execution to trace back those disallowances reflected in the Receivables - Disallowances/ Charges.
observations.managementAction: Settlement of the said disallowances are not yet addressed by liable persons.
observations.reason: Said disallowances were not yet addressed by liable persons.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to expedite the submission of the request for write-off of these dormant accounts to the Commission on Audit which should be supported with Sangguniang Bayan Resolution requesting the write-off of the dormant accounts, certification as to extent of validation made by them on these accounts and reasons why the records could not be located, aging schedule and available records pertaining to these dormant accounts, if there is any.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor together with the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer are yet to expedite the submission of the request for write-off of these dormant accounts supported with necessary documents for verification. The Municipal Mayor commits to comply with the submission.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to enforce collection/settlement of the disallowances, where liable persons other than the direct recipient of the paid claims should be required to affect the settlement of audit disallowances and/or recorded shortages of former employees of the Municipal Government. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant be directed to record the audit disallowances of P4,117,000.00 as Receivables–Disallowances/Charges, provide subsidiary records such as the reference ND and the liable persons and include disclosures in the Notes to Financial Statements regarding the audit disallowances of P3,654,662.51 that is subject of a recently-submitted request for reconsideration to COA RO IV, Quezon City, and cause the corresponding settlement of all the audit issues contained in the Credit Notices resulting from the audit of cash advance liquidations.
observations.managementAction: Settlement of said disallowances are not yet addressed by liable persons. The Municipal Accountant is yet to record the receivables and provide subsidiary records.
observations.reason: Said disallowances were not yet addressed by liable persons. Not all supporting documents were made available to record receivables.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the concerned department heads to immediately initiate action to enforce the settlement of the disallowances in accordance with COA Revised Rules of Procedure on the Settlement of Accounts and secure supporting documents.
observations.managementAction: Settlement of the said disallowances are not yet addressed by liable persons.
observations.reason: Said disallowances were not yet addressed by liable persons.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to expedite the submission of the request for write-off of these dormant accounts to the Commission on Audit supported with Sangguniang Bayan Resolution and necessary certification, aging schedule and available records.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor together with the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer are yet to expedite the submission of the request for write-off of the dormant accounts supported with necessary documents for verification.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to enforce collection/settlement of the disallowances and to record the audit disallowances of P4,117,000.00 as Receivables–Disallowances/Charges with subsidiary records and disclosures in the Notes to Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: Settlement of said disallowances are not yet addressed by liable persons. Municipal Accountant is yet to record receivables.

observations.recommendation: We recommended continuation of physical counts, reconciliation, identification of discrepancies, preparation of AREs, and application of remedies under COA Circular No. 2020-06.
observations.managementAction: Inventory Committee to continue physical count and reconcile records; Replacement costing and labor payroll yet to be completed by Municipal Accountant and GSO.

observations.recommendation: We recommended GSO prepare stock cards, conduct periodic inventor ies, prepare RPCI and submit to Accounting; Municipal Accountant to record purchases to Inventory and maintain supplies ledger cards.
observations.managementAction: GSO to implement preparation and maintenance of stock cards; Municipal Accountant yet to fully implement proper recording and maintenance of supplies ledger.
observations.reason: Lack of resources/implementation pending.

observations.recommendation: We recommended MPDC, in coordination with GAD Focal Point, to establish GAD Database (CBMS), ensure implementation and evaluate data for gender-responsive plans.
observations.managementAction: MPDC assures compliance and to spearhead CBMS/GAD database formulation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended immediate enforcement of settlement and securing of documents to support collection or write-off.
observations.managementAction: Settlement not yet addressed by liable persons; Municipal offices to pursue collection and documentation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: a. General Services Section Chief to conduct the required annual physical inventory to determine the status or condition of all the PPEs reported in the Inventory Report or adopt the one-time cleansing of PPE to ensure that recorded PPEs were existing and rid the books of unaccounted properties; and b. Municipal Accountant to reclassify the PPE transferred from the Trust Fund.
observations.managementAction: The General Services staff had reconciled with the accounting office based on records and coordinated with all offices during the actual physical count. The Municipal Accountant reclassified the PPE and furnished the Audit Team copy of JEV No. 100-23-02-066-0337 dated February 28, 2023.
observations.reason: The Municipality has not yet adopted one-time cleansing of PPE.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MENRO to strictly implement the ordinances on solid waste management and impose the appropriate fines and penalties in violation thereof.
observations.managementAction: The Management will propose to the Sangguniang Bayan to include the said fines in the amendment of the Revenue Code of the Municipality. A citation ticket was prepared based on the prevailing Environmental Code of San Pascual and was submitted to the Municipal Treasurer for approval of the Sangguniang Bayan.
observations.reason: The delay on the implementation was due to non-inclusion of the said fines on the Revenue Code of the Municipality.

observations.managementAction: Reconciliation is still ongoing. After conduct of complete physical count, reconcile inventory results with property and accounting records; The Property and Accounting units to perform reconciliation particularly on the noted difference of P5,232,843.77 and make the necessary adjustments/recording of entries, if any; The Inventory Committee to ensure that all counted items are properly specified and identified detailing physical attributes and location; The Municipal Accountant to disclose in the Notes to the Financial Statements the results of reconciliation of RPCPPE and accounting records as well as the amount of PPE items, if any, without available supporting documents; The existing Inventory Committee are studying the provisions of COA Circular 2020-006.
observations.reason: Reconciliation is not completed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the balances of the RPT and SET receivables and their reciprocal accounts with the total Tax Delinquency reported, particularly at the end of the year and thereafter, make the necessary adjustments to their respective records.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Report of Delinquencies in RPT to the Municipal Accountant for reference in making the necessary adjustments in the records. Thereafter, makes the necessary adjustments to their respective records.
observations.reason: Reconciliation is not completed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the: a. Municipal Health Officer to obtain from the donors the necessary information as to the unit cost and quantities of the COVID-19 vaccines or any other medicines acquired by the Municipality in the future. Likewise, render a monthly report to the Municipal Accountant on the receipt, issuance and balances of the vaccines and inventories received from outside source; and b. Municipal Accountant in coordination with the MHO, to determine and recognize in the books the full cost of the ending inventory of vaccines and supplies as at year-end and henceforth record future receipts and issuances of the same.
observations.managementAction: Will coordinate with the Provincial Health Office and City Health Office and the MHO. The ending inventory reports to be submitted by the MHO.
observations.reason: The consumed vaccines were donations and the costing/price information was not available; inventory balances from MHO were not submitted as proof of recording.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a. create a separate Inventory Committee for one-time cleansing of PPE as required under COA Circular No. 2020-006 and approve the PIP necessary for the conduct and complete physical inventory; b. direct the Inventory Committee to assign Property Number (PN) for each PPE items and incorporate in the report such PN; d. instruct the duly created Inventory Committee, the Municipal General Services Office and Accounting Office to reconcile inventory count per RPCPPE with property and accounting records; g. create Administrative Disposal and Appraisal Committee that will (i) inspect unserviceable property and property; (ii) set the final appraised value of all the disposable properties; (iii) recommend the disposal to the Local Chief Executive for his approval and the manner of disposal; and (iv) forward the approved documents to the Municipal Accountant for the necessary adjustments in the PPE or for derecognition of the unserviceable properties totaling P48,903,194.19 in the books for a more reliable balances of PPE accounts in the financial statements. We further recommended that the Municipality follow the guidelines set forth in COA Circular No. 2020-006 for the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts.
observations.managementAction: A separate Inventory Plan was prepared. The Property Inventory, Appraisal and Disposal Committee in the Municipality of Talisay, Batangas created the Committee per Administrative Order No. 08 Series of 2022. The Municipal PRAISE has already been approved by the CSC last June 1, 2023. The Municipal Accountant disclosed in the Notes to Financial Statements the results of audit reconciliation pertaining to CY 2022 PPE. The Municipality is studying implementing one-time cleansing.
observations.reason: One-time cleansing is not yet adopted; reconciliation is ongoing; physical inventory is ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor submit a written justification for non-filling of application for disposal/divestment of Talisay Public Market as well as that of other unserviceable property plant and equipment to the Commission. Further recommended that Municipal Accountant to submit the JEVs and their supporting documents which was used as basis for drawing a JEVs for the derecognition for the said property plant and equipment.
observations.managementAction: Submitted JEVs and supporting documents; Inventory of PPE as of December 31, 2019 had been submitted. Waste Materials Report (WMR) and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) were not among those submitted as basis to derecognize PPE.
observations.reason: WMR and IIRUP not submitted; supporting documents lacking.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the General Services Officer reconcile the physical count report with the accounting records and investigate and clear found discrepancies, if any, and render the adjustment in the process of preparing and updating its property cards. The Municipal Accountant shall provide depreciation to all properties of the Municipality as provided in IPSAS 17 and further emphasized in Philippine Application Guidance 3 and 4, issued by Public Sector Accounting Standards Board (PSASB), to fairly present the affected accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The General Services Officer – Designate had identified that the discrepancies noted between the RPCPPE and the Financial Statements were due to the following: 1.) beginning balances of prior year’s acquisitions and other improvements; 2.) unreconciled, unlocated PPEs; 3.) PPEs that will be reclassified; 4.) Undervalued recording of acquisition cost; and 5.) Unsubmitted RPCPPE of Slaughterhouse account. She further commented that incomplete information on the RPCPPE was due to on-going update of records and the recent establishment of the General Services Office. The committee that will be responsible for the one-time cleansing is still to be created since the personnel that will be assigned will be temporarily relieved of their responsibility.
observations.reason: The Report on Physical Count of Local Road Network was still not reconciled as of December 31, 2023.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality adhere to the guidelines for implementation provided in Section C.1 (5) of Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 and that the Municipality ensure GAD PPAs implemented are those planned in the GAD Plan and Budget and that GAD Focal Person and GAD TWG attend to planning, budgeting and attribution of GAD programs, projects and activities.
observations.managementAction: Municipal GAD Focal Person had provided that some of the PPAs were implemented but have incurred minimal to no expenditures thus the funds for GAD were unutilized. In addition, some of the PPAs have few participants, thus the Municipality had opted not to utilize the fund or change the actual implementation of in a particular GAD activity. For the additional recipients of school supplies which were not included in the submitted GAD Plan and Budget, they had provided the Audit Team with a supplemental GAD Plan and Budget. Similarly, they assured the Audit Team that in the succeeding year, the Municipality will arrive into a more achievable and implementable GAD Plan and Budget.
observations.reason: GAD Focal Person is still to attend seminars and trainings for proper planning, budgeting and attribution of GAD programs, projects and activities.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality fully implement the approved Municipal Solid Waste Management Plan covering the period CY 2017 to 2027 in compliance with declared policy of the State to adopt a systematic, comprehensive and ecological solid waste management program knowing that the LGUs are primarily responsible for the implementation and enforcement of the provisions of the Ecological Solid Waste Management Act.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality was continuously implementing the approved Municipal Solid Waste Management Plan particularly among its barangays and the activation of the materials recovery facility. The Municipality entered into a joint venture agreement with private entity for the disposal of its waste.
observations.reason: Construction of sanitary landfill which was included in the Solid Waste Management Plan will no longer be implemented by the Municipality and had entered into joint venture with private entity for the disposal of its waste.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality fully implement the approved Municipal Solid Waste Management Plan covering the period CY 2017 to 2027 and provide status reports of the scheduled solid waste management activities.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality was continuously implementing the approved Municipal Solid Waste Management Plan particularly among its barangays and the activation of the materials recovery facility. The Municipality entered into a joint venture agreement with private entity for the disposal of its waste.
observations.reason: The Municipality opted to dispose garbage cases and to private entity which was declared possible the Agency will update once the Agency allows the collection and fuel consumption of garbage hauling.

observations.recommendation: The GSO – designate to conduct the physical count of its Local Road Network and prepare the Report on the Physical Count of the Local Road Network (RPCLRN). Make use of the template provided in Annex C of COA Circular No. 2015-008 and complete the necessary information in every field and submit the accomplished RPCLRN to the Accounting Office for reconciliation with the books. Also prepare the Road Network Property Card for each Local Road Network; and The Municipal Engineer to provide cost segregation of every road networks owned by the Municipality per road component for the prior and succeeding years and provide to the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal GSO – Designate. Likewise, provide technical assistance to the inventory committee in the conduct of physical count of its Local Road Networks; and The Municipal Accountant to maintain the necessary Road Network Ledger Cards and have it reconciled with the balance in the Financial Statements. As to the identified Road Network projects, coordinate with the Municipal Engineer to provide individual cost per road component to be the basis in the computation of depreciation expenses.
observations.managementAction:
The Acting Municipal Engineer provided assistance to the inventory committee in the conduct of physical count of buildings and infrastructure assets; however, no report was submitted to the Audit Team.
The department claimed that they are having difficulty finding records of the road projects implemented from 2017 and below.
observations.reason: The concerned department claimed that they are having difficulty finding records of the road projects implemented from 2017 and below.

observations.recommendation: Based on the foregoing, we recommended the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to immediately submit the lacking documents listed in the Annex for further evaluation of the transactions.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant have partially submitted the lacking documents for six projects on April 18, 2023 and eight projects on April 28, 2023. Lacking documents of the five remaining projects are yet to be submitted.
observations.reason: Lacking documents of the five remaining projects are yet to be submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to immediately submit the disbursement vouchers, their supporting documents and liquidation reports and documents of the noted cash advances.
observations.managementAction: Their supporting documents and amounting to P145,500.00 were submitted to the previous Audit Team. The Municipal Accountant is yet to submit the remaining documents for full compliance.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant is yet to submit the remaining documents for full compliance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor require: - BAC to submit the following: approved Annual Procurement Plan or any supplemental or revision thereof inclusive of all purchases made in response to COVID-19 pandemic; BAC Resolution for the justification of alternative mode of procurement and recommendation for the award of the contract, proof of posting of Request for Quotation, Notice of Award and Purchase Order, ITR for CY 2019, original copy of OSS and Mayor’s Permit or Business Permit for CY 2020 of BSMA Trading for the purchase of medicines; and justification for inconsistencies in the posting of Notice for Negotiated Procurement in the PhilGEPS, Contract Agreement and distribution of the relief goods purchased from L and H Marketing under Check No. 1758900.
observations.managementAction: Several disbursement documents were noted. Some supporting documents were submitted to the previous Audit Team. Pending full compliance to the recommendation.
observations.reason: Pending full compliance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Engineer to compute and claim from the contractor the liquidated damages incurred for the three (3) projects. Otherwise, such amount of damages not deducted from the payment to the contractor shall be disallowed in audit. We recommended the Municipal Accountant, as part of the BAC-TWG agreed to submit the noted documents to avoid suspension in audit.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant, as part of the BAC-TWG agreed to submit the noted documents to avoid suspension in audit. Transmittal letters showing the submission of lacking documents to the previous and current Audit Team were not provided by the Municipality.
observations.reason: Transmittal letters showing the submission of lacking documents to the previous and current Audit Team were not provided by the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor to cause the immediate submission of the noted lacking documents. Otherwise, transactions shall be suspended in audit.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor caused the submission of the noted lacking documents. Otherwise, transactions shall be suspended in audit.
observations.reason: Transmittal letters showing the submission of lacking documents to the previous and current Audit Team were not provided.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer, Treasurer and Accountant to cause the refund of the total amount of P296,900.00. Otherwise, said transactions shall be disallowed in audit.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor required the Municipal Engineer, Treasurer and Accountant to cause the refund of the total amount of P296,900.00.
observations.reason: Copies of Official Receipt pertaining to the refund of wages to laborers were not submitted to the audit team as of December 31, 2023.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor to require the concerned contractors to rectify immediately the deficiencies noted to conform with the approved program of works. Otherwise, forfeit the Performance Security/Warranty Security and consider blacklisting of the concerned contractors who refuses to effect the necessary corrections and submit documents to support the action taken.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor required the concerned contractors to rectify immediately the deficiencies noted to conform with the approved program of works. Otherwise, forfeit the Performance Security/Warranty Security and consider blacklisting of the concerned contractors who refuses to effect the necessary corrections and submit documents to support the action taken.
observations.reason: Copies of documents relative to the rectifications made by the contractor on the noted defects were not provided to the previous and current Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: a. Municipal Engineer to provide the pre-repair inspection report and a written and valid justification on the propriety and necessity of the repair, subject to evaluation, otherwise the said transaction shall be disallowed in audit; b. Municipal Engineer to demand from the contractor the detailed costing of the repair using the prescribed computation for procurement of goods/services and refund the resulting cost difference plus liquidated damages for the noted delay; and c. Municipal Engineer to submit for evaluation the approved plan for the excavation itemizing the site, dimensions, scope of work, and others lacking details noted and the Bids and Awards Committee for the invitation request to other contractors, otherwise this is suspended in audit.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer was required to provide the pre-repair inspection report and a written and valid justification on the propriety and necessity of the repair; to demand from the contractor the detailed costing of the repair using the prescribed computation for procurement of goods/services and refund the resulting cost difference plus liquidated damages for the noted delay; and to submit for evaluation the approved plan for the excavation itemizing the site, dimensions, scope of work, and others lacking details noted and the Bids and Awards Committee for the invitation request to other contractors.
observations.reason: No pre-repair inspection report submitted. Detailed costing of the repair must be prepared by the contractor. Pending full compliance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: a. Municipal Accountant and the concerned officials/employees to submit the liquidation reports and documents of P114,600.00 cash advances, Itinerary of Travel and Reimbursement Expense Receipt (RER) for van hire totalling P121,743.10. Otherwise, said transactions shall be suspended in audit. b. concerned officials/employees to refund the excess travel expenses and registration fee.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant and concerned officials/employees were required to submit the liquidation reports and documents. Out of P95,700.00 a total of P76,980.00 and P9,280.00 had been refunded last CY 2021 and CY 2022, respectively. Hence, a total settlement of P86,260.00 was made, leaving a balance of P9,440.00 as of CY 2022. Full settlement was not made in CY 2023.
observations.reason: Full settlement was not made in CY 2023.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Engineer to:

a. Prepare the plan for the whole project with a clear delineation of work for each
phase showing marks, signs, or color codes to indicate the phases of the
project, , and the corresponding estimated costs of the project’s total cost by
phase up to its completion as a technical reference for future repair/renovation
and/or rehabilitation of the structure;

b. Fast track the completion of the project to be operational/usable according to
their intended purpose; and

c. In case of construction by phases for future projects, ensure that there is a clear
delineation of work for each phase up to its completion, which must be usable
or readily serve its purpose, and structurally sound pursuant to Item 4, Annex A
of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Assessor and City Accountant
to submit lacking documentary requirements to support the payment of the purchased
real properties and observe the timely submission of contracts as prescribed under
COA Circular Nos. 2009-001 and 2012-001 and the IRR of RA No. 10752 for further
evaluation of the Audit Team.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Engineer to:

a. Facilitate the installation of the uninstalled items totaling P1,041,395.65 once all
the necessary works are completed; and henceforth,

b. Strictly comply with the plans and technical specifications provided in the
contract agreement.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the:

a. CPDO to submit within 60 days from receipt thereof, the City’s plan for the
utilization and implementation of the unutilized balance of 20% DF; and

b. CPDO and City Budget Officer to utilize the savings from appropriation of
completed project.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the LDRRM Officer to:

a. Judiciously plan on how to maximize the utilization of the LDRRM Fund and to
resolve the issues hounding implementation of PPAs in order to achieve the
goal/target of the City regarding disaster relief, recovery, and risk reduction and
management; and

b. Ensure that all available fund for the LDRRM has a corresponding PPAs that will
be included in the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund
Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP).

Based on the foregoing, we recommended that the City Mayor to create an Inventory
Committee for the One-Time Cleansing and shall be required to perform the following:
 plan/strategize on how the City would apply the One-Time Cleansing and
incorporate it in the PIP to be submitted to the City Mayor for his approval to ensure
that the conduct of inventory is properly and adequately planned;
 submit a copy of the approved PIP to COA Audit Team at least 10 calendar days
before the scheduled inventory-taking activities; and
 immediately start the application of One-Time Cleansing of the PPEs in accordance
with the approved PIP.

In view thereof, we recommended that the City Mayor require the OIC-City Accountant
to make the necessary adjusting entries relative to the misstatement of P60,585,439.25
pertaining to the carrying value of semi-expendable items that were still recorded as
PPE in order to reflect fairly the said accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the OIC-City Accountant to:
a. recognize the appropriate asset, revenue, and expense accounts in the Trust Fund
books upon receipt and utilization of funds from other agencies arising from non-
exchange transactions based on the Revised Chart of Accounts; and
b. cause the preparation and submission of the SFPer of the Trust Fund along with
the other financial statements of the City of Calaca.

Pursuant to IPSAS 24 and COA Circular No. 2016-004, we recommended that the City
Mayor instruct the OIC- City Accountant to:
a. prepare the Reconciliation between the Actual Amounts as presented in the SCBAA
and the SFPer as additional disclosures in the NTFS to clarify the discrepancies
between the two reports, and submit the same to the Audit Team within 15 days
from receipt hereof; and
b. for the succeeding year, prepare the NTFS using the prescribed Pro-forma NTFS.
Ensure that each item on the face of the statement of financial position, statement of
financial performance, statement of changes in net assets/equity and cash flow
statement shall be cross-referenced to any related information in the notes.

Hence, we recommended that the City Mayor require the OIC-City Accountant and GSO
to:
a. cause the disposal of the unserviceable properties and to derecognize the latter
from the book of accounts; and
b. if pending disposal, disclose in the NTFS the carrying amount of PPE retired from
active use and held for disposal.

Hence, we recommended that the City Mayor require the CPDO to:
a. ensure the implementation of the identified priority projects in the continuing
appropriations from previous years in conformance with the guidelines set in DILG-
DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No.2017-01 dated February 22, 2017; and
b. propose and facilitate the re-appropriation of savings from projects completed in
prior years to other development projects.

Therefore, we recommended that the City Mayor require the:
a. requesting Unit, OIC-City Accountant and Budget Officer to review the obligated
purchase request and prepare necessary adjustment on the RAAO;
b. requesting Unit, to ensure that the purchase orders or contracts are duly forwarded
to the City Budget Office to make timely recording of the Obligation and updates on
their database;
c. Budget Officer, to record only items as obligated in RAAO when they are already
supported by a contract or purchase order; and
d. OIC-City Accountant, to ensure that the actual amount to be recorded in the SCBAA
are obligations as defined by the budget operations manual.

Foregoing considered, we recommended that the City Mayor require the City Engineer to:
a. provide a backup computation of the projects’ ABC for re-evaluation to justify the
cost. Otherwise, the excess amount shall be disallowed in audit; and
b. ensure that the quantities are reasonably estimated to avoid excessive
expenditures in order to conform with COA Circular No. 2012-003.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Engineer and the OIC-City
Accountant to:
a. impose the liquidated damages of P2,751,319.01 as computed and reported by TAD
on the Inspection Report; and
b. continuously impose the liquidated damages until the project is completed as
provided in the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.
We also recommended that the City Engineer cause the expeditious completion of the
project.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the GFPS TWG to strictly comply with
the provisions on the preparation of the GAD Agenda to ensure that the PPAs indicated
in the annual GPB are aligned with it or with the identified gender issues to be
addressed during the three-year term of the LCE’s leadership.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the GFPS to:
a. review the GPB thoroughly, submit any changes therein to the DILG-Regional Office
and strictly monitor the actual expenditures to ensure that the City has enough
funds for the implementation of all its GAD activities; and
b. plan thoroughly the activities of the City in attaining women’s empowerment and
addressing gender equality, focusing more on priority projects while taking into
consideration the limited resources of the City with respect to funds, time, and
manpower.

Hence, we recommended that the City Mayor require the:
a. City Budget Officer to appropriate funds for property insurance of the City subject
to its financial capacity; and
b. City GSO to submit the consolidated Property Inventory Form listing of all the
insurable properties and other assets to GSIS for their evaluation and approval,
copy furnish the Office of the Auditor, in compliance to Section 5 of RA No. 656 and
COA Circular 2018-002.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the OIC-City Accountant to:
a. identify the items that could have caused the variances between the amount
withheld and remitted to the concerned agencies;
b. draw Journal Entry Voucher to make necessary adjusting entries; and
c. in case of identified unremitted contributions, immediately remit the amount to the
concerned agency.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the CGSO, City Accountant and the members of the Inventory Committee to follow the procedural guidelines on the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances prescribed under COA Circular No. 2020-006 such as (1) Preliminary activities prior to the conduct of inventory taking; (2) Inventory taking; (3) Reconciliation of inventory count per RPCPPE with property and accounting records; (4) Disposition procedures for non-existing/missing PPEs; (5) Procedures in the derecognition of non-existing/missing PPEs without available record of accountability; and (6) Accounting entries and disclosure in the Notes to Financial Statements.
