observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to exert further efforts to communicate with GSIS and PAG-IBIG to confirm the balances and/or to check with the records whether all premiums/loan repayments/contributions deducted from the payroll of employees and Government Share (GS) for all employees were remitted in order to identify the affected employees on the unremitted balance, if any. As to BIR, exhaust all available means to secure documents and journals to trace back those old balances and identify the specific taxpayers. Otherwise, if the mentioned agencies were able to confirm that they have no outstanding claims from the Municipality, request for the reversion of such payables following the applicable guidelines.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality will try to communicate with the agencies for proper reconciliation and if proved to have no outstanding balance, they will request for the reversion of these payables.
observations.reason: Some records/documents are hard to find; some balances relate to employees no longer in service; efforts to communicate with agencies ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Engineer to be more efficient with the IRR of the Revised RA No. 9184. Submit justification on the delays incurred in the construction of the Municipal Cemetery, and if warranted, collect liquidated damages. Clarify the inconsistencies with the documents submitted and the delay in the construction. The Municipal Accountant to submit all the documentary requirements on the procurement of infrastructure projects in order to validate the propriety of the transactions. Likewise, we recommended strict compliance with all the provisions of the implementing rules and regulations of the Government Procurement Act.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer said that the delay in the construction of Municipal Cemetery is because of weather condition and soil strata. He will provide supporting documents after it was completed.
observations.reason: Delay due to weather conditions and soil strata; supporting documents to be provided after completion.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and concerned officers to:
a. Effect the imposition of surcharges to defaulting lessees as provided in the lease contract. However, revisit the provision of the contract in terms of the specific sanctions to be imposed against defaulting stallholders in order to enforce collection of monthly rentals and utilities; and 
b. Create ordinances to help promote the market which can draw support from people and community. Also consider amnesty relative to the delinquent stall holders as provided in the lease contract.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality effected the imposition of surcharges to defaulting lessees as provided in the lease contract. The Sangguniang Bayan requested from the Market Supervisor the list of delinquent stall holders and enacted Municipal Ordinance No. 369, series of 2023 dated December 28, 2023 providing amnesty to all delinquent stallholders.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Municipal Assessor to retrieve required information and documents necessary for the titling and refer the matter to the concerned agencies to facilitate the processing of title; 
b. Municipal Budget Officer to request the Sangguniang Bayan to appropriate funds for the titling of land owned by the Municipality; and
c. Municipal Accountant to take up necessary accounting entries to recognize unrecorded lots after the conduct of comprehensive reconciliation of lands owned by the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The Inventory Committee already traced the PPEs for recognition. Investigation was made and the persons responsible for the missing items were determined. The Municipal Assessor coordinated with the concerned agencies for the processing of the required documents and the Municipal Budget Officer indicated additional funds for titling were appropriated. The Accounting Office is still awaiting for the final determination of the status of the land for adjustment.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Municipal Accountant and Acting Municipal Treasurer to analyze their records and determine the cause of the deficiencies noted between their records, prepared reconciliation and make the necessary adjustments, and submit to the Audit Team the results and the actions taken thereon within 60 days upon receipt hereof; and
b. Municipal Accountant to conduct necessary procedures to secure/produce all relevant and applicable documents under Item 8 of COA Circular No. 2016-005 to support the filing or request for authority to write-off of noted dormant receivable accounts from the Commission on Audit.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant submitted the necessary course of actions for the noted fund transfers and reconciliations. Request for write-off was already submitted by the Agency. The concerned departments are regularly reconciling their respective records and prepare the necessary adjustments, and submit the results to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to observe the proper journal entries for recording of receipts and disbursements under the Trust Fund and prepare the necessary Statement of Financial Performance (SFP) under the Trust Fund starting CY 2023 for proper monitoring of the balances of the accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant has started preparing the SFP under the Trust Fund for CY 2023 for proper monitoring of the accounts.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and Municipal Treasurer:
a. Invest the excess/idle cash of the General Fund in time deposit with the authorized government depository bank for a specified period to earn higher interest and generate additional income; and
b. Seek an authority from Sangguniang Bayan before placing the excess/idle funds in time deposits.
observations.managementAction: The agency utilized the excess/idle supplemental budget in CY 2023. The Municipality invested the excess/idle cash thru time deposits during the year and sought required authorities as necessary.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to inform the concerned suppliers/contractors for the bidders’ bond and warranty deposit within 30 days from receipt thereof and require them to prepare the letter request for the refund of the amount due them which claim shall be supported with the certified photocopy of the disbursement voucher showing such amount was deducted. In case the concerned suppliers/contractors were not anymore existing or no response was received from them, prepare Journal Entry Vouchers, after seeking the authority from the Sangguniang Bayan, for the possible reversion to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund and effect the necessary adjustment in the Municipality’s financial statement by debiting the account Guaranty Deposit Payable and crediting the account Miscellaneous Income or Prior Period Adjustment.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality started sending letters to the concerned suppliers/contractors and requested the necessary documents. The Municipality prepared adjustments on the books after the receipt of replies. Some refunds were processed; other balances awaited responses or Sangguniang Bayan action.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
a. BAC and Municipal Accountant to cause the submission of the noted lacking documents for evaluation by the Audit Team;
b. BAC to strictly comply with the provisions of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 and use the 6th Edition of the Philippine Bidding Documents (PBD) to establish the regularity of the procurement process and efficient contract implementation; and
c. Municipal Accountant to ensure that disbursements are properly supported with complete supporting documents pursuant to Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 2012-001. Refrain from processing payment for claims that are not sufficiently documented.
observations.managementAction: The lacking documents were already submitted to the Audit Team. The BAC has been using the updated PBD. The Municipal Accounting Office has been strictly checking the supporting documents prior to payments and refrains from processing claims not sufficiently documented.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
I.a. Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer to formulate plans and programs in accordance with COA Circular No. 2012-002 and NDRRMC JMC No. 2013-01; and
I.b. Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant to charge only eligible items against the LDRRM Fund following JMC No. 2014-01 of the NDRRMC, DILG, DBM and CSC;
II.a. LDRRMO and Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to strive for the timely and full implementation of LDRRM related PPAs to address the goals and objectives of the fund; and
II.b. Municipal Mayor to accelerate implementation within the year as planned.
observations.managementAction: LDRRM Investment Plan was prepared as prescribed by Circulars. The LDRRMO has been exerting efforts to improve utilization and submitted justifications on non-implementation. Some purchases were charged correctly under eligible items. Updated LDRRM Investment Plan including unutilized CY 2023 balances was submitted to COA.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Municipal Mayor and members of the Municipal Development Council to:
a. assess the feasibility of the identified PPAs in the planning stage to ensure that only those projects which are procurement and implementation ready will be given budget allocation; and
b. ensure the optimal and timely utilization of 20% DF in succeeding periods by review, reevaluation and reassessment of its planned PPAs.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality made substantial utilization of its 20% DF in CY 2023; some projects were completed while others remain on-going or unimplemented due to various reasons. The LDC, MEO and MPDC to conduct further assessments.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor create an Inventory Committee and adopt the procedural guideline provided in COA Circular No. 2020-006 to complete the conduct of physical count of all Property, Plant and Equipment for the One-Time Cleansing of PPE account balances.
observations.managementAction: The Inventory Committee already started adopting in the CY 2023 the One-Time Cleansing of its PPE accounts. Reiterated under Observation No. 1. The Inventory Committee had already traced the PPEs for recognition. Investigation was made and the persons liable for the missing/ unaccounted PPEs.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Municipal Mayor and members of the Municipal Development Council to:
a. assess the feasibility of the identified PPAs in the planning stage to ensure that only those projects which are procurement and implementation ready will be given budget allocation; and
b. ensure optimal and timely utilization of 20% DF in succeeding periods by including development projects that are well-planned and procurement-and-implementation-ready pursuant to DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 2020-01.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality made substantial utilization of its 20% DF in CY 2023. Some projects were implemented while others were reversioned, on-going or unimplemented. LDC, MEO, MPDC to undertake further actions.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council to include PPAs in the LDRRMFIP that can strengthen the capacity of the Municipality to build a disaster resilient community and enhance disaster preparedness and response and to accelerate their implementation within the year as planned.
observations.managementAction: The updated LDRRM Investment Plan including the unutilized CY 2023 balances was submitted. The LDRRMO provided justifications on non-implementation and exerting efforts to improve utilization. Some PPAs implemented; others pending due to procurement, donor, or technical issues.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the BAC and its Secretariat to submit the copies of perfected Contracts/Purchase Orders (POs) and their supporting documents within five (5) working days after execution/issuance thereof pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-001.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality submitted the copies of perfected Contracts/POs and supporting documents within the deadline set last CY 2023.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and the designated GSO to ensure coordination towards the full implementation of the perpetual inventory method in recording and reporting of all procured supplies and materials and relief goods for distribution and consumption pursuant to GAM for LGUs.
observations.managementAction: Purchases of supplies and materials were procured and the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued was prepared. The Inventory account was established and entries were adjusted accordingly.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor create an Inventory Committee and GSO to prioritize the disposal of unserviceable properties in accordance with COA Circular No. 92-386 and to adopt the procedures for the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances of the Municipality pursuant to COA Circular No. 2020-006.
observations.managementAction: The Inventory Committee started the One-Time Cleansing and disposal of unserviceable properties were made in CY 2023.

The Municipal Engineer has been requested to provide the complete description and cost segregation of the road components of every road project undertaken; the designated GSO and Municipal Accountant are coordinating to secure these documents. Some information remains incomplete and is being awaited from the Municipal Engineer.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Designated Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer and designated GSO to prepare and submit the Summary/ List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances and the related documents, and furnish/provide the Audit Team of the one-time report required to be submitted to the NDRRMC, through the Office of the Civil Defense pursuant to COA Circular No. 2020-009.
observations.managementAction: The updated report was submitted to the NDRRMC and the Office of the Civil Defense. The Municipality prepared the Listing and records for donations received and distributed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Development Council headed by the Municipal Mayor ensure the optimal utilization of the 20% DF in CY 2023 through review, reevaluation and reassessment of its planned PPAs.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality made substantial utilization of its 20% DF in CY 2023. Some projects were implemented, some on-going, some reverted. Continued monitoring and assessment by LDC, MEO and MPDC are being undertaken.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: a. Municipal Treasurer to send written notice to the bank payees of the stale checks, notify them of the existence of such checks and require submission of the required documentation to facilitate the issuance of replacement checks; and b. Municipal Accountant to prepare the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to cancel the payment recorded for the stale checks amounting to P217,372.76, and restore the said amount to cash and appropriate liability accounts. Remove the stale checks from the list of outstanding checks in the bank reconciliation statements and furnish the Municipal Treasurer with a copy of the JEV as her basis for debiting the amount of stale checks in the respective cashbook.
observations.managementAction: JEV Nos. 200-2023-04-028 and 100-2023-04-094, both dated April 30, 2023 were prepared by the Municipal Accountant to restore the amounts of staled checks to the Cash in Bank and Accounts Payable accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to analyze their records and determine the cause of the deficiencies noted between their records, prepare reconciliation and make the necessary adjustments, and submit to the Audit Team the results and the actions taken thereon.
observations.managementAction: RPT/SET Receivables and Deferred RPT/SET Income are tally. The Municipal Treasurer distributed the Statement of Accounts to the taxpayers. The Municipal Accountant prepared reconciliations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal General Services Officer to immediately prepare the PIF in the prescribed format containing the complete information for all the insurable assets, properties and interests of the Local Government Unit and submit thereof to the GIF of the GSIS not later than April 30 every year to ensure sufficiency of insurance coverage and protection in case of unforeseen natural or man-made hazards. Furnish copy of the PIF and proof of submission to the GSIS and to the COA Audit Team for evaluation of the actions taken.
observations.managementAction: The General Services Officer recommended that she will do as recommended. As initial compliance with the recommendation, MGSO already submitted the PIF to the GSIS, a copy to be forwarded to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the members of the MPDC to: a. submit a concrete plan with time-bound undertakings on how the Municipality will make the satellite market productive and useful, henceforth stop it from being idle; and b. Municipal Planning and Development Council and Bids and Awards Committee to meticulously and judiciously plan all procurement, conduct a thorough feasibility study and technical viability on identified projects to ensure that these could be implemented in compliance with PD No. 1445 and Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.
observations.managementAction: The MPDC stated that they are planning to implement the remaining projects this CY 2023. Meanwhile, the Bids and Awards Committee commits to comply with the recommendations for succeeding procurement. The property is currently being considered for lease to interested private entities. One that has expressed intent is a proposed birthing facility.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer and the Public Employment Service Office (PESO) to consider: employ the use of an Automatic Teller Machine (ATM) based-payroll system for the payment of wages to Job Order employees so as to protect the funds of the LGU from possible risks and to enable more efficient and safer government transactions. In addition, re-adopt the use of a biometric machine which had been initially used in prior years with confirmed advantage to the LGU.
observations.managementAction: In a letter dated February 14, 2022, the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal HRMO informed that they had coordinated with Land Bank of the Philippines (LBP) Lemery branch relative to the mass application of ATM visa payroll for JO personnel. They also stated that the LGU is willing to re-adopt the use of biometric machines but lack of budget for procurement of software systems and computer units to avoid overloading of files. They plan to include the same in the supplemental budget.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the Sangguniang Bayan consider updating the daily wage rate taking into account the daily minimum wage rate in the private sector, and if funds are available, a daily rate that is comparable to the position in the government sector. We also recommended that the HRMO strengthen the system of internal control and selection process in recruiting JO personnel by (i) creating written policy and guidelines for hiring JO employees; (ii) requiring the applicants to submit valid proofs of identity and suitability for the job being applied for; and (iii) providing a Statement of Duties and Responsibilities to JO employees to clearly identify their responsibilities and perform their duties efficiently.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal HRMO informed that they will review the LGU’s financial capability in increasing the salary of personnel under JO employment. The HRMO showed an initial draft of the policy and guidelines to be used in hiring JO employees which he will present to the Municipal Mayor for approval.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MPDC and Local Development Council to: a. assess the viability of the identified PPAs in the planning stage to ensure that only those projects which are procurement and implementation ready will be given budget allocation; and b. ensure the optimal and timely implementation of the projects to be funded of the 20% Development Fund to address the development needs of the residents and to reap the desirable socio-economic benefits at the earliest pursuant to DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 2020-01.
observations.managementAction: Of the five unimplemented projects at the close of the year, three had been completed as of 2nd quarter of CY 2024. Management also explained that the purchase of lot which is the LGU’s counterpart for the Saboo Dams 1 and 2 projects is still ongoing but they commit to complete the process until full payment to the owner-seller is made.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office (MDRRMO) to cause the preparation and submission of the duly approved LDRRMF Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) using the prescribed format of COA Circular No. 2012-002, specifying in detail the list of PPAs to be funded from the current appropriation and prior year’s unexpended fund, copy furnished the COA. PPAs shall be fully assessed as to viability before incorporating them in the LDRRMFIP so as to ensure full implementation.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal officials submitted the new LDRRMIP plan to the audit team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC-Municipal Engineer to adopt in the future infrastructures projects of the Municipality, the design and format of project signboards and include the required information therein as provided under Section 2.2.3 and Annex A of the Circular and henceforth, strictly comply with COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013.
observations.managementAction: The implementation of the projects covered by this observation was prior to the designation of the OIC-Municipal Engineer. The concerned official said the mistake was not intentional and assures compliance with the audit recommendation for upcoming projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Bids and Awards Committee and the Municipal Engineer to require the winning contractors of the infrastructure projects to submit their DOLE-approved CSHP. Also require the Municipal Accountant to ensure that the disbursements to the contractors are supported by the DOLE-approved CSHP. Likewise, require all future contracts be supported with a DOLE-approved CSHP and that the BAC require the contractors to include in their program of works the cost of protective equipment to be provided to their laborers.
observations.managementAction: The OIC-Municipal Engineer commented that the contractors of the infrastructure projects were informed and advised to comply with this requirement. The contractors were requested to submit the DOLE-approved CSHP and succeeding contracts will contain the provision. The Municipal Engineer will ensure compliance for future projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the: a. Municipal Health Officer to secure Property Transfer Reports (PTRs) from the source agencies of donations, henceforth, provide the Municipal Accountant with the inventory reports, attach all Requisition Issue Slips and PTRs, of all donations in-kind received by the Municipality. For donations in-kind without available cost information, the Municipal Accountant to properly recognize the inventory using the fair values at the date of receipt, which may be ascertained by reference to an active market, or by appraisal, in accordance with Paragraph 97 of IPSAS 23; and b. Municipal Accountant to record in the books of the Municipality as inventory the donations in-kind with available cost information in accordance with Paragraph 96 of IPSAS 23.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Health Officer stated they are requesting from the source agencies the documents to determine the cost of donated vaccines. As of May 12, 2023, the remaining donated vaccines were 272 vials. The Municipal Accountant commented that the necessary adjusting entries will be made upon determination of the cost with relevant supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to reconcile their records to establish the accurate and reliable balances of the RPT/Deferred RPT Income and SET Receivable/Deferred SET Income reported in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: RPT/SET Receivables and Deferred RPT/SET Income were reconciled and reported. The Municipal Treasurer distributed the Statement of Accounts to the taxpayers. The RPT/SET balances are being reconciled.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and BAC Secretariat to ensure that subsequent procurement projects are supported with complete documentation and properly accomplished and signed documents in compliance with the existing laws, rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant attached a list of documentary requirements for each disbursement. The municipal procurement processes were adjusted and corrective actions instituted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MDRRMO to prepare the LDRRMF Investment Plan using the prescribed format of COA Circular No. 2012-002. Incorporate in the report the details of the PPAs to be implemented and funded from the unexpended balances of previous years.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Office submitted the LDRRMFIP and assessments of PPAs; the report was prepared and updated.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer send written notice to the payees for the existence of stale checks and require submission of documentation to facilitate replacement checks, and Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary journal entries to correct the accounts and restore amounts to cash and appropriate liability accounts.
observations.managementAction: A written notice was served to the payee and one stale check was addressed. Initial corrective journal entries were prepared.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer to: a. provide the change order and requesting the contractor to immediately correct the deficiencies noted in the TAS Inspection and Evaluation Report and request for another inspection upon compliance to validate completeness of the project per plans and specifications; otherwise demand payment from the contractor the amount of P262,342.89 with corresponding liquidated damages for the unfinished work and for the Office of the Municipal Engineer to complete the project accordingly.
observations.managementAction: The contractor provided corrective actions and requested re-inspection. Some corrective works were completed and re-inspection conducted. The monitoring by the Municipal Engineer confirmed corrections.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to request authority from the Sanggunian Bayan to revert the long outstanding payables to Unappropriated Surplus in pursuant to Section 122 of the GAAM and Sections 4.9 and 6.7 of COA Circular No. 94-013.
observations.managementAction: Unexpended balances from funds received from NGAs had been remitted/returned.

a. Municipal Accountant to establish the correct amount of the Cash in Bank of the LEE - Market with abnormal/negative balance of P8,915,735.34 as of December 31, 2022, and submit to the COA Audit Team the action taken thereon; b. Municipal Accountant to prepare the BRS for the six cash in bank accounts of the Municipality and submit to the COA Audit Team. For succeeding months, the Municipal Accountant to consistently cause the preparation and submission of the BRS for all the cash in bank accounts of the Municipality; c. Municipal Treasurer to prepare the complete lists of unreleased checks every year-end and submit all to the Municipal Accountant, in time for the closing of the books of account. Both the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer, to restore to the Cash in Bank and appropriate liability accounts, in their respective records, the amount corresponding to the unreleased checks at year-end; and d. Municipal Treasurer to prepare and keep an updated cashbook for the four bank accounts, considering the complete and timely recording of all cash transactions in the cashbooks, and submit to COA Audit Team all the lacking cashbooks for evaluation.
observations.reason: BRS for the accounts maintained under the Land Bank of the Philippines were still not submitted due to unavailability of bank statements. Cashbooks for PRDP transactions were completed and liability restored; the Municipality provided BRS for other bank accounts.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entries for prior year to capture in the books the effects of the understatement of assets, revenue and expenses, including any accumulated depreciation for the unrecorded PPEs, and submit the same to the COA Audit Team for evaluation; and
b. Recognize appropriate revenue/expense accounts in the TF books upon utilization of funds received from other agencies arising from non-exchange transactions, and cause the preparation and submission of the SFPer for the TF along with the other financial statements of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: a. Prepare necessary adjusting entries for prior years to capture effects of understatement including accumulated depreciation for unrecorded PPEs and submit to COA Audit Team; b. Recognize income and expenses in TF books upon utilization of funds received from other agencies and prepare and submit SFPer for the TF along with other financial statements.
observations.reason: The SFPer for the Trust Fund for the year ended December 31, 2023 was prepared thereby reflecting the Municipality’s affecting revenue and expense accounts.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Municipal Treasurer, at the beginning of the year, to prepare a detailed certified list of all taxpayers with the amount due and collectible for the year and an updated list of delinquent taxpayers with unpaid taxes due to the Municipality at the end of the year using the RPT Taxpayer’s Index Card in his custody and submit to the Municipal Accountant for recording and reconciliation of records. Furnish the Audit Team with copies of the duly certified lists; and
b. Municipal Accountant to verify the discrepancies between the RPT and SET Receivables as well as the SET Receivables and Deferred SET. Accordingly, make the necessary adjustments and submit to the Audit Team.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer prepared and submitted the certified list of all taxpayers and list of delinquent taxpayers together with a summary per barangay. The Municipal Accountant verified discrepancies and prepared necessary adjustments.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Acting MGSO and the Inventory Committee to:
a. Prepare a timetable for the immediate disposal of remaining unserviceable properties in the custody of the LGU to avoid further deterioration, wastage, loss, and unnecessary consumption of storage and submit a copy to the COA Audit Team; and
b. Determine the nature of the item in the amount of P100,000.00 that requires adjustments and exclude it in the IIRUP, if warranted. Exclude also the sold and demolished properties since these items no longer existed in the LGU’s premises to come up with a reliable list and amount of unserviceable properties to be disposed of.
We also recommended strict compliance with the provisions of RA No. 7160, the Manual on Supply and Property Management in the Local Governments and COA Circular No. 2020-006 relative to the disposal of unserviceable, obsolete and/or no longer needed PPEs.
observations.managementAction: The initial request for disposal of several unserviceable properties has been submitted, excluding the items amounting to P100,000.00 and those sold, donated and demolished properties that no longer exist in the LGU premises.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. Instruct the Acting MGSO and Inventory Committee to consider in the guidelines under succeeding PPE counts a detailed verification and identification of the Municipality’s properties and adopt COA Circular No. 2020-006; b. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to conduct reconciliation with MGSO and review the noted discrepancies between their records, make the necessary adjustments and provide disclosures in the notes to financial statements for variances which cannot be adjusted; and c. Create a Disposal and Appraisal Committee that will (i) inspect before disposal and set the final appraised value of all disposable properties; (ii) recommend the disposal to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) for his approval and the manner of disposal; and (iii) forward the approved documents to the Municipal Accountant for the necessary adjustments in the PPE.
observations.managementAction: The GSO and Inventory Committee adopted guidelines under COA Circular No. 2020-006 as lead office for inventory counts. Reconciliation and verification of the accounts that need to be adjusted started; the Disposal and Appraisal Committee was created and reactivated in CY 2021 for disposal of unserviceable equipment and heavy equipment.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to:
a. recognize income and expenses in the TF books upon utilization of funds received from other agencies or any other sources, whether private or government, that are considered as non-exchange transactions and record entries relevant to the transactions; and
b. prepare and submit for succeeding years the SFPer for the TF along with the other financial statements of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The Office has recognized income and expenses in TF books upon utilization of funds received from other agencies for the year 2023; and prepared and submitted the SFPer for the Trust Fund for the year ended December 31, 2023 along with the other financial statements of the Municipality.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
a. Municipal Accountant to consistently prepare and submit to the Audit Team the BRS of all Cash in Bank accounts of the Municipality; and
b. Municipal Accountant to verify the accuracy of the negative cash in bank balance pertaining to market operations of the Municipality and prepare the necessary correction entries, if any.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant prepared the BRS for the bank accounts of the Municipality; verification and adjustments were made for affected accounts except where hard copies of bank statements cannot be obtained (particularly Landbank of the Philippines).
observations.reason: BRS for some accounts (Landbank of the Philippines) still not submitted due to unavailability of bank statements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the concerned personnel to indicate in the PO the complete specifications of the items being requisitioned, including brand names and all other necessary information relative to the procurement of supplies/goods/property, to facilitate the inspection and the determination of the reasonableness of the prices and quality of the items procured and delivered.
observations.managementAction: The concerned personnel implemented the recommendation and now indicate complete specifications and other necessary information in the PO to facilitate inspection and determination of reasonableness of prices and quality.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to:
a. recognize income in the TF books upon utilization of funds received from other agencies or any other sources, whether private or government, that are considered as non-exchange transactions and record entries relevant to the transactions; and
b. prepare and submit for succeeding years the SFPer for the TF along with the other financial statements of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The SFPer at the close of CY 2023 reflecting the Municipality’s transactions affecting revenue and expense accounts has been submitted and the SFPer for the Trust Fund for the year ended December 31, 2023 was prepared.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Business Planning and Licensing Officer and the Municipal Treasurer Officer to verify, assess and validate the noted under-collection/payment thereof.
observations.managementAction: The LGU is using the Integrated Business Permit and Licensing System sponsored by the Department of Information and Communications Technology. The Municipal Treasurer and Business License Offices already submitted reply with detailed explanation on the under-collection of P25,307.30 to previous Audit Team.
observations.reason: System-generated permits and licensing transactions; previous audit team was furnished the details.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer prepare a certified list of all taxpayers with the amount due and collectible for the year and submit to the Municipal Accountant for recording and reconciliation of records; and that the Municipal Accountant verify discrepancies and make necessary adjustments.
observations.managementAction: The Office of the Municipal Treasurer submitted the certified list of taxpayers and the Municipal Accountant prepared the necessary adjustments.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant on the strict compliance of the previously cited auditing and accounting laws and rules and the immediate submission of the listed DVs to the auditor to be subjected to audit and to prevent the unnecessary issuance of audit suspension and/or disallowance.
observations.managementAction: The listed DVs were already submitted to the Audit Team. The Municipal Accountant submitted the DVs to the auditor.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to reclassify the remaining balance to appropriate account in the GF book and record the subsequent funds receive pertaining to the shares from lotto draws and STL, shares from fire safety inspection fees, and shares from the collection of fees for the cutting of coconut trees, to its appropriate income accounts in the GF pursuant to COA Circular No. 2015-009.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant assured that she will comply with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Municipal Accountant and General Services Officer to prepare the Local Road Network Ledger Card and Local Road Network Property Card, respectively, using the prescribed format under COA Circular No. 2015-008 for all recorded road networks of the Municipality; and
b. Municipal Accountant to stop the practice of providing residual value for the depreciable component of the road network systems pursuant to COA Circular No. 2015-008.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accountant adjusted the recorded road networks per Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) No. 101-2024-04-42047 dated April 22, 2024 and revised the Schedule of Road Networks to reflect the proper carrying value.

In addition, the Municipal Accountant and GSO assured that they will comply on the preparation of Local Road Network Ledger Card and Local Road Network Property Card for all the local road networks of the Municipality.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. record the subsequent reversion of staled checks and cancellation of checks to its appropriate accounts pursuant to COA Circular No. 2015-009; and
b. recognize in the books the taxes withheld simultaneously with the setting up of Accounts Payable for all goods received or services rendered, inspected and accepted pursuant to BIR RR No. 2-98, as amended by RR No. 12-2001.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accountant adjusted the noted audit observations per Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) No. 101-2024-04-42162 dated April 22, 2024 and JEV Nos. 101-2024-04-41878, 101-2024-04-41940, 101-2024-04-41935, 101-2024-04-41936, 101-2024-04-41937, 101-2024-04-41939, and 101-2024-04-41942, all dated April 8, 2024.

During the exit conference, the Municipal Accountant reasoned out that the taxes were not withheld for the noted one transaction due to late submission of documents to the Accounting Office by the concerned department. Nevertheless, taxes were withheld upon payment on February 6, 2024 and already remitted to the BIR in March 2024.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Municipal Treasurer to monitor the effectivity and expiration dates of fidelity bonds of all accountable officers and see to it that renewal thereof shall be made before or 30 days prior to the expiration as required under Treasury Circular No. 02-2019; and
b. Municipal Accountant to grant cash advances to Accountable Officers with valid fidelity bond only pursuant to Section 305 of RA No. 7160.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Treasurer assured that they will strictly monitor the expiration dates of fidelity bonds of all accountable officers and process the renewal before the expiration dates to avoid any problem.

In addition, the Municipal Accountant committed to ensure the existence of valid fidelity bond prior to the granting of cash advances.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to stop the practice of granting simultaneous and overlapping cash advances and require the Accountable Officer to liquidate immediately the first cash advances before granting another cash advances; and
b. Municipal Accountant to propose specific and achievable measures to resolve delays in the liquidation of cash advances, which include, among others, the regular sending of demand letters to Accountable Officers with a reminder that their salaries could be withheld should they still fail to liquidate after demand and non-entitlement to the Performance-Based Bonus.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Treasurer replied that the Municipality is in the process of entering into a Memorandum of Agreement between the servicing bank on the payroll system for all the volunteers, beneficiaries and personnel who received honorarium to avoid simultaneous and overlapping cash advances.

In addition, the Municipal Accountant replied that the matter was already coordinated with the Municipal Treasurer in prior years and recommended measures on how to prevent the overlapping of cash advances. Nevertheless, she assured the Audit Team that cash advances will be granted only upon liquidation of the previous cash advance.

Moreover, the Municipal Accountant stated that her Office had been consistently coordinating with the concerned officials and employees relative to the liquidation of cash advances within the prescribed period, however, still failed to liquidate on time due to various reasons. She also informed that written demand letter will be sent to concerned accountable officers to ensure that cash advances granted will be liquidated on time.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Budget Officer to prepare the RAAO per function/program/project, allotment class, current/continuing appropriations and fund and record the obligations based on the source documents, and subsequently prepare the SAAOB based on the prepared RAAO, pursuant to COA Circular No. 2006-002 and Section 18 of Volume II of the Manual on NGAS.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Budget Officer replied that she acknowledged the audit observation and assured that her Office was actively working to address the concerns and to strengthen the financial management practices. She also stated that the Supplemental Budget for the year 2023 was based on the unobligated balances determined by the Local Finance Committee and used as the source of appropriations.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Inventory Committee and General Services Officer (GSO) to conduct the yearly inventory taking of all PPEs, prepare the corresponding report and submit to the COA within the prescribed period; and
b. GSO, Inventory Committee and Municipal Accountant to adopt the guidelines and procedures provided in the COA Circular No. 2020-006 for the implementation of the one-time cleansing of PPEs.
observations.managementAction:
The GSO prepared and submitted the Physical Count of Equipment, Furnitures and Fixtures, and Other PPE, as well as the Report on the Physical Count of the Inventory (RPCI). After which, require the immediate reconciliation of the GSO report versus the Accounting records to facilitate the review of the noted variances and effect the necessary adjustments.

The GSO and the Municipal Accountant shall commence with the preliminary activities relative to the One-Time Cleansing of PPEs. The GSO has started verifying the existence of the PPE records found in the records of the Accounting, and preparing reports relative to the items which are no longer in physical existence.

Continue with the ongoing preliminary activities to arrive at the reconciled balances of PPE.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to recognize the revenue on RPT and SET at gross amounts, and record the discounts and penalties on such collections in the appropriate accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant shall recognize the revenue on RPT and SET at gross amounts and record the discounts and penalties in the appropriate accounts. (Attached are the copy of CY2024 ledgers for RPT, SET, Discounts on RPT, Discounts on SET, and the Fines and Penalties - Property Taxes)

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the BAC Secretariat to cause the submission of contracts and its supporting documents to the COA within five working days upon its perfection pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-001.
observations.managementAction: The BAC Secretariat shall submit the copy of the contracts and its supporting documents to the COA within five working days from the perfection thereof. The BAC Secretariat submitted the POs/contracts to the Audit Team within five days from the perfection thereof.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Inventory Committee:
a. in cooperation with all offices concerned, to adopt COA Circular 2020-006 for the implementation of the one-time cleansing of PPEs, including reconciliation of accounting records versus physical count; and
b. to use the format of RPCPPE provided in the Annex 49 of NGAS Manual for LGUs and adopt a uniform property identification system for PPE.

We also recommended that the GSO prepare and maintain property cards for all classes of PPE and ensure that these assets be validated during physical count.
observations.managementAction:
The GSO and the Municipal Accountant shall proceed with the preliminary activities in connection with the One-Time Cleansing of PPE. The GSO has started verifying the existence of the PPE records found in the records of the Accounting, and preparing reports relative to the items which are no longer in physical existence.

The GSO shall use the format of RPCPPE as provided in the NGAS manual. The GSO shall prepare Property Cards for all classes of PPE. Attached are the copy of property cards prepared by the GSO.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and GSO to immediately comply with the full implementation of the perpetual inventory method in recording and reporting all procured goods, supplies and materials for distribution and consumption pursuant to the NGAS Manual for LGUs. We recommended that the GSO conduct the yearly inventory taking of all PPEs, prepare the corresponding report and submit the Report of Physical Count of Inventory (RPCI) to the Accounting Office and copy furnish the Audit Team.
observations.managementAction:
The GSO shall conduct the yearly inventory taking of all PPEs, prepare the corresponding report and submit the RPCI. After which, require the immediate reconciliation of the GSO report versus the Accounting records to facilitate the review of the noted variances and effect the necessary adjustments.

The GSO and the Municipal Accountant shall commence with the preliminary activities relative to the One-Time Cleansing of PPEs. The GSO has started verifying the existence of the PPE records found in the records of the Accounting, and preparing reports relative to the items which are no longer in physical existence.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure the completeness of the supporting documents as required under Section 9.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 before processing payments to suppliers and contractors and submit immediately all lacking documents to the Office of the Auditor for verification and audit, henceforth, comply strictly with Section 9.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001; and
b. direct the BAC Chairman to comply with Section 37.1.6 and 37.4.2 of Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 on the required posting of Notice of Award (NOA), Notice to Proceed (NTP), and Approved Contract in the PhilGEPS website.
observations.managementAction: The copy of the Municipal Warranty Security and other documents required for the specified projects shall be submitted to the COA. The BAC Chairman will ensure compliance with the required posting in the PhilGEPS website. Required postings in PhilGEPS website were made. Screenshot of sample postings were submitted.

observations.recommendation: Therefore, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to provide justification on the above-discussed audit observation. The Municipal Accountant should strictly implement the guidelines set forth in COA Circular No. 97-002 for the grant and liquidation of cash advances, particularly with respect to the prescriptive period for the latter. She must not accept liquidation reports not duly accomplished for their processing. Finally, the Municipal Accountant must cause or order the withholding of the payment of any money due to the concerned employees.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant shall strictly monitor the liquidation of cash advances. In addition, all cash advances were liquidated as of December 31, 2023. The Municipal Accountant shall implement the guidelines set forth in COA Circular No. 97-002 for the grant and liquidation of cash advances, particularly with respect to the prescriptive period for the latter.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. GSO designate to prepare and maintain road networks property cards and Report on Local Road Network based on the report submitted by the Municipal Engineer; and
b. Municipal Engineer to exhaust measures to provide the Municipal Accountant and GSO the complete description and cost segregation of road components to the Office of the Municipal Accountant; and
c. Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain the prescribed property ledger cards for Local Road Networks and disclose all the required information for the said property in the notes to Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction:
The GSO prepared the Local Road Network Property Cards. The Municipal Accountant prepared adjusting journal entry to correct the balance of the accummulated depreciation of the Local Road Networks to reflect zero residual value to the said account.

(See attached copy of JEV relative to the adjustments)

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Gender and Development Focal Point System (GFPS) to administer the HGDG and test and PIMME in the PPAs, to strengthen the implementation and evaluation of gender-related issues as required under JMC No. 2013-01.
observations.managementAction:
The GAD Focal person already complied with the HGDG and PIMME. Attached is the copy of the PIMME.

The transmittal letter duly received by the CO was submitted.

The LGU thru the Municipal Planning and Development Office together with the Municipal Council to ensure that those projects will be implemented as scheduled and planned.
The Municipal Accountant was not able to fully reconcile. The balances are not yet reconciled. The Inventory Committee will still work and will abide with the ongoing recommendation of reconciling records.
observations.reason: details for old balances are not available. Reconcilation of PPE is still ongoing.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and members of the Municipal Development Council to:
a. Assess the feasibility of the 19 out of 21 development projects of the LGU and responsiveness to the current needs of the constituents and those unimplemented projects;
b. Conduct regular coordination with the implementing offices to ensure that PPAs are undertaken within the target period; and
c. Observe the guidelines on the appropriation and utilization of 20% Development Fund under JMC No. 1 of the DBM, DOF and the DILG dated November 4, 2020.
observations.managementAction: The LGU thru the Municipal Engineer together with other concerned offices to provide coordination and monitoring together with the Municipal Planning and Development Office to ensure that PPAs are implemented. The LGU implement regular coordination with the implementing offices to ensure undertakings within target period.
observations.reason: 19 out of 21 development projects of the concerned LGU were implemented.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer to:
a. Employ close and regular monitoring and supervision of projects to achieve more regular efficient supervision of projects to avoid any potential cause of any unnecessary delays;
b. Consider the timeliness of filing of notice by the contractor of any request for contract time extension. When issuing approval, the LGU take into account the contractor's deadlines for notice filing and any contract time extension requests; and
c. Conduct thorough validation and evaluation of the reasons provided for requested extension if based only on the allowed circumstances stated in Item 10 of Annex “E” of 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 otherwise, impose liquidated damages for every day of delay.
observations.managementAction: When issuing approval, the LGU take into account the contractor's deadlines for notice filing and any contract time extension requests. The LGU validates and evaluates the reasons provided by the contractors for extension.
observations.reason: The LGU validated and evaluates the reasons provided by the contractors for extension.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
a. Municipal Health Officer to obtain from the donors the necessary information as to the unit cost and quantities of the vaccines or any other medicines acquired by the Municipality in the future. Likewise, render a monthly report to the Municipal Accounting Office on the receipt, issuance and balances of vaccines and supplies directly received from outside source; and
b. Municipal Accountant in coordination with the MHO, to determine and recognize in the books the full cost of the ending inventory of vaccines and supplies as at year-end and henceforth record future receipts and issuances of the same.
observations.managementAction: Only numbers of vaccines were able to determine as of December 31, 2022 based on the documents received by the MHO together with the donated vaccines mentioned in the audit findings. The Municipal Health Officer obtains from the donors the necessary information as to the unit cost and quantities of the vaccines received. Municipal Accountant in coordination with MHO will request the Municipal Health Officer to DOH 4A for costing and will record inventories accordingly.
observations.reason: Health Officer to request costing as basis of recording but no reply received. All remaining vaccine were used and consumed before December 31, 2023.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the HRMO to ensure that payment is in accordance at least with the regular minimum wage with the regular employees and similar positions having comparable rate for fair and equitable compensation.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal HRMO to ensure that payment is in accordance at least with the regular minimum wage with the regular employees and similar positions having comparable rate for fair and equitable compensation.
observations.reason: From three hundred fifteen pesos (P315.00) daily rate of JOs in CY 2023, it was increased to three hundred fifty pesos (P350.00) in CY 2024.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer and other concerned officials to provide the Audit Team at the beginning of the year the list of ongoing and those that are to be implemented during the year and those that are funded from their local budget or financial assistance or grants from other national agencies and those to be undertaken by the other Municipality in order to determine whether funded from their local budget or by other national government assistance or grants.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal management submitted the list of ongoing PPAs and those to be implemented. In addition, the Procurement Monitoring Report (PMR) is also being submitted.
observations.reason: Submitted the list of ongoing PPAs and Procurement Monitoring Report (PMR) with the required details.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reconcile the municipal records with the province’s books of accounts to establish the correct balances of the reciprocal accounts and request from the Provincial Accountant the copies of the JEVs showing all the transactions recorded in the books of accounts of the Province that should be taken up in the Municipality’s books of accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant had made reconcile the municipal records with the province’s books of accounts to establish the correct balances of the reciprocal accounts and request from the Provincial Accountant the copies of the JEVs to present and reconcile with the Province’s accounts. The Municipal Accountant prepared JEV No. 300-167D under Trust Fund.
observations.reason: Reconciled with Province’s accounts. JEV No. 300-167D under Trust Fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to observe the proper journal entries for recording of receipts and disbursements under the Trust Fund and refrain from making shortcut entries for proper monitoring of the balances of the accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Effective January 1, 2023, income and expenses accruing to the Trust Fund are already recognized.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to: a. In cooperation with the Municipal Engineer, to initiate the adoption of policy providing the specific estimated useful life for each specific asset based on their experience; b. Recompute the depreciation based on the approved estimated useful life of each Infrastructure Assets; and c. Prepare the appropriate journal entries and other accounting records to correct the depreciation and effect the necessary adjustments in order to rectify the errors and present the asset accounts to their proper carrying amounts.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor issued Executive Order (EO) No. 16, series of 2023 last December 18, 2023 to adopt the Department of Public Works and Highways (DPWH) Guidelines and Procedures in the Recognition and De-Recognition of Infrastructure Assets, including the adoption of estimated useful lives of infrastructure assets. The approved EO was furnished by the Municipal Engineer to the Municipal Administrator, Accountant and Budget Officer on January 31, 2024. Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 2, Part II, Page Nos. 42-44 of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the: a. Municipal Health Officer to secure Property Transfer Reports (PTRs) from the source agencies of donations, henceforth, provide the Municipal Accountant with the inventory reports, attach all Requisition Issue Slips and PTRs, of all donations in-kind received by the Municipality related to its COVID-19 Vaccination Program; b. Municipal Accountant to record in the books of the Municipality the inventory as of year-end of all donations in-kind with available cost information, in accordance with Paragraph 16 of IPSAS 12; and c. Municipal Accountant to properly recognize the inventory as of year-end of all in-kind donations without available cost information at their fair values at the date of receipt, which may be ascertained by reference to an active market, or by appraisal.
observations.managementAction: The updated list of vials as of December 31, 2023 was already submitted to the Municipal Accountant for proper recognition in the books. The Municipal Accountant makes sure to record all donations made to the Municipality based on available cost information.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MDRRMO and Municipal Planning and Development Office to: a. Submit the Municipality’s plan for the implementation of the unutilized balance of LDRRMF amounting to P41,311,145.94; b. Submit written justifications and explanations regarding the foregoing observations; c. Evaluate and review the LDRRMF Investment Plan with emphasis on the proper identification and selection of specific priority PPAs to accelerate their implementation and judiciously plan on how to significantly increase the utilization of the LDRRM Fund to achieve the goal/target of the Municipality regarding disaster relief, recovery, and risk reduction and management; and d. Prepare the LDRRMF Investment Plan or LDRRM Plan which includes the list of projects and activities charged to the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years and submit a copy to the COA Audit Team.
observations.managementAction: Unutilized balances were presented to the MDRRM Council in its 2nd Quarter Council dated June 30, 2023 for the reprogramming of funds into projects resulting to MDRRM Council Resolutions. The same was presented to MPDC and verified with the Accounting Office. After which, the proposal was submitted to the Sangguniang Bayan for reprogrammed mitigation projects. Furthermore, for the CY 2024, an additional P15,300,000.00 was reprogrammed for DRRM projects. As of now, the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF is P20,843,020.00, excluding the QRF. Of the said amount, P17,571,700.18 pertains to unbidded projects, wherein Purchase Requests were submitted to the Bids and Awards Committee. The MDRRMO will coordinate with the Municipal Accountant for the remaining unexpended balance for reprogramming.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct: a. Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) and Municipal Accountant to cause the submission of the documents required by Items 9.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012, for further evaluation of the Audit Team; b. Municipal Accountant to check the completeness of supporting documents prescribed under the circular to ensure compliance with Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 before processing payments in order to establish the legality, validity and propriety of the disbursement; and c. BAC to strictly comply with the provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 to strictly comply with the provisions of the procurement process and award of contract.
observations.managementAction: The BAC has submitted the lacking documents noted above or provide justification for the deficiencies in the procurement of goods and infrastructures for further evaluation. The Municipal Accountant is constantly checking the completeness of supporting documents before processing payments in order to establish the legality, validity and propriety of the disbursement. The BAC has consistently observed and complied with the provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal General Services Officer to submit explanations why the brand name, model and specifications are not indicated in the POs, and to ensure that all future POs include the brand name, model and complete detailed specification of the items procured, in compliance with COA Circular No. 96-010 dated August 15, 1996.
observations.managementAction: The inclusion of brand name, model and specifications are already implemented in the preparation of POs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the BAC to require the winning contractors of on-going infrastructure projects to submit their DOLE-approved CSHP, in compliance with DOLE DO No. 13, s. 1998 and Section 37.2.3(f) of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184. We further recommended that all future contracts for projects to be implemented be supported with an approved CSHP and that the BAC require the contractors to include in their program of works the cost of protective equipment to be provided for their laborers.
observations.managementAction: The BAC requires proof of filing of submission to DOLE and approved CSHP prior to issuance of contract and accomplishment reports, respectively. The BAC requires proof of filing of submission to DOLE and approved CSHP prior to issuance of contract and accomplishment reports, respectively.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the concerned departments, to submit explanation on the deficiency observed during the distribution of rice grains and ensure that all subsequent transactions were properly documented before processing payments.
observations.managementAction: Justification letter was submitted to the Audit Team containing the explanation for the lapses in the distribution of rice grains. List of beneficiaries and recipients were likewise submitted.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct: a. the Municipal Accountant to record the Land in the books as of year-end and adjust the Land account by the appraised value of all the real properties. Reconcile Accounting records of the landholdings of the Municipality; b. All concerned department heads to coordinate with each other and exert extra efforts to secure documents relative to the purchase made from Mr. Reynaldo Cargado. Work together towards the reconciliation and registration of all landholdings to protect the municipality against undue risk of third-party claim.
observations.managementAction: On property acquisition, the part of the Assessor is the Property Appraisal which was complied with together with the Board of Appraisal and Assessment Committee of the Province of Batangas. On the other part, once the documents of transfer have been completed and the need for transfer of Tax Declaration arise, the Assessor’s Office will transfer the same. The members of the Sangguniang Bayan commits to ratify the purchase of land thru SB Ordinance indicating the purpose of purchase, cost, location and other details needed for the authorization of the local chief executive to enter into contracts on behalf of the local government unit.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the BAC Chairman in coordination with the BAC Technical Working Group to explain the observed inconsistencies in the procurement process and to immediately submit the aforementioned lacking documents and other relevant supporting documents/reports to facilitate further review and evaluation by the Audit Team and the COA Information and Technology Audit Office. We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct all department heads concerned to resolve all the issues hindering the full implementation of the computerization project, discuss them with the supplier, and come up with a favorable solution.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality fully implemented the computerization project for some modules. The BAC and the Municipal Accountant provided the required documentation for the Audit Team. The Municipality appointed personnel who encoded in the system and the Municipal Assessor completed encoding of the RPT data. The MBO and CITS will coordinate with the system provider for needed customizations and re-training. While the required data were already encoded in the system, the computerization project remained not fully maximized/utilized.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MENRO to expedite the compliance of the requested documents necessary for the approval of the 10-Year Municipal Solid Waste Management Plan (2019-2028) by the NSWMC. We further recommended that the MENRO continue the disposal and safe closure and rehabilitation of dumpsite in Brgy. Dayap and fully implement the SCRP, and pursue the updated 10-year plan for approval by NSWMC.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality submitted the updated version of the plan (2023-2033) for approval. The DENR EMB returned the plan for updating. The approved updated plan was forwarded for NSWMC. The open dumpsite safe closure and rehabilitation in Brgy. Dayap was implemented and settled in CY 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive enjoin the concerned officials to ensure the annual preparation of the required reports, the conduct of inventory of roads and submission of the same to the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed period.
observations.managementAction: The GSO and the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal Engineer, shall start accomplishing said report effective CY 2024.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the BAC Chairman in coordination with the BAC Technical Working Group to explain the observed inconsistencies in the procurement process and to immediately submit the aforementioned lacking documents and other relevant supporting documents/reports to facilitate further review and evaluation by the Audit Team and the COA Information and Technology Audit Office. We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct all department heads concerned to resolve all the issues hindering the full implementation of the computerization project, discuss them with the supplier, and come up with a favorable solution.
observations.managementAction: The BAC provided the required documents and explanations. The Municipal Assessor, CITS and other end users coordinated with the system provider for enhancements and parallel runs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MENRO to expedite the compliance of the requested documents necessary for the approval of the 10-Year Municipal Solid Waste Management Plan (2019-2028) by the NSWMC and to continue the disposal and safe closure and rehabilitation of the dumpsite in Brgy. Dayap and complete other required actions.
observations.managementAction: The updated plan was submitted and is pending validation and final deliberation schedule. The open dumpsite in Brgy. Dayap safe closure and rehabilitation was implemented and settled in CY 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive enjoin the concerned officials to ensure the annual preparation of the required reports, the conduct of inventory of roads and submission of the same to the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed period.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer and concerned officials will start accomplishing the said report effective CY 2024.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the BAC Chairman explain inconsistencies and submit lacking documents for review and that department heads resolve issues, discuss with supplier and come up with solutions.
observations.managementAction: BAC submitted required documentation; end-users and CITS coordinated with system provider for enhancements; partial implementation and encoding completed for some modules.

observations.recommendation: We recommended expediting compliance and submission of required documents for NSWMC approval and completion of SCRP.
observations.managementAction: Updated plan (2023-2033) submitted for approval; SCRP activities implemented; validation pending.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Accountant to record and reconcile land records, and for department heads to secure documents and pursue registration.
observations.managementAction: Assessor complied with property appraisal; Sangguniang Bayan to ratify purchase via ordinance; Municipality to secure outstanding documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the BAC and concerned offices submit lacking documents and resolve issues with the supplier to achieve full implementation.
observations.managementAction: BAC and Municipal Offices submitted documents; system provider engaged for fixes; partial implementation achieved; full utilization pending.

observations.recommendation: We recommended MENRO expedite compliance and submission of necessary documents for NSWMC approval and implement SCRP.
observations.managementAction: Updated plan submitted for NSWMC approval; SCRP implemented at Brgy. Dayap and settled in CY 2021; follow ups ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended concerned officials ensure annual preparation and submission of the required reports and conduct inventories of roads.
observations.managementAction: GSO and Municipal Accountant, with Municipal Engineer, will start accomplishing said report effective CY 2024.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the following: a. The Municipal Accountant to undertake a thorough review and analysis of the details of the payable accounts that remained outstanding for two years or more. Secure documents that will support such payables and coordinate with the identified creditors to ascertain the existence, validity and legality of their claims with the Municipality; facilitate the payment; b. The Municipal Treasurer to continuously secure information as to the whereabouts of those payees whose checks have been stale and send notification letter to them. Likewise, coordinate with the Municipal Accountant for those issued checks to payees that are nearing the 6-month period for immediate notification and to avoid being stale; and, c. The Human Resource Management Officer to notify those former job order and co-terminous employees with unclaimed salaries and Pag-IBIG premiums and coordinate with the Municipal Accountant for the processing of claims.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant has thoroughly reviewed four Accounts Payable with a total amount of P1,105,191.00 and secure documents that will support such payables. The Municipal Accountant sent letters to the identified creditors requesting them to submit the supporting documents to the Municipality; facilitate the payment. The Municipal Treasurer has sent written notice to payees whose checks have been stale. The HRMO notified the concerned.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant and coordinate with the department heads who are implementors of programs for the funds received from other government agencies/private organizations to submit copies of the Memorandum of Agreement to the Municipal Accounting Office every time they receive fund transfers for proper recording in the books and henceforth, cause the immediate implementation of projects and utilization of those fund transfers. For the unexpended balances of fund pertaining to completed projects, remit the same to the source agency. Also ensure correctness of account codes used in the Subsidiary Ledgers and reconciled with amounts reported in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Copies of the Memorandum of Agreement are provided for the receipt of fund transfers. The Municipal Department Heads had provided copies for the receipt of fund transfers and the Management implemented ensuring the correctness of the account codes used by coordinating with the concerned Departments/Heads. For the unexpended balances of fund pertaining to completed projects, the Management reverted savings from CY 2021 and previous years balances and prepared documentation to remit the same to the source agencies when warranted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Engineer and Municipal Planning and Development Officer to ensure optimal and timely utilization of 20 percent DF in the succeeding period by including development projects that are well-planned and procurement-and-implementation-ready pursuant to DBM, DOF and DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 1, s. 2020. Likewise, lessen frequent changes in the planned PPAs to ensure immediate implementation and completion within the target date.
observations.managementAction: Delays were still noted in the implementation of the projects funded under the 20 percent DF.
