The Municipal Engineer and Planning Office committed to implement the proposed projects and to prepare the necessary planning documents (PPMP and APP) as guide for proper implementation and completion within the target date.
observations.reason: Delays were still noted in the implementation of projects funded under the 20 percent DF.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct: a. The Municipal Assessor and the General Services Officer to exhaust all remedies to secure Certificate of Titles or other forms of ownership of the aforementioned lands and take the necessary actions to expedite the transfer of ownership to the Municipality. Coordinate with the Municipal Budget Officer regarding fund requirements for inclusion in the Annual/Supplemental Budget in the succeeding periods, if warranted; and b. The Municipal Accountant to secure copies of those documents lacking on the procurement of land and submit it to the Audit Team, once available.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Assessor and the General Services Officer had furnished to the Audit Team on November 19, 2024 copies of the Tax Declaration of Real Property. The Municipal Accountant requested copy of documentation but copies of Transfer Certificate of Title for the three lots were not submitted.
observations.reason: The Municipal Management is still in the process of securing the required documents.

observations.recommendation: Based on the foregoing, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the following: a. The Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office (MDRRMO) implement the programs and projects under Current and Continuing Appropriation and under the Trust Liability – DRRM account, within the intended time to avoid delay in the delivery of its benefits to the constituents. Conduct thorough planning and analysis to ensure that those identified PPAs to be included in the LDRRM Plan are those that can be implemented and completed within the target period; b. The Municipal Budget Officer revert savings from CY 2021 and previous years’ balances of completed projects under Capital Outlay to be allotted to other disaster preparedness and mitigation PPAs; and c. The MDRRMO and the Municipal Accountant to conduct periodic monthly/ quarterly review and reconciliation of Trust Liability – DRRMF account.
observations.managementAction: The MDRRMO had conducted monitoring and consultative planning for DRRM Projects. The Municipal Budget Officer had reverted the savings from CY 2021 and previous years’ balances and reprogrammed the balances of completed projects under Capital Outlay thru Supplemental Budget No. 02-2023. The Municipal Accountant ensured that the posted Warranty Security is in the form prescribed in Section 62 of the IRR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the GFPS Focal Point Person, GAD TWG and the Gender and Development Section Head to: a. Conduct thorough planning and analysis to ensure that those identified PPAs to be included in the GAD Plan and Budget are those that can be implemented and completed within the target period. Take into consideration the feasibility and propriety of the proposed PPA before including it in the plan. In addition, update the GAD Database of the Municipality to properly identify the gender issues in need of immediate action; and b. Coordinate and reconcile with the Municipal Budget Office to accurately present the total appropriation and utilization of the GAD Fund.
observations.managementAction: A thorough planning is done by the Local Finance and GFPS to identify PPAs that are feasible and can be implemented as scheduled. The GAD section is regularly coordinating and reconciling with the Municipal Budget Office to accurately present the GAD AR but since the Accomplishment Report was encoded to the DILG portal prior the finalization of RAAO, some variances cannot be avoided.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the HRMO to: a. Ensure that the item/position of the JO employees are stated in the CoS and the rates paid correspond to the rate stated in the CoS; b. Require the requesting office to submit justification for the hiring of JOs based on the actual workload of the employees vis-a-vis the number of organic personnel of the concerned offices; and c. Provide a written policy on the hiring of JOs and ensure that payment is in accordance at least with the minimum wage rate issued by the DOLE or if funds are available, a comparable rate with the regular employees and similar items or positions having similar rate for fair and equitable compensation.
observations.managementAction: The HRMO complied to the recommendation by ensuring the item/position of the JO employees are stated in the CoS and the rates paid correspond to the rate stated in the CoS. The HRMO had requested the office to submit justification for the hiring of JOs. The management commented that they cannot increase the rates in accordance with the minimum wage due to budget constraints.
observations.reason: The management commented that they cannot increase the rates in accordance with the minimum wage due to budget constraints.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: a. Inventory Committee, in cooperation with all offices concerned, verify the existence and identify the unspecified and unidentified PPE and the details of the other PPEs. Revisit all available records to account for these PPEs including its custody. Indicate in the remarks portion of RPCPPE the actual condition of these assets after sufficient validation of its existence. b. the GSO to prepare and maintain property cards and Report on Local Road Network starting only in CY 2011 and ensure that these assets be validated during PPE physical count.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal submitted a comprehensive explanation on the unspecified and unidentified PPE. The GSO is still in the process of preparing property cards.
observations.reason: Observation No. 1, Page 38 of CY 2022 AAR was revised.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the Honorable Members of the Sangguniang Bayan include among its top priorities the construction of a building for the safekeeping and accommodation of the increasing volume of documents generated by the various departments/offices of the Municipality, particularly those for submission to the audit team, with consideration on the preservation period of ten (10) years as mandated by law.
observations.managementAction: Construction of the new municipal building is ongoing and includes provision for stockroom of the audit team.
observations.reason: The new municipal building is still under construction.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure optimal and timely utilization of 20 percent DF in the succeeding period by including development projects that are well-planned and procurement-and-implementation-ready pursuant to DBM, DOF and DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 1, s. 2020. Likewise, lessen frequent changes in the planned PPAs to ensure immediate implementation and completion within the target date.
observations.managementAction: No action taken.
observations.reason: Closed revised in Observation No. 4, Page 44 of CY 2022 AAR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: a. Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant and GSO with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for all road projects; b. Inventory Committee to conduct inventory count of all the Municipality’s Road Network systems and accordingly submit a Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network System as of the end of the year; and c. Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain the prescribed property ledger cards for Local Road Networks and disclose all the required information for the said property in the Notes to Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer submitted to GSO a partial inventory of road networks starting only in CY 2018 since she assumed office at the MEO. The GSO maintained the road network property ledger cards starting in CY 2011. The Municipal Accountant implemented the disclosure for FS is on-going.
observations.reason: The submitted inventory report was not in the prescribed form and lacks complete details, however the road networks property ledger cards were already maintained by the GSO from CY 2011 onwards.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Business Permits and Licensing Office and Municipal Treasurer’s Office to require submission of sworn statement of gross receipts/sales and maintain certified copies of income tax return so as to come-up with an accurate basis for the computation of business taxes; and to verify, assess and validate the reported under-collections and assess the under-collection and demand collection/payment of the under-collections.
observations.managementAction: The BPLO and Municipal Treasurer already required submission of sworn quarterly statement of gross receipts to determine taxable income. The Municipal Treasurer sent collection letters and was able to collect P23,998.91 for the under-collections.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure that procurement under SEF strictly comply with the Revised IRR of RA 9184 and that implementing offices secure complete documentation and compliance with procurement rules. Follow-up on outstanding obligations and recoveries as necessary.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer has issued demand letters to the beneficiaries; promised to pay and as of May 15, 2024 a total amount of P457,068.21 was already collected.
observations.reason: Some beneficiaries promised to pay their loans on a staggered basis.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to: a. Recognize Income and expenses that are considered as non-exchange transactions and record entries relevant to the transactions; and b. Prepare and submit for succeeding years the Statement of Financial Performance for the Trust Fund.
observations.managementAction: Statement of Financial Performance for the Trust Fund was prepared and submitted for the succeeding years.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct: a. Municipal HRMO and the rest of the PRAISE Committee submit the Municipality’s Program on Awards and Incentives for the Civil Service Excellence and secure a copy of the CSC-approved PRAISE; b. Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to stop processing any succeeding grant of rice subsidy unless a valid legal basis is available; and c. PRAISE Committee to ensure that the provisions of CSC MC No. 01, s. 2001 and CSC Resolution 010112 particularly on providing incentives and awards based on performance, innovative ideas and exemplary behavior are adhered to by establishing set of criteria, performance indicators and adequate evaluation procedures to identify qualified grantees.
observations.managementAction: The LGU presented some clarifications on the audit recommendations: a. First, if the "local administrator" referred in the provision is the person who administers and acts in all transactions in behalf of the local government or the local chief executive. b. Second who would be the assistant in case of temporary absence or incapacity of the foregoing officials and considering that the Municipal Administrator has no office or staff and under the Office of the Mayor. c. Third, whether hierarchy of position is not considered in countersigning checks drawn, that while the Municipal Mayor signed and approved the disbursement voucher, the Municipal Administrator is the one who will countersign on the check, where in fact he is under the office of the Municipal Mayor per se. Thus, they said that with all these presented clarifications in the implementation of above-mentioned recommendation, they respectfully will remain to the practice that the checks drawn in settlement of the obligations except for SB budget be countersigned by Municipal Mayor. Considering that this practice, they believe, does not compromise nor violate the principle of check and balance for the simple reason that both the Municipal Mayor and Municipal Administrator belongs to the same department/office of the executive. Likewise, the management ensures that they exercise proper management of funds, disbur funds properly and accordingly.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Engineer to: a. Employ close and regular monitoring and supervision of ongoing projects to ensure successful outcome and to avoid any circumstance that may lead to unnecessary delay in completion which affected the timely delivery of the desired benefits to intended users; b. Consider the timeliness of filing of notice by the contractor of any request for extension on granting time extension; and c. Conduct thorough validation and evaluation of the reasons provided for requested extension if based only on the allowed one-tenth of one percent of the total project cost per day otherwise, impose liquidated damages of one-tenth of one percent of the total project cost per day for projects that fail to complete or finish on time. The office also sends notice letters to contractors whose projects are approaching their deadlines.
observations.managementAction: The LGU is implementing more regular monitoring and supervision of projects to ensure successful outcome and to avoid any potential cause of delays. When issuing approval, the LGU take into account the contractor's notice filing deadlines for requests. The LGU validates and evaluates the reasons provided for extension otherwise they impose liquidated damages of one-tenth of one percent of the total project cost per day for projects that fail to complete or finish on time. The office also sends notice letters to contractors whose projects are approaching their deadlines.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and members of the Municipal Development Council to: a. Assess the feasibility of the projects; b. Conduct regular coordination with the implementing offices to ensure PPAs are undertaken within the target period; and c. Observe the guidelines on the appropriation and utilization of 20% of 20% Development Fund under JMC No. 1 of the DBM, DOF and the DILG dated November 4, 2020.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Planning and Development Office, Engineering Office and other concerned Offices monitored the implementation of projects and undertook those within the target period.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and other concerned municipal officials to: a. Ensure that the development program/ projects/ activities in the LGU’s Annual Investment Program are the ones to be budgeted and implemented; and b. Strictly observe the provisions of Items 4.24 and 8.8 of the DILG, NEDA, DBM and DOF JMC No. 1 dated November 18, 2016 and Section 3.2.1 of the DBM, DOF and the DILG JMC No. 1 dated November 4, 2020.
observations.managementAction: The PPAs in the LGU’s Annual Investment Program are contained in the LGU’s Annual Investment Program that are budgeted and implemented. The LGU observes the provisions.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the LDRRMO and Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council to: a. Prepare the LDRRMFIP in accordance in the prescribed format under Annex A of COA Circular No. 2012-002 and include the PPAs to be charged against the unexpended appropriation of previous years, continuing appropriations and PPAs that can strengthen the capacity of the Municipality to build a disaster resilient community and enhance disaster preparedness and response; and b. Evaluate and study the feasibility of the projects, especially those held as continuing appropriations to ensure delivery of its intended services to the public and maximize all available sources of DRRM Funds.
observations.managementAction: The MDRRMO prepared and submitted the LDRRMFIP to the Audit Team. The Municipality computed the LDRRM Fund appropriation based on the total revenue from regular sources.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to: a. Make necessary action to inform and demand the personnel about their obligations to the municipality and cause the immediate refund/restitution of the cash shortage and collection of the amount due from municipal officials and employees representing unpaid personal telephone bills and bank loans; c. Initiate appropriate legal actions to recover from officials and employees who were already separated from the service; d. Intensify collections of Loans and Other Receivables, designate key personnel who will monitor the loans/assistance granted and enforce payment thereof. Otherwise, resort to appropriate legal remedies; and e. Coordinate with DepED personnel and exhaust all possible means to reconstruct or locate the liquidation documents/transmittal of records submitted by them in support to their unliquidated cash advances.
observations.managementAction: Demand letters were sent to listed debtors. Payments were made and the former Assistant Treasurer was restituted fully. Coordinated with DepEd; notarized sworn statements regarding liquidation were submitted by the DepEd personnel.
observations.reason: Some collections were made; legal action not yet instituted for separated employees; analysis and disposition of dormant accounts ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the: a. perform sufficient planning and detailed engineering activities and ensure that issues such as site problems, concerns by local/national government units and other matters that could delay project implementation are resolved prior to procurement of infrastructure contracts; and b. ensure that projects are completed within the contract time to avoid delays resulting in deferred benefits to the public apart from the incurrence of liquidating damages. Also recommended BAC to submit justification and the lacking documents.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer and concerned offices implemented projects within the target period. Notices and resumption orders were issued to contractors approaching deadlines. Procurement plans were submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the LDRRMO and Municipal Disaster Risk Management Council to: a. prepare and submit the LDRRMFIP to the Office of the Auditor in the prescribed format within the time frame and b. include in yearly budget the statutory requirement of at least five percent (5%) of the total appropriations for LDRRMF.
observations.managementAction: The MDRRMO prepared and submitted the LDRRMFIP to the Audit Team. The Municipality computed the LDRRM Fund appropriation for CY 2023 based on total revenue from regular sources.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the GAD Focal Person to: a. conduct regular monitoring on implementation of GAD Plan and Budget to ensure full utilization of the fund; and b. Municipal Budget Officer to include in yearly budget the statutory requirement for GAD of at least five percent (5%) of the Municipality’s total appropriations.
observations.managementAction: The GAD Focal Person scheduled the conduct of GAD agenda formulation and GAD Planning and Budgeting Training on June 2024. They also submitted the GAD Accomplishment Report in prescribed format.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the: a) Municipal Accountant to exert extra effort to locate the supporting documents pertaining to unpaid/underpayment of GSIS premium of municipal officials and employees as per GSIS schedule; compare the documents/records with that of the GSIS schedule to determine if really there are unpaid/underpayments of premiums for remittance to GSIS; and b) Municipal Accountant and her staff to expedite the analysis of the Due to GSIS account to eliminate the P120,112.73 negative balance and make necessary adjustments, if warranted, and attach relevant documents and analysis of the accounts to the JEVs for any adjustment to be made.
observations.managementAction: Continue to closely coordinate with the Municipal Human Resources Office for updating of membership records for uploading to GSIS.
observations.reason: Reconciliation and locating payroll records and remittance documents for earlier years (2012, 2013) ongoing; payroll records not available.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the management agreed that the Municipal Mayor require the Human Resource Management Officer, Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to control the accommodation given to financing institutions and ensure that minimum monthly take-home pay of all officials and employees shall not be lower than Five Thousand Pesos (P5,000.00).
observations.managementAction: Strictly monitor loan application before approval. Uploaded ARA Forms to GSIS Web and monitored loan applications; no municipal employee has net take-home pay below P5,000 as of March 2024.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor submit a written justification for non-filling of application for disposal/divestment of Talisay Public Market as well as that of other unserviceable property plant and equipment to the Commission. Further recommended that Municipal Accountant to submit the JEVs and their supporting documents which was used as basis for drawing a JEVs for the derecognition for the said property plant and equipment.
observations.managementAction: Submitted Inventory Report; copy of JEV and supporting documents submitted. Inventory of PPE as of December 31, 2019 had been submitted. Waste Materials Report (WMR) and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) were not among those submitted as basis to derecognize PPE.
observations.reason: WMR and IIRUP supporting documents not furnished to COA.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct to: a. Release the retention money to the concerned suppliers in accordance with Section 62.1 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184; and b. Conduct a thorough review and analysis and ascertain the cause of the negative and small amount of balances of this payable accounts and effect the necessary reclassification or adjustments. Attach relevant documents and analysis of the accounts to the JEVs for any adjustment to be made.
observations.managementAction: Retention money of four (4) suppliers were released as of December 2023. Reconciliation and analysis ongoing.
observations.reason: Some retention monies released; reconciliation and full adjustments pending.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Office-in-Charge to: a. Implement measures to increase the income and decrease the expenses of the Operation of Tourism to increase its collection particularly by drafting Resolution for ecological fee collections and ensuring decrease of Municipality’s expenditures within the Tourism Office.
observations.managementAction: Implemented strategies to increase income and decrease expenses; drafting of resolution for ecological fee collections and ensuring decrease of expenses within the Tourism Office.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated that the Municipal Mayor require the MDRRM Officer to prepare their LDRRMFIP. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor compute the LDRRM Fund appropriation and include in yearly budget the statutory requirement of at least five percent (5%) of the total appropriations for LDRRMF.
observations.managementAction: MDRRMO prepared and submitted the LDRRMFIP. The Municipality computed the LDRRMF appropriation for CY 2023 based on regular sources.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reconcile the balances of the deferred accounts to fairly present these accounts in the financial statements of the Municipality as of December 31, 2022.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality reconciled the balances of the deferred accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality observe strictly the accounting and reporting guidelines of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 and: a. Prepare the annual Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP); and b. Transfer the unexpended/unobligated balance of Quick Response Fund and Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund – Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses for CY 2022 to the Special Trust Fund Books.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality had already prepared the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) as of CY 2023 and transferred the unexpended balance of Quick Response Fund and Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund – Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses for CY 2022 to the Special Trust Fund in March 2023.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality strictly observe the provisions of Section 325 (h) of RA No. 7160 that actual extraordinary and miscellaneous expenses shall be two (2) percent only of the actual collection of basic real property tax in the next preceding calendar year.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality noted that actual extraordinary and miscellaneous expenses for CY 2023 was P240,022.93 while the two percent of actual collection of Real Property Tax for CY 2021 of P13,269,951.14 was P265,399.02, hence the actual extraordinary and miscellaneous expenses did not exceed the prescribed limit.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the 20 Percent Development Fund programs, projects and activities in the Municipality’s Local Development Investment Program for CYs 2023-2025 be those included in the LDIP and that those programs/projects/activities also shall be in its AIP that shall be the ones to be budgeted and implemented pursuant to Section 3.2.1 of JMC No. 1 of the DBM, DOF and the DILG dated November 4, 2020.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality stated that projects charged to 20% Development Fund in CY 2023 were those included in the Local Development Investment Program for CYs 2023-2025.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant provide depreciation to all properties of the Municipality and maintain the subsidiary records for property, plant and equipment or the Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Card required in Section 114 of Chapter 7 of New Government Accounting System Manual for Local Government Units and provide depreciation to all properties of the Municipality, to fairly present these accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality prepared PPE Ledger Card for the recorded PPEs in the books as of December 31, 2023.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the development programs, projects and activities to be charged to the 20% Development Fund shall be those included in the LDIP and that the AIP shall be the annual slice of the LDIP and those programs/projects/activities shall be the ones to be budgeted and implemented pursuant to Sections 106 and 305(i) of RA No. 7160 and Article 410 of the Implementing Rules and Regulations.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality implemented projects that were included in the Local Development Investment Program and the Annual Investment Program for the succeeding period.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality observe strictly the provisions of Republic Act No. 10121; prepare the annual Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) as prescribed in COA Circular No. 2012-002; transfer the unexpended balances to the special trust fund; maintain subsidiary ledgers per year of transfer; and ensure proper supporting documents (RIS) for distribution of welfare goods.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality had already prepared the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) for CY 2023 and is in accordance with the LDRRM Plan. The unexpended balance of 70% Mitigation Fund - MOOE and 30% Quick Response Fund for CY 2022 were transferred on March 2023 to the special trust fund. The Municipality is continuously implementing the programs and has updated its procedures to comply with COA Circular No. 2012-002.

observations.recommendation:
Based on the foregoing, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Inventory Committee to:
a. To conduct the physical inventory within three months from receipt hereof and submit the corresponding RPCPPE to the Audit Team within 15 days after the end of inventory count; and
b. Strictly follow the general guidelines, procedural guidelines, reconciliation of inventory count per RPCPPE with property and accounting records, disposition procedures for non-existing/missing PPEs, procedures in the derecognition of non-existing/missing PPEs without available records of accountability, applicable accounting entries and required disclosure in the Notes to the Financial Statements required under COA Circular No. 2020-006.
observations.managementAction: RPCPPE as of December 31, 2023 was submitted to the Audit Team on May 12, 2024.
observations.reason: Not One-time cleansing was not yet started and procedural guidelines mentioned in the recommendation were not yet observed.

observations.recommendation:
Based on the foregoing, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct:
a. The Municipal Accountant to take up necessary accounting entries to recognize unrecorded lots after the conduct of comprehensive reconciliation of lands owned by the Municipality; b. The Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Assessor to include in the appropriations the titling of land owned by the Municipality; and c. The GSO to coordinate with the Municipal Assessor’s Office for the conduct of physical count of land to determine the actual location of the lot.
observations.managementAction:
With appropriation for CY 2024 in the amount of P500,000.00.
The Municipal Accountant drew JEV No. 101-2023-05-17185 dated May 9, 2023 to take up necessary accounting entries.
Report the Physical Count of land was submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. Recompute the monthly depreciation in accordance with the provisions of IPSAS 17; b. Prepare the appropriate Journal Entry Voucher adjusting journal entries to correct the depreciation to rectify the errors and present the PPE accounts to their proper carrying amount; and c. Strictly comply with the provisions of IPSAS 17.
observations.managementAction:
Depreciation was recomputed in accordance with the provisions of IPSAS 17.
Journal Entry Voucher No. 100-2023-05-17222 dated May 12, 2023 and JEV No. 200-2023-05-01460 dated May 12, 2023 were made to take up necessary accounting entries. Revised PPE Schedules for General and Special Education Fund was submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to:
a. Recognize residual values for Infrastructure Assets equivalent to five percent of acquisition costs, in accordance with PAG 6 of IPSAS 17; b. Recompute the depreciation based on the change in accounting estimate due to provision of residual values and apply prospectively; and c. Prepare the appropriate journal entries to correct the depreciation and effect the necessary adjustments to rectify the errors and present the PPE accounts to their proper carrying amount.
observations.managementAction:
Journal Entry Voucher No. 100-2023-05-17214 dated May 10, 2023 was made to take up necessary accounting entries.
Depreciation was recomputed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to set up the RPT and SET every beginning of the calendar year based on the total assessed value of taxable real properties of the preceding year from the Municipal Assessor and List of Taxpayers certified by the Municipal Treasurer. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to review and reconcile their records on the delinquent RPT and SET receivables and submit the same to the Audit Team. Henceforth, strictly comply with Section 111 of PD No. 1445 and Sections 19, 20 and 23 of the NGAS Manual.
observations.managementAction: Records on the delinquent RPT and SET receivables were reconciled.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to submit Statement of Accounts and Summary Amounts of Delinquent RPT Taxpayers to the Provincial Treasurer’s Office and the Sangguniang Bayan on or before the 31st of December of the year immediately succeeding the year in which the delinquencies were incurred, with the request for assistance in the availment of the remedies for collection therein; b. Direct the Municipal Treasurer, Municipal Administrator and Municipal Assessor to coordinate and jointly exert extra efforts in the collection of delinquent accounts and adopt necessary measures to enable the maximum collection of unpaid taxes; and c. Henceforth, strictly comply with the provisions of RA No. 7160 on collection of real property taxes.
observations.managementAction:
Reports mentioned in the recommendations were submitted to the Provincial Treasurer’s Office and Sangguniang Bayan on May 7, 2024.
Collections increased by P1,215,703.32 in CY 2023.
Henceforth, strictly comply with the provisions of RA No. 7160 on collection of real property taxes.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Inventory Committee to: a. To conduct the physical inventory within three months from receipt hereof and submit the corresponding RPCPPE to the Audit Team within 15 days after the end of inventory count; and b. Strictly follow the general guidelines, procedural guidelines, reconciliation of inventory count per RPCPPE with property and accounting records, disposition procedures for non-existing/missing PPEs, procedures in the derecognition of non-existing/missing PPEs without available records of accountability, applicable accounting entries and required disclosure in the Notes to the Financial Statements required under COA Circular No. 2020-006. The Municipal Engineer to provide technical assistance to the Municipal GSO – Designate in the conduct of physical count of its buildings and infrastructure assets.
observations.managementAction:
RPCPPE was submitted to the Audit Team.
The Office of the Acting Municipal Engineer provided a staff to assist the MGSO on the conduct of the physical count of buildings and infrastructure assets.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to set up receivables for the RPT and SET every beginning of calendar year based on the List of Taxpayers certified by the Municipal Treasurer and recognize income for penalties only for the Municipal share. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer reconcile their records to present a reliable RPT and SET Receivables in the financial statements. Henceforth, strictly comply with the provisions of PD No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: RPT and SET receivables were set up.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor designate additional disbursing officers to avoid simultaneous cash advances.
observations.managementAction: An Inter-Office Memorandum No. 009 dated May 2, 2024 was issued by the Municipal Mayor to designate additional disbursing officer of the Municipality to avoid simultaneous cash advances.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustment for erroneous transfer of unutilized capital outlay payments amounting to P150,000.00 and for the payments made to job order workers totaling P27,115.00; to prepare Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF and submit same to the Audit Team on or before the 15th day after the end of each month; to provide disclosure on the amount and details of the unexpended balance of LDRRMF in the Notes to the Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant provided disclosure on the amount and details of the unexpended balance of LDRRMF in the Notes to the Financial Statements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to undertake the immediate disposal of unserviceable PPE to declutter the accounting books of non-value items.
observations.managementAction: Inspection and Inventory Report of Unserviceable Property were claimed to be prepared but not presented to the Audit Team. Also, the Municipality averred the plans on disposal for CY 2024.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor instruct:
a. The City Accountant to take up necessary adjusting entries to reclassify the
road lots under the Land account to the Road Networks account and transfer
the same from the Land Ledger Cards to Road Networks Ledger Cards;
b. The City General Services Office to transfer the road lots from the Land
Property Cards to the Road Networks Property Cards; and
c. The City Accountant to take up necessary accounting entries to recognize
unrecorded lots in the Land and Road Networks accounts .
Henceforth, we recommended the strict compliance with IPSAS 1, IPSAS 17 and COA
Circular No. 2015-008.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the Inventory Committee to expedite
the completion of the one-time cleansing activity in compliance with
COA Circular No. 2020-006. Adopt the disposition procedures on the non-existing
and/or missing PPEs as prescribed by Section 7 of the same Circular.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Accountant to record and
provide depreciation for the two completed school buildings in compliance with the
recognition principle of PPE within 15 calendar days from receipt thereof.
Henceforth, comply strictly with IPSAS 17.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Assessor to evaluate the
appraised values of properties under the jurisdiction of the City based on prevailing
market prices and propose the necessary revisions on the City Code and every three
years thereafter, to comply with the provisions of Sections 201 and 219 of
RA No. 7160.

We recommended that the City Mayor enforce timely project implementation by
requiring the City Engineer in coordination with City Planning and Development
Officer (CPDO) to:
a. Conscientiously carry out detailed engineering addressing any issues on the
viability of the project sites to ensure that projects are implementation-ready
before they are posted for bidding;
b. Thoroughly review and evaluate the Program of Works to ensure that project
designs and estimates are properly prepared and suit the needs of end-users and
that all phases of the projects are covered to minimize time extensions; and
c. Strict monitoring and supervision of the status/progress of projects being
implemented.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the CPDO and City Engineer to submit
within 60 days from receipt thereof, the City’s plan for the utilization and
implementation of the unutilized balance of 20% DF.
Likewise, we recommended that the City Mayor ensure the optimal and timely
utilization of 20% Development Fund in the succeeding period by including
development projects that are well-planned and procurement-and-implementation-
ready.
We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the: a. City Accountant and the City General Services Officer to evaluate the causes of the discrepancies
observations.managementAction: and b. Adopt the guidelines prescribed under COA Circular No. 2020-006 in the conduct of inventory taking for one-time cleansing of PPE.
observations.reason: Partially Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Accountant and the City Treasurer to reconcile the balance of RPT and Deferred RPT and SET Receivables as reported in the City’s books of accounts and List of Delinquent Taxpayer as of December 31, 2021, and effect the necessary Adjusting journal entry vouchers.
observations.managementAction: Adjusting entries were prepared as a result of the quarterly reconciliation.
observations.reason: On-going reconciliation

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Accountant to prepare the adjusting Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to recognize the unutilized balance of ER1-94 Funds received by the City as follows: Trust Fund Book: Due to NGAs (Dr) P195,679,626.28 Cash in Bank – LCCA (Cr) P195,679,626.28 and General Fund Book: Cash in Bank – LCCA (Dr) P195,679,626.28 Prior Year Adjustment (Cr) P195,679,626.28. In addition, require the City Accountant to record all subsequent shares of Funds under the Share from National Wealth account in the General Fund book of the City, pursuant to the Manual on the New Government Accounting System for LGUs and BLGF Memorandum Circular No. 03-01-2017.
observations.managementAction: Starting April 1, 2022, subsequent shares were credited to the Share from National Wealth account.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor require concerned City Officials to strictly follow the guidelines and procedures in the write-off of dormant receivables as required under COA Circular No. 2016-005.
observations.managementAction: The one stall per stallholder policy has been strictly implemented by the City Market Administrator. The City reviewed the Market Code provisions and recommended amendments where necessary. (Note: management action text in table: The one stall per stallholder policy has been strictly implemented by the City Market Administrator. Collection of rental fees is being enforced. The City Treasurer reviewed the Market Code.)

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Accountant to reclassify the remaining balance of the Trust Fund account as follows: Trust Fund Book Due to NGAs (Dr) P2,393,043.18 Due To NGAs (Cr) P2,393,043.18 and ensure all Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Funds received from national government are strictly recorded under the Trust Liability-DRRMF account.
observations.managementAction: Adjusting journal entry was made on April 13, 2022 to reclassify the remaining balance of the Trust Fund account as recommended. Starting April 1, 2022, subsequent ER1-94 shares were credited to the Share from National Wealth account.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor require the: a. City General Services Officer and City Assessor to conduct site inspection and prepare the reports on the actual usage of each parcel of land and existence of unlawful occupants, if any, and provide the said report to the City Accountant; and b. OIC-City Budget Officer and the OIC - City Assessor include the titling of land owned by the City in the City budget. Appropriation for the titling of land is available in the budget of the City.
observations.managementAction: Appropriation for the titling of land is available in the budget of the City. The City is coordinating the titling process and site inspections.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Sangguniang Panlungsod ratify the utilization of the ER 1-94 funds through an Ordinance and ensure that all subsequent disbursements are in accordance with approved appropriations. Also require the City Accountant and the City Budget Officer to maintain separate Account in the General Fund and separate Registries for the Share from National Wealth or ER 1-94 Funds pursuant to NGAS for LGUs.
observations.managementAction: The Sangguniang Panlungsod ratified the utilization of the ER 1-94 funds through SP Resolution Nos. 30 and 222, Series 2021. The City Accountant and City Budget Officer started to maintain separate accounts in the General Fund for the ER 1-94 funds.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LDRRMC headed by the City Mayor: a. Evaluate and review the LDRRMF under the Investment Plan with emphasis on the proper identification and selection of specific priority PPAs to accelerate their implementation; and b. Require the City Disaster Risk Reduction Management Office (CDRRMO) to include in the LDRRMFIP the PPAs to be charged against the unexpended appropriation of previous years, continuing appropriations and those financial assistance received from the National Government for affected areas of calamities.
observations.managementAction: The LDRRMC facilitated the preparation of a complete PPAs for the LDRRMF which included current year unexpended balances from previous years and transfers/grants/donations. The DRRM Planning and Development Office included the LDRRM related PPAs in the LDRRMF Investment Plan.
observations.reason: Evaluation and review of PPAs is being undertaken

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor enjoin the City Planning and Development Coordinator and City Budget Office to: a. Assess the feasibility of the projects from previous years and cause the reversion of any savings from completed appropriations
observations.managementAction: b. Judiciously appropriate budget for the identified developmental projects avoiding unutilized appropriations to attain optimal utilization of the fund
observations.reason: The CPDO will submit the Annual Procurement Plan (APP) with the updated timeline on the implementation of the unutilized balance of 20% DF. The CPDO will establish and maintain more effective coordination with the City Engineer’s Office regarding the preparation of Program of Works including plans and bill of materials, lot donation, Right of Way issue and other concerns to facilitate a more efficient procurement process and project implementation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor: a. Require the OIC-City Market Administrator to strictly implement the prohibition on occupying more than one stall per stallholder and the prohibition on sub-leasing as enunciated in the Revenue Code of the City
observations.managementAction: and b. Recommend to the Sangguniang Panlungsod to revisit the Batangas City Revenue Code of 2009 for any necessary amendment of the provisions related to Market.
observations.reason: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor require the end-user/each department to submit the Project Procurement Management Plans (PPMPs) during the preparation of budget to the City Budget Officer for thorough evaluation in terms of amount and coherence with the proposed budget of the City Government and ensure that all procurement items are incorporated in the Annual Procurement Plan. Also recommend that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) Secretariat consolidate the PPMPs into an indicative APP simultaneous with the budget preparation in strict adherence to the 2016 IRR of R.A. No. 9184.
observations.managementAction: PPMPs are now strictly required by the City Budget Officer in the preparation of the budgets for each office. Said PPMPs are being consolidated to come up with the APP in adherence to the provisions of the 2016 IRR of R.A. No. 9184.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Accountant to coordinate with the responsible officials for emergency procurement to facilitate the immediate submission of the missing documentary requirements and ensure that all documentary requirements for emergency purchase are complied with before certifying as to completeness of claims.
observations.managementAction: The required documentary requirements were duly submitted to the Audit Team. Henceforth, ensure that all documentary requirements are observed and certified before claims.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: a. The BAC incorporate the contract provisions in accordance with COA Circular No. 2012-01 and submit the post-qualification requirements provided by the contractor or justify the evaluation
observations.managementAction: b. The City Environment and Natural Resources Office (ENRO) to strictly require adherence to the controls of daily monitoring of trips such as the required signatures of witnesses and indication of time by the drivers and impose applicable penalties to all deviations noted
observations.reason: and d. The General Services Office to strictly check and periodically monitor the trucks utilized by the contractor in compliance with the contract’s requirements and constraints.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the City Mayor require: a. The City Accountant to immediately take appropriate actions to adjust the PPE accounts captioned as unaccounted/ auctioned/ demolished and derecognize the auctioned/sold and demolished properties from the books
observations.managementAction: b. The Inventory Committee of the GSO to identify/ locate the Flood Control System properties and to exclude from the RPCPPE costs of repairs and maintenance incurred by the City
observations.reason: The City has executed the one-time cleansing activity as prescribed by COA Circular No. 2020-006, through the creation of the Inventory Committee and preparation of the Physical Inventory Plan. Ongoing reconciliation of records is being made between offices and the City is currently expediting the completion of the one-time cleansing process to arrive at a reliable and accurate record of PPE balances.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor: a. Require the City Accountant, City Treasurer and the CSWDO, OCVAS and CPDO to implement and continuously exert efforts to locate borrowers and document all procedures employed to establish the location or whereabouts of borrowers including the actions taken to collect the amounts due; b. Instruct concerned City Officials to explore other appropriate actions, including filing of appropriate cases, if necessary, against erring borrowers; c. If all possible actions and effort to collect were exhausted but still no progress of collections, seek approval from the concerned City Officials for the appropriate remedial measures to rid the books of invalid receivables; and d. Require the City Accounting Office and other concerned offices to reconcile their records of the Loans Receivable - Others account so that the records being maintained by both offices reflect the same account balance.
observations.managementAction: The City Accountant, CSWDO, OCVAS and CPDO are continuously exerting efforts to locate borrowers and establish procedures to document actions taken to collect the amounts due. Some borrowers are deceased or cooperatives ceased operations; efforts to collect continue.
observations.reason: Some borrowers are deceased or records unavailable

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor: a. Instruct the Market Administrator to strictly implement the provisions of the contract of lease of market stalls and to collect rental fees and revoke contracts of delinquent tenants/lessees
observations.managementAction: b. Recommend to the Sangguniang Panlungsod to revisit the Batangas City Revenue Code of 2009 for any necessary amendment of the provisions related to Market.
observations.reason: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: a. Inventory custodians maintain complete and updated stock cards to account for the receipt and disposition of inventory items and to facilitate reconciliation of the inventory records; and b. The City Accountant and the City General Services Officer or Inventory custodians to have regular reconciliation of their records to immediately discover and reconcile discrepancies, if any.
observations.managementAction: Inventory custodians are required to maintain complete and updated stock cards. Reconciliation is being done between the GSO and the Accounting Office
observations.reason: Partially Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor: a. Require the City Social Welfare and Development Office (CSWDO), City Health Office (CHO) and OCVAS to conduct physical inventory of all undistributed and unconsumed inventories on hand and to prepare and submit the reports to the City Accountanting Office for proper recording of inventories.
observations.managementAction: Physical inventory of unconsumed and undistributed inventories was prepared by some implementing offices; however, the City Health Office was not able to report the undistributed unconsumed inventory of COVID-19 vaccines.
observations.reason: Inventory custodian of City Health Office unable to report unconsumed COVID-19 vaccines

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Accountant to strictly comply with all the documentary requirements before claims/processing for approval of claims.
observations.managementAction: Strict compliance with documentary requirements was enforced and documentary requirements were observed before approval of claims.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor: a. Instruct the LDRRMC to facilitate the preparation of a complete PPAs for the LDRRMF, covering current year unexpended balances from previous years and transfers/grants/donations to utilize and deliver more benefits to its constituents; and b. Direct the DRRM Planning and Development Officer and other concerned officials to plan, discuss and identify the DRRM related PPAs which need urgent implementation and prepare and submit the necessary reports.
observations.managementAction: The LDRRMF Investment Plan for CY 2022 included the unexpended balances from previous years and donations
observations.reason: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor require: a. The General Services Officer (GSO) to coordinate with the City Engineer in the conduct of yearly physical inventory of local road network projects, and cause submission of the Report on the Physical Count of Local Road Network (RPCLRN) in the format prescribed in COA Circular No. 2015-008, and maintain Local Road Inventory and Road Map; b. The City Accountant to keep and maintain subsidiary ledger cards for local roads and road elements for every road network; and c. Both the City Accountant and General Services Officer to reconcile the record per book and the result of physical inventory at the end of each year so as to ensure existence and accuracy of road networks presented in the financial statements of the City Government.
observations.managementAction: The City has executed the one-time cleansing activity as prescribed by COA Circular No. 2020-006. Actual physical count and reconciliation of records is being expedited to include local road networks.
observations.reason: One-time cleansing activity ongoing

observations.recommendation: We, therefore, reiterated our recommendation that the Provincial Governor direct the: a. Provincial Veterinarian (Provet) to conduct inventory of existing Biological Assets in the prescribed form- Report of the Physical Count of Biological Assets (RPCBA) providing information on the description and nature of whether consumable and bearer biological assets, the mature or immature assets, non-financial measures or estimates of the physical quantities of output/produce during the period, existence and carrying amounts with restrictions, commitments, or risk to facilitate proper recognition and reporting in accounting records and submit the same to the Accounting Office for reconciliation of records and furnish the same to the COA office; and b. Provincial Veterinarian to follow-up from the beneficiaries, the submission of documents supporting the reported deaths such as certificate of death of large cattle, autopsy report and other documents that would prove death of the animals.
observations.managementAction: The Audit Team did not receive the RPCBA. The Provet submitted documents to the Audit Team but the submission pertains to the beneficiaries, the submission of documents supporting the reported deaths, and not the death of the animals. The Taal Volcano eruption and limited movement of personnel brought by the Coronavirus Disease (COVID-19) pandemic were among the reasons cited by the employees of the Provet for the non-monitoring of the animals in calendar year (CY) 2020, but the Audit Team has yet to receive the reason for CY 2021.
observations.reason: This has been a recurring observation since CY 2018 AAR. The Office of the Provet submitted documents to the Audit Team but the submission pertains to beneficiaries and not the required RPCBA and evidence for animal deaths.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Provincial Governor direct the PhilPORTS’ officials to comply with the provisions stated under Sections 4, 5-7 of the JVA to ensure completion of the improvement of the building, otherwise require them to pay the amount of P15 million as stated in Section 6 of the JVA.
observations.managementAction: No visible improvement was made during the year, per inspection made on March 30, 2022.
observations.reason: Series of events that happened between 2019 up to present affected the project implementation such as: i. Negotiation with the claimants and other groups re: right to use BPLC; ii. Taal Volcano eruption; iii. Sta. Clara fire wherein victims used BPLC as shelter; iv. COVID-19 pandemic; v. Proposal of Governor to convert the building as quarantine hub; vi. Proposed amendment of the JVA; and vii. Extension of payment moratorium application at Sangguniang Panlalawigan.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Provincial Governor direct the Provincial Engineer to consider the option of terminating the contracts and possible recovery of payments and/or consider (1) purchase of water finder to look for possible source of water; (2) purchase of water finder to look for possible source of water, (3) look for another location where there is available water; (4) consider site relocation; (5) consider other alternative remedies in accordance with the guidelines set forth in Annex I of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.
observations.managementAction: The PEO explained that they could not proceed with termination of the project due to the concerned Sangguniang Barangay's fervent request to continue the search for a prospective location for water source for the project because water is a vital necessity of their constituents. The PEO also cited reasons such as no water finder, site relocation, contractor's workers were affected by COVID-19, and breakdown of drilling equipment.
observations.reason: No water source was found; site relocation issues; contractor's workers affected by COVID-19; breakdown of drilling equipment; and barangay requests to continue search for water source.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor:
a. direct the BAC Secretariat to submit to the Audit Team the Bidder's PhilGEPS registration or Mayor's/Business permit, notarized OSS, ITR or BTR and Agency or DTI existing price data or preliminary document showing the prevailing market prices and practice for the awarded contracts listed; and
b. instruct the City Accountant to strictly comply with all the documentary requirements before processing for approval of the claims.
observations.managementAction: The BAC Secretariat submitted to the Audit Team the required documents. The City Accountant will ensure strict compliance with all documentary requirements before processing for approval of claims.
observations.reason: Claims under negotiated procurement on CY 2021 were not supported with complete documentation. This is reiterated with modifications in Part II, Audit Observation No. 12, Page Nos. 65 to 66.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor:
a. direct the Planning and Development Officer and other concerned officials to plan, discuss and identify the DRRM related PPAs which need urgent implementation and prepare the necessary reports for consideration of the LDRRMC; and
b. direct the LDRRMC to evaluate and include the PPAs to be charged against the unexpended appropriation of previous years and transfers/grants/donations in the LDRRMFIP.
observations.managementAction: The CDRRMO replied that they are currently in the process of updating the LDRRMFIP for the year 2022 in order to achieve the goal of providing disaster risk reduction and management services for constituents. They will be coordinating with the Budget and Accounting Offices and will include the PPAs to be charged to the Continuing Fund and Trust Fund.
observations.reason: LDRRMFIP for CY 2021 still did not present specific projects to be charged; the City cannot exhaust/fully utilize the unexpended balances since the nature of disasters often equates to unpredictability and widespread effects like the Taal Volcano eruption and COVID-19 pandemic. Reiterated in Part II, Observation No. 8, Page Nos. 62 to 66.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Provincial Governor create the Roads Inventory
Committee and direct the same to conduct inventory and prepare the
corresponding inventory report, to ensure that the recorded Road Networks are
existing;
We also recommended that the Provincial Governor determine the condition of
the equipment to be donated before closing any donation transaction, to prevent
spending much on the cost of shipping and handling fees especially if donations
would come from other countries;
We further recommended that that Provincial Governor direct the:
 Head of the Provincial Tourism and Cultural Affairs Office (PTCAO), who is
in-charge in the processing of the donations, to make representation with
the donors and the shipping company for the undelivered items;
 General Services Officer to conduct an inventory of the Furniture, Medical
Equipment, medical supplies and medicines received, segregate those that
are already unserviceable or expired and dispose of properly, repair those
which are still usable and in good condition, distribute to the hospitals in
need of the equipment and include them in the inventory report; and
 Provincial Accountant to determine the value of the serviceable, add the
handling, storage and shipping fees and record the serviceable equipment
received to the appropriate asset account.

We recommended that the Provincial Governor instruct the Provincial Treasurer
to use the weighted average method in costing the inventories, as it is the
method used by the Accounting Office thru the e-NGAS, to reconcile the
balances of the accountable forms with the balance appearing in the accounting
records; follow-up from the NPO the undelivered AF purchased during the last
quarter of 2021; ascertain the cause/s for the discrepancies noted in the balances
of some AF, determine the Accountable Officer liable for the loss, if there is any,
and require payment of the corresponding cost of AF lost for the settlement of
discrepancies of the AF; and prepare Requisition and Issue Slips (RIS) for the
cost of the cashbooks and land tax records issued to personnel of the Revenue
and Cash Divisions.

We also recommended that the Provincial Governor direct the Inventory
Committee of the General Services Office to consider the deficiencies noted
during the reconciliation made with accounting records, as follows:
a. Establish a cut-off for the preparation of monthly RSMIs so that the report
could be submitted to the Accounting Office on or before the end of the
month to preclude discrepancies due to timing difference;
b. Submit the RSMIs to the Accounting Office for those inventories already
issued per GSO records but still included in the accounting records;
c. Verify from the accounting records those inventories which were already
issued per release order attached to the vouchers but still included in the
RPCI and correct or adjust GSO records;
d. Determine the causes for the inclusion of inventory balances in the GSO
despite absence in the accounting records, and effect the necessary
adjustments, if necessary. In case of loss, determine the person accountable
for the loss and require payment or submission of the documents for the
corresponding request for relief from accountability; and
e. Determine the correct cost of inventories for those with discrepancies with
accounting records and effect the necessary corrections.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Provincial Governor direct the
Provincial Veterinarian to:
a. categorically determine the existence of the Biological Assets by conducting
a comprehensive inventory of the Biological Assets recorded in the books,
and validate existence of those without status and for visitation noted in the
SLCs;
b. require the recipients the submission of certificate of death of large cattle for
deceased animals from the concerned LGUs where the recipients belong;
c. determine the details as to quantity of the cattle purchased on December 3,
2005 amounting to P289,600.00 and P710,400.00 for the Work Animals and
Breeding Stocks, respectively, and trace the recipients to determine
accountability; and
d. follow-up from the recipients the remittance of the proceeds from the sale of
the cattle for proper credit to their account.

We also recommended that the Provincial Governor direct the Provincial
Accountant to provide disclosures on the nature of activities involving each group
of biological assets, description of each group of biological assets, and other
information on the nature and extent or restriction, commitments and risks on
the Biological Assets.

We recommended that the Provincial Governor direct the concerned Provincial
Health Officer to submit the necessary program for the proper utilization of
the PhilHealth Capitation Fund amounting to P7,891,945.60.

We also recommended that the Provincial Governor direct the Provincial
Accountant to transfer the Energy Resources Production Fund amounting to
P2,922,629.31.00 to the General Fund, in the absence of any Memorandum of
Agreement for its utilization to serve as additional fund for the
implementation of various programs of the Province.

We recommended that the Provincial Governor:
a. direct the Provincial Planning and Development Coordinator (PPDC) and
Provincial Engineering Office (PEO) to ensure 100 percent implementation of
planned activities under the 20 percent DF considering that funds are available
for these activities; and henceforth,
b. instruct the PPDC to conduct regular coordination with the implementing
offices, and only include procurement-and-implementation-ready projects
under the Development Fund PPAs to ensure that they are undertaken with the
target period.

We recommended that the Provincial Governor direct the Provincial Disaster Risk
Reduction and Management Council (PDRRMC) to maximize the utilization of the
LDRRM Funds, and to accelerate their implementation in order to achieve the
goal/target of the Province regarding disaster relief, recovery, and risk reduction
and management.

We recommended that the Provincial Governor direct the Provincial Health
Officer or the concerned administrator/manager of the LEE to prepare the BP,
which shall contain the capitalization and sources of financing, organization and
staffing, pricing policy, and sunset provision mandating closure of the LEE if it
has not achieved financial self-sufficiency over a specified period of time, to
serve as framework in the operation of the economic enterprise in accordance
with LBC 111.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the:
a. City Accountant and the City General Services Officer to evaluate the
causes of the material discrepancies; and
b. adopt the guidelines prescribed under COA Circular No. 2020-006 in the
conduct of inventory taking for one-time cleansing of PPE.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Accountant and the City
Treasurer to reconcile the balance of RPT and SET Receivables as reported in
the City’s books of accounts and List of Delinquent Taxpayer as of December
31, 2021, and effect the necessary adjustments.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Accountant to prepare
the adjusting Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to recognize the unutilized balance
of ER1-94 Funds received by the City as follows:
Trust Fund Book:
Due to NGAs ₱195,679,626.28
Cash in Bank - LCCA ₱195,679,626.28
General Fund Book:
Cash in Bank - LCCA ₱195,679,626.28
Prior Year Adjustment ₱195,679,626.28
In addition, require the City Accountant to record all subsequent shares of
Funds under the Share from National Wealth account in the General Fund book
of the City, pursuant to the Manual on the New Government Accounting
System for LGUs and BLGF Memorandum Circular No. 03-01-2017.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct concerned City Accountant to
strictly follow the guidelines and procedures in the write-off of dormant
accounts as required under COA Circular No. 2016-005.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Accountant to prepare
the adjusting journal entry to reclassify the remaining balance of the account
as follows:
Trust Fund Book
Due to NGAs P2,393,043.18
Trust Liability-DRRMF P2,393,043.18
Likewise, record all subsequent financial assistance from the National
Government under Trust Liability-DRRMF, pursuant to COA Circular No.
2012-002.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the:
a. City General Services Officer and City Assessor to conduct sites
inspection and prepare the reports on the actual usage of each parcels of
land and existence of unlawful occupants, if any, and provide the said
report to the City Accountant; and
b. OIC-City Budget Officer and the OIC Assessor include in the appropriations
the titling of land owned by the City Government.

We recommended that the Sangguniang Panlungsod immediately issue an
Ordinance ratifying the utilization of the ER 1-94 Funds as approved under SP
Resolution Nos. 30 and 222, both Series of 2021 and ensure that all
subsequent disbursements are in accordance with the approved
appropriations.
We also recommended that the City Mayor require the City Accountant and the
City Budget Officer to prepare and maintain Separate Account in the General
Fund and separate Registries for the Share from National Wealth or ER 1-94
Funds pursuant to the Manual on the New Government Accounting System for
LGUs.

We recommended that the LDRRM Council headed by the City Mayor to:
a. evaluate and review the LDRRMF Investment Plan with emphasis on the
proper identification and selection of specific priority PPAs to accelerate
their implementation in order to achieve the goal/target of the City
regarding disaster relief, recovery, and risk reduction and management; and
b. require the CDRRMO to include in the LDRRMFIP the PPAs to be charged
against the unexpended appropriation of previous years, continuing
appropriations and those financial assistance received from the National
Government for affected areas of calamities.

We recommended that the City Mayor enjoin the City Planning and
Development Coordinator and City Budget Office to:
a. to assess the feasibility of the implementation of projects from CY 2021
and previous years, cause the immediate reversion of any savings from
completed projects and appropriations for projects no longer deemed
priorities and consider replacing them with well-planned and
implementation-ready development projects;
b. judiciously appropriate budget for identified developmental projects
avoiding unutilized appropriations to attain optimal utilization of the fund;
and
Also we recommended to instruct the City Planning and Development
Coordinator to make a thorough and careful study of the priority projects to
ensure their implementation throughout a given year.

We recommended that the City Mayor:
a. require the OIC-City Market Administrator under the supervision of City
Treasurer to strictly implement the prohibition on occupying more than one
stall per stallholder and sub-leasing as enunciated in the Revenue Code of
the City.
b. recommend to the Sangguniang Panlungsod to revisit the Batangas City
Revenue Code of 2009 for any necessary amendment of the provisions
related to Market.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that the City Mayor require the end-
user/each department to submit the PPMPs during budget preparation to the
City Budget Officer for thorough evaluation in terms of amount and coherence
with the proposed budget of the City Government to ensure that all
expenses/procurement items appropriated with funds are incorporated in the
Annual Procurement Plan. We also recommended that the BAC Secretariat be
required to consolidate the PPMPs into an indicative APP for goods and
infrastructure projects simultaneous with the budget preparation in strict
adherence to the provisions of the 2016 IRR of R.A. No. 9184 relative to budget
linkage, procurement planning and activities.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Accountant to
coordinate with the responsible officials to facilitate the immediate submission
of the lacking documents. Henceforth, ensure that all documentary
requirements for emergency purchase are complied with before certifying as
to completeness of the supporting documents of the disbursement vouchers.

We recommended that the City Mayor require:
a. the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to incorporate the required contract
provisions in accordance with COA Circular No. 2012-01 and submit the
post-qualification requirements provided by the contractor or justify on the
evaluation undertaken that rendered the contractor as passed despite the
lack of some of the technical requirements;
b. the City ENRO to strictly require adherence to the controls of daily
monitoring of trips such as the required signatures of witnesses and
indication of time by the drivers and impose applicable penalties to all
deviations noted; given the limited manpower under City ENRO, consider
requiring the barangay officials to submit periodic monitoring report of
daily trips;
c. the City ENRO to set parameters for late and early collection and
consistently apply penalties to all occurrence of violations; and
d. the General Services Office to strictly check and monitor the trucks
enlisted and utilized by the contractor in compliance to the contract’s
requirements and constraints;

We recommended that the Provincial Governor direct the General Services Officer to: a. prepare the IIRUP for all the properties found to be unserviceable during the inventory taking and proceed to the disposal process as prescribed in Sections 165 and 166 of COA Circular No. 92-386 to prevent further deterioration and reduction of economic value of the properties and to decongest the area occupied by the unserviceable properties; and b. reconcile with the Provincial Accountant the difference of P65,331.78 between the Accounting and Property Records.
observations.managementAction: The IIRUP as of November 30, 2021 was already prepared by the GSO and submitted to the Audit Team on December 9, 2021 while the request for inspection of the unserviceable property was forwarded to COA Technical Audit Specialist(TAS) by the Audit Team on January 25, 2022. Reconciliation was made and the updated difference amounted to a minimal amount of P20,614.36.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Provincial Governor direct the Provincial Accountant and the Provincial Treasurer to reconcile and trace back the transaction to determine the nature of the transaction and thereafter prepare a Journal Entry to record the deposits amounting to P147,725.76. On the other hand, in the absence of any advice of allotment/deposit from any government agency or private individuals, the Province may record the amount of P1,112,267.00 as Donations for Taal Volcano victims.
observations.managementAction: The unrecorded deposits were already adjusted per verification from the Bank Reconciliation Statements as of December 31, 2021 made by the hospitals for P4,023,956.48, and P147,725.76 in the SEF was recorded on December 31, 2021 per JEV No. 2021-12-01173. Donations amounting to P1,112,267.00 was recorded on February 26, 2021 under JEV No. 2021-03-00522.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Provincial Governor execute an agreement with the officials of the PCDO-BEMPC, wherein the objectives, source of fund, procedures to be adopted, the target beneficiaries, the period to operate, and other relevant information necessary to ensure the viability of the Mobile Market and that the objectives of the Province in addressing the ill-effects of the pandemic are met, be stated.
observations.managementAction: The MOA was already executed between the Province and the PCDO-BEMPC, wherein the objectives, source of fund, obligation of the parties, recording of transactions, billing, mark-up and other relevant information were stated. Copy of the MOA was submitted to the Audit Team on July 28, 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Provincial Governor: a. instruct the Provincial Accountant to set up receivables from PCDO-BEMPC representing the cost of the merchandise purchased for the operation of the Mobile Market; and b. direct the PCDO-BEMPC, through the Provincial Cooperative Livelihood and Entrepreneurial Development Office (PCLEDO), to monitor payment of receivables by the client-cooperatives to ensure continuity of the program and preclude previous experience where receivables from various cooperatives were not collected.
observations.managementAction: The amount of P5,336,946.30 was already recorded as receivables from PCDO-BEMPC in the General Fund under Accounts Receivable per JEV No. 2021-07-028826 dated July 30, 2021 and properly disclosed in the Notes to Financial Statements under Note 6. According to the Head of the PCLEDO, proper monitoring was made and they do not deliver new orders unless previous orders were paid. Of the total Receivables of P2,546,340.81 as of December 31, 2021, 69 percent were current, 28 percent -30 days but below one year, and 3 percent –more than one year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Provincial Governor direct the Provincial Accountant to set up a Special Account in the General Fund for the Department of Energy (DOE) ER 1-94 funds and prepare the necessary Journal Entries in accordance with LGUs-NGAS Manual, Volume I, BLGF MC 03-01-2017 and COA Circular 2015-009 to present the correct year-end balances of the affected accounts.
observations.managementAction: The balances of the accounts pertaining to Share from National Wealth were transferred to the General Fund on July 30, 2021. Similarly, the adjusting entries were already effected in the General Fund books on the same date per JEV No. 2021-07-02886. Supplemental Budget No. 2 was approved on October 1, 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Provincial Governor require the employees hired under the BPLC operation to submit the accomplishment report which were mainly to assist in the negotiation/facilitate meetings between the Provincial Governor and various stakeholders related to the operation of the BPLC.
